title: Research: Vercel pricing model ŌĆö snapshot + VM cost per workspace
description:
**Question:** What does a workspace cost per user per day on Vercel Sandbox? Informs retention + idle policies.

**Reference (from spec):**
- VM time: billed per compute-second. Idle sandboxes hibernate (cheap).
- Snapshot storage: billed per GB-hour.
- API calls: likely free but check.

**Deliverable:** a one-page cost model `docs/VERCEL_COSTS.md` with:
- $/day for a typical user (active chat + idle).
- Snapshot retention trade-off: keep-last-2 is our current default; is that right?
- When to force sandbox expiry (e.g., 24h idle ŌåÆ destroy, force user to re-provision).

**Acceptance:** cost model drafted; retention + idle policies confirmed or revised.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cost model drafted in docs/VERCEL_COSTS.md. Retention + idle policies confirmed or revised.
close_reason: Shipped Vercel pricing research with cost model doc + spreadsheet table (packages/agent/docs/VERCEL_COSTS.md, packages/agent/bench-results/vercel-cost-model-2026-04-23.csv). Conclusions: keep-last-2 retention, stop on session end + <=60m idle stop, no default warm pool. Cross-review via claude -p: SHIP.

title: M1+M2-Risk: Pi abort propagation to child processes
description:
**Risk class:** Accepted risk ŌĆö but we need to know if it's broken. Spec: "Pi's `abortSignal` may not reach child bash processes inside bwrap and Vercel Sandbox."

**Test:** dedicated suite that verifies abort cleanly halts in each mode.

**Per-mode scenarios:**
- **direct:** exec `sleep 30` ŌåÆ abort after 500ms ŌåÆ assert promise rejects with AbortError within 200ms + process exit code indicates SIGTERM.
- **local (bwrap):** same as above; additionally `ps aux | grep bwrap` shows no orphaned processes.
- **vercel-sandbox:** `sleep 30` remotely ŌåÆ abort ŌåÆ assert `sandbox.runCommand` promise rejects; Vercel dashboard shows no runaway tasks.

**If any scenario fails:** document the failure mode in a follow-up risk bead + propose a workaround (timeout-based kill inside the sandbox adapter).

**File:** `src/__tests__/abort-propagation.test.ts`.

**Logging:** `[abort]` prefix; log signal sent, wait time, final status, orphan check output.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 3 modes (direct/bwrap/vercel): abort halts within 200ms. No orphaned processes.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship. Added harness abort unit test that spawns a child process and verifies client abort propagates through piSession.abort() to process termination.

title: core-M1: PostgresUserStore impl ((userId, appId) composite, real upsert)
description:
## Goal
Implement `PostgresUserStore` against the schema, FIXING v1's two known bugs: (1) querying by `user_id` only, (2) `putUserSettings` no-op when no row exists.

## Why it matters
v1's UserStore silently leaked cross-app data and dropped writes for first-time users. Both are show-stoppers. Fixing them is M1's main correctness commitment for the user layer.

## Acceptance criteria
- Implements `UserStore` interface (CORE.md ┬¦DB ┬¦Stores):
  - `getById(id)`, `getByEmail(email)`, `upsert(userId, data)` against `users` table.
  - `getUserSettings(userId, appId)` queries `user_settings` by composite key (NOT just userId).
  - `putUserSettings(userId, appId, updates)` does a real `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` ŌĆö never a no-op.
- Email normalization: every `getByEmail` and `upsert` lowercases + trims input first.
- All SQL parameterized via Drizzle's query builder (no string interpolation).

## Considerations
- v1 bug refs: `packages/boring-ui/packages/cloud/src/server/providers/PostgresUserStore.ts:16, 33, 103` ŌĆö codex flagged these in pass 1.
- displayName + email fields on user_settings get updated alongside the JSON settings blob.
- `users` table is owned by better-auth; this store reads from it but never writes (better-auth handles user creation/deletion via its own paths).

## Tests
- Conformance suite: shared `describeStoreConformance(makeUserStore)` runs against both Postgres and Local.
- Cross-app isolation: insert settings for (userA, appX); query for (userA, appY); assert null/empty (not leaked).
- Upsert path: putSettings on first call (no row); assert insert. Second call (row exists); assert update.
- Email normalization: `getByEmail('  Foo@Bar.COM ')` finds the row created with `'foo@bar.com'`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦DB ┬¦UserStore; ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ PostgresUserStore app_id ignored.
close_reason: M1 PostgresUserStore shipped. Fixes v1 cross-app leak + no-op putSettings. Real INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE with (userId, appId) composite. Email normalization. 12 integration tests. Codex SHIP.

title: Phase 2: Shared Editor Lifecycle Hook
description:
## Goal
useEditorLifecycle() hook (~100 LOC) shared by CodeMirror and Tiptap editors. Eliminates ~80 LOC duplication.

## Background (Risk 16 mitigation)
Tiptap (markdown) + CodeMirror (code) both need dirty tracking, auto-save, external file change detection, theme wiring. Maintaining the same lifecycle logic in two places is a maintenance burden. This shared hook extracts the common lifecycle into a single location.

## What to implement

### useEditorLifecycle (src/hooks/useEditorLifecycle.ts)
- Shared between CodeMirrorPane and TiptapPane
- Editor provides adapter: { isDirty: () => boolean, save: () => Promise<void>, getContent: () => string }
- Hook handles:
  1. **Dirty state tracking** ŌåÆ bridge.markDirty(path) / bridge.markClean(path)
     - Updates dirtyFiles slice in Zustand store
     - Tab dirty indicator wiring (Badge on tab)
  2. **Auto-save debounce** (1000ms after last change)
     - Only fires when editor reports isDirty() = true
     - Calls adapter.save()
     - On successful save: bridge.markClean(path)
  3. **External file change detection**
     - Compare server mtime with last-known mtime (from useFileContent query)
     - Auto-sync when not dirty (refetch content, update editor)
     - Suppress stale reads for 3s after save (prevent saveŌåÆrefetchŌåÆoverwrite loop)
  4. **Tab dirty indicator wiring**
     - Set dirty badge on tab via PanelLifecycleApi or bridge event
  5. **flushSave() method** (tenth pass ŌĆö critical for onClose):
     - Cancels pending debounce timer
     - Calls adapter.save() immediately
     - Returns Promise that resolves when save completes
     - onClose for editor panels calls flushSave() before resolving
     - If still dirty after flush (save failed) ŌåÆ prompt user "Save changes?"
     - The shared hook IS the single save path ŌĆö onClose never saves independently

### Adapter interface
```typescript
interface EditorLifecycleAdapter {
  isDirty: () => boolean
  save: () => Promise<void>
  getContent: () => string
}

interface UseEditorLifecycleReturn {
  isDirty: boolean
  isSaving: boolean
  flushSave: () => Promise<void>
  lastSavedAt: number | null
}
```

### Integration pattern
```typescript
// In CodeEditorPane:
const editorRef = useRef<EditorView>(null)
const lifecycle = useEditorLifecycle(path, {
  isDirty: () => editorRef.current?.state.doc.toString() !== savedContent,
  save: async () => { await writeFile(path, editorRef.current.state.doc.toString()) },
  getContent: () => editorRef.current?.state.doc.toString() ?? '',
})

// In onClose lifecycle hook:
onClose: async (api) => {
  await lifecycle.flushSave()
  if (lifecycle.isDirty) {
    const shouldSave = await promptUser("Save changes?")
    if (shouldSave) await lifecycle.flushSave() // retry
  }
}
```

## v1 references
- EditorPanel.jsx (417 lines) ŌĆö file load/save/dirty pattern
- The isDirty/isSaving/auto-save pattern is duplicated across CodeEditor.jsx and Editor.jsx in v1

## Considerations
- This is the SINGLE save path for both editors
- flushSave() is critical for onClose integration (panel lifecycle)
- External change detection: compare server mtime with last-known mtime
- 3s stale read suppression prevents saveŌåÆrefetchŌåÆoverwrite race
- Hook is ~100 LOC ŌĆö simple but critical

## Unit tests (hooks/useEditorLifecycle.test.ts)

### Dirty state tracking
- Test: content change ŌåÆ isDirty becomes true
- Test: isDirty true ŌåÆ bridge.markDirty(path) called
- Test: save completes ŌåÆ isDirty becomes false, bridge.markClean(path) called
- Test: dirty state correctly reported for each editor independently

### Auto-save debounce
- Test: content change ŌåÆ save NOT called immediately
- Test: content change ŌåÆ 1000ms elapses ŌåÆ save called once
- Test: rapid changes within 1000ms ŌåÆ only one save
- Test: no save when content hasn't changed (isDirty = false)
- Test: successful save ŌåÆ markClean called

### flushSave
- Test: flushSave cancels pending debounce timer
- Test: flushSave calls save immediately
- Test: flushSave returns Promise that resolves after save
- Test: flushSave when not dirty ŌåÆ no-op (resolves immediately)
- Test: flushSave when save fails ŌåÆ isDirty remains true
- Test: multiple flushSave calls ŌåÆ only one save (deduplication)

### External file change detection
- Test: server mtime changed while not dirty ŌåÆ content auto-synced
- Test: server mtime changed while dirty ŌåÆ NO auto-sync (user changes preserved)
- Test: within 3s of save ŌåÆ mtime changes ignored (stale read suppression)
- Test: after 3s window ŌåÆ mtime changes detected again

### Integration with onClose
- Test: onClose ŌåÆ flushSave called first ŌåÆ then dirty check
- Test: onClose with successful flush ŌåÆ panel closes cleanly
- Test: onClose with failed flush ŌåÆ user prompted

## Success criteria
- Both CodeMirrorPane and MarkdownEditorPane use this hook
- Dirty state correctly tracked and reported to bridge
- Auto-save fires after 1000ms debounce
- flushSave() cancels debounce and saves immediately
- External file changes detected and auto-synced when clean
- Stale read suppression works (3s window after save)
close_reason: done

title: M1: CLI entrypoint bin/boring-agent (+ flags + key prompt + gitignore + SSH-aware open)
description:
**Goal:** `npx @boring/agent` experience. File: `bin/boring-agent.ts` (~200 LOC).

**Startup sequence (matches spec ┬¦CLI product shape > What the CLI does on startup):**

1. **Resolve config** ŌĆö flags > env > config file > defaults. Via `loadConfig()` in `gov.config-schema`.
2. **Auth check** ŌĆö read `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from env; if missing, prompt once (hidden terminal input) and persist to `~/.config/boring-agent/env`. File mode 0600; dir mode 0700.
3. **Gitignore hygiene** ŌĆö if workspace has `.git/` AND `.gitignore` doesn't list `.boring-agent/` AND `.pi/`, append both with a comment. One-time silent write. Skippable via `--no-gitignore`.
4. **Mode auto-detect** ŌĆö Linux+bwrap ŌåÆ `local`, macOS/Windows-WSL ŌåÆ `direct`. `vercel-sandbox` opt-in via flag or env.
5. **Workspace** ŌĆö default root = cwd; workspaceId = stable hash (SHA256 truncated to 12 chars) of absolute path. Same folder ŌåÆ same workspaceId ŌåÆ resumes sessions.
6. **Port picker** ŌĆö try `--port` (default `BORING_AGENT_PORT=8787`); if EADDRINUSE, increment up to 10 tries; confirm "our server" via `GET /health` returning our version string (disambiguates from other services). See `m1.route.health`.
7. **Boot Fastify** ŌĆö Production mode: serves pre-built `dist/frontend/` static assets. Dev mode (`--dev` flag): proxies to Vite dev server running on separate port for HMR.
8. **Open browser ŌĆö conditionally** (see below for skip conditions).
9. **Print endpoint + ctrl-c handler** ŌĆö graceful shutdown (see below).

**API key persistence (`~/.config/boring-agent/env` format):**
```
# Added by boring-agent CLI on 2026-04-22T14:30:00Z
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
```
- File mode: `0600` (user-only readable).
- Directory: `~/.config/boring-agent/` auto-created with `0700`.
- Loading: `fs.readFile()` + parse KEY=VALUE lines; supports `#` comments + blank lines.

**Browser open ŌĆö skip conditions (spec ┬¦CLI startup > step 8):**
- `$SSH_TTY` or `$SSH_CONNECTION` set ŌåÆ skip (user on remote host).
- `$CI=true` or `$CI=1` ŌåÆ skip (CI environment).
- `--no-open` flag ŌåÆ skip.
- Linux with empty `$DISPLAY` AND empty `$WAYLAND_DISPLAY` ŌåÆ skip (headless Linux).
- When skipped: print URL **prominently** + SSH port-forward hint (`ssh -L 8787:localhost:8787 <host>`).
- Otherwise: `open <url>` (macOS), `xdg-open <url>` (Linux+DISPLAY), `start <url>` (Windows/WSL).

**Same code, two modes (spec ┬¦Same code, two modes):**
- **CLI invocation** (`npx @boring/agent` ŌåÆ `bin/boring-agent.ts`): production Fastify + pre-built frontend assets bundled. No Vite dev server.
- **Package dev** (`pnpm --filter @boring/agent dev`): runs `bin/boring-agent.ts --dev` which spawns Fastify + Vite dev server (HMR for frontend). Same entry, one flag.

**CLI flags (spec ┬¦CLI flags):**
| Flag | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `--port <n>` | `8787` (auto-increment if busy) | HTTP port |
| `--mode <m>` | auto | `direct` / `local` / `vercel-sandbox` |
| `--model <id>` | pi default | Claude model override (passed via env to harness) |
| `--no-open` | opens by default | Skip browser-open |
| `--no-gitignore` | append by default | Skip gitignore hygiene |
| `--workspace <path>` | cwd | Alternate way to set workspace folder |
| `--config <file>` | `~/.config/boring-agent/config.toml` | Config file override |
| `--logout` | ŌĆö | Remove persisted API key + exit |
| `--reset-key` | ŌĆö | Delete persisted key + re-prompt |
| `--dev` | false | Package-dev mode (spawns Vite for HMR) |
| `--verbose` / `-v` | false | Debug logging; sets `BORING_AGENT_VERBOSE=1` |
| `--help` / `-h` | ŌĆö | Print usage |
| `--version` | ŌĆö | Print version + exit |

**Graceful shutdown (SIGINT / SIGTERM):**
- Abort any in-flight pi stream (AbortController).
- In vercel-sandbox mode: snapshot the sandbox to persist state.
- Close Fastify (stop accepting new connections; drain existing 5s).
- Exit 0.
- 5-second max; falls through to hard exit if anything hangs.

**Windows:** unsupported natively (spec decision). Use WSL2 ŌåÆ Linux path works.

**Tests:**
- `--logout` wipes env file; second boot prompts again.
- Port picker skips occupied ports; uses /health to confirm "our server"; errors after 10 tries.
- SSH detection: set `SSH_CONNECTION=x`, assert no browser-open attempt; print hint.
- Signal handlers: send SIGINT during active stream, assert clean shutdown within 5s (no orphans).
- API key file: mode 0600, correct parsing (KEY=VALUE, comments, blank lines).
- Auto-gitignore: in git repo w/o `.boring-agent/` or `.pi/` ŌåÆ both appended with comment. In non-repo ŌåÆ skip silently.
- `--dev` flag: Vite dev server spawned; confirm HMR works for a sample change.
- `--version` prints exact version from package.json.

**Logging:** `[cli]` prefix. Log port selection + picker attempts, mode resolution, workspace root, API key source (flag/env/file/prompt ŌĆö NEVER the key itself), browser-open decision + reason. Verbose mode logs every step of the startup sequence.

## Acceptance Criteria

`npx @boring/agent` in a random directory: opens a browser (or prints URL+SSH hint), answers "list files in /tmp" with a streamed bash tool call + result. All 10+ flags work per the table. `--dev` wires Vite HMR. Graceful shutdown completes within 5s with no orphans. API key persists securely with correct file perms. Auto-gitignore writes both `.boring-agent/` and `.pi/` when applicable.
acceptance_criteria: `npx @boring/agent` in a random directory opens a browser (or prints URL via SSH detection), answers 'list files in /tmp' with bash tool call + streamed reply.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: Phase 2: File Tree Pane
description:
## Goal
Virtualized file tree using react-arborist. Standalone FileTree component (props-only) + FileTreePane wrapper (dockview + context).

## What to implement

### FileTree (src/components/FileTree.tsx) ŌĆö STANDALONE, Tier 3
- Props-only, zero context dependency (tenth pass contract)
- Props: files: FileEntry[], selectedPath?, expandedDirs?, searchQuery?, onSelect?, onExpand?, onCollapse?, onContextMenu?, onDragDrop?
- Uses react-arborist (~15KB gz) for virtualized rendering
- O(visible) rendering, handles 10K+ files
- Keyboard navigation (arrow keys expand/collapse/navigate) ŌĆö provided by react-arborist
- ARIA tree semantics (role=tree/treeitem/aria-expanded) ŌĆö provided by react-arborist
- Drag-and-drop reordering (file/folder onto directory)
- Collapsible directories, file icons (lucide-react)
- No git status badges (agent responsibility)
- Flat tree, single root, no section system

### FileTreePane (src/panes/FileTreePane.tsx) ŌĆö dockview wrapper
- Reads file list from DataProvider (useFileList hook)
- Polling: refetchInterval 3000ms for file list, paused during edits
- Search/filter input at top (shadcn Input) with debounced search (200ms)
  - Client-side filter for <5K files
  - Falls back to server-side /api/v1/files/search for larger workspaces
- Context menu (shadcn DropdownMenu):
  - New file (POST /api/v1/files)
  - New folder (POST /api/v1/dirs)
  - Rename (POST /api/v1/files/move)
  - Delete (DELETE /api/v1/files) ŌĆö with AlertDialog confirmation
  - Copy path (client-only clipboard)
  - Open to side (bridge command)
- Drag-and-drop file moving via POST /api/v1/files/move
  - Drop validation (can't drop into self/children)
  - Visual drag-over feedback
- Wires onSelect to bridge.openFile()
- Lazy-loaded via dynamic import

## v1 references
- FileTree.jsx (1129 lines) + FileTreePanel.jsx (344 lines)
- Rewrite: replace custom CSS with shadcn, use react-arborist instead of custom tree

## Unit tests ŌĆö FileTree (standalone, vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Rendering
1. Renders empty state when files=[] (shows placeholder text)
2. Renders flat file list (3 files, no directories)
3. Renders nested directory structure (dir with children collapsed by default)
4. Renders 10K+ files without crashing (virtualized ŌĆö only visible rows in DOM)
5. Applies selectedPath highlight to correct node
6. Renders file icons from lucide-react (different icon per extension: .ts, .json, .md, folder)

### Keyboard navigation
7. Arrow Down moves focus to next visible node
8. Arrow Up moves focus to previous visible node
9. Arrow Right on collapsed directory expands it
10. Arrow Left on expanded directory collapses it
11. Arrow Left on file moves focus to parent directory
12. Enter on file calls onSelect with file path
13. Enter on directory toggles expand/collapse
14. Home moves to first node, End moves to last visible node

### Search/filter
15. searchQuery prop filters tree to matching files only
16. Partial match works (query "app" matches "App.tsx" and "app.config.ts")
17. Case-insensitive matching
18. Directory stays visible if any child matches
19. Empty search results shows "no matches" state

### Context menu
20. Right-click on node calls onContextMenu with (event, node) args
21. onContextMenu receives correct FileEntry for clicked node
22. Right-click does NOT trigger onSelect

### Drag and drop
23. onDragDrop called with (sourcePath, targetDirPath) when file dropped on directory
24. Drop on file (not directory) is rejected (no callback fired)
25. Drop on self or own descendant is rejected
26. Visual feedback (CSS class) applied to valid drop targets during drag

### Expand/collapse
27. onExpand called with directory path when expanding
28. onCollapse called with directory path when collapsing
29. expandedDirs prop controls which directories are expanded (controlled mode)
30. Multiple directories can be expanded simultaneously

### Accessibility
31. Root element has role="tree"
32. Each node has role="treeitem"
33. Directories have aria-expanded="true"/"false"
34. Selected node has aria-selected="true"

## Unit tests ŌĆö FileTreePane (vitest + @testing-library/react, mocked DataProvider)

### Data loading
35. Shows loading skeleton while useFileList is pending
36. Renders FileTree when data arrives
37. Polls for updates every 3000ms (verify refetchInterval via mock timer)
38. Pauses polling when a file is being renamed/edited (optimistic UI)

### Search
39. Search input renders at top of pane
40. Typing in search debounces 200ms before filtering
41. Client-side filter used when file count < 5000
42. Server-side /api/v1/files/search called when file count >= 5000
43. Clearing search input restores full tree

### Context menu actions
44. "New file" triggers POST /api/v1/files with correct directory path
45. "New folder" triggers POST /api/v1/dirs with correct parent path
46. "Rename" triggers POST /api/v1/files/move with old and new path
47. "Delete" shows AlertDialog confirmation before DELETE /api/v1/files
48. "Delete" confirmation dialog ŌĆö Cancel does NOT delete
49. "Copy path" calls navigator.clipboard.writeText with file path
50. "Open to side" calls bridge.openFile with splitRight option

### Drag-and-drop
51. File dropped on directory triggers POST /api/v1/files/move
52. Optimistic update: file appears in target directory before server response
53. On move failure (4xx/5xx), reverts to original position and shows error toast

### Bridge integration
54. Clicking file calls bridge.openFile(path) with correct path
55. bridge.openFile called with mode="edit" for editable files

## E2E test specs (Playwright, run against workspace-playground app)
56. File tree loads and displays project files within 3s
57. Click file ŌåÆ editor tab opens with file content
58. Right-click ŌåÆ context menu appears with all 6 actions
59. Create new file via context menu ŌåÆ file appears in tree
60. Delete file via context menu ŌåÆ confirmation dialog ŌåÆ file removed
61. Drag file to different directory ŌåÆ file moves
62. Type in search ŌåÆ tree filters in real-time
63. Keyboard: arrow keys navigate, Enter opens file

## Success criteria
- FileTree renders 10K+ files without lag (virtualized)
- Keyboard navigation works (arrows, enter to open)
- Context menu actions: create, rename, delete, copy path
- Drag-and-drop moves files correctly
- Search filters client-side for small trees, server-side for large
- Standalone FileTree works without WorkspaceProvider (props-only)
- FileTreePane works inside dockview with DataProvider context
- All 63 tests pass
close_reason: done

title: core-M3: utils ŌĆö apiFetch + apiFetchJson + HttpError + WS helpers + getHttpErrorDetail + sanitize utils + routes
description:
## Goal
Frontend transport + utility surface. Implements the apiFetch normative contract (credentials always, no retry, throws HttpError on non-2xx).

## Acceptance criteria
- `apiFetch(path, init?): Promise<Response>` ŌĆö sets `credentials: 'include'`, prepends `getApiBase()`, returns Response.
- `apiFetchJson<T>(path, init?): Promise<T>` ŌĆö calls apiFetch + parses; throws HttpError on non-2xx with proper code/message/requestId from envelope.
- `HttpError` class with `{status, code: ErrorCode, message, requestId?}`.
- `getHttpErrorDetail(err: unknown): { code, message, status? }` ŌĆö coerces unknowns into HttpError-shaped object.
- `getApiBase(): string` (reads from runtime config).
- `buildApiUrl(path: string): string`.
- `getWsBase(): string` + `buildWsUrl(path)` + `openWebSocket(path, protocols?): WebSocket`.
- `routes: RouteMap` typed route table; `routeHref(name, params): string`.
- `sanitizeMarkdown(input)` + `sanitizeToolOutput(input)` (DOMPurify-based).
- `debounce<T>(fn, ms): T` ŌĆö standard debounce.

## Considerations
- apiFetch does NOT retry ŌĆö v1's 401-retry was dropped intentionally.
- HttpError is also exported from `@boring/core/shared` so server + client share the type.

## Tests
- Mock fetch returning 401 + envelope ŌåÆ apiFetchJson throws HttpError(401, 'unauthorized', ...).
- Mock fetch network error ŌåÆ apiFetchJson throws HttpError(0, 'internal_error', 'Network error').
- sanitizeMarkdown strips `<script>` tags.
- buildWsUrl converts `https://` ŌåÆ `wss://`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Layer 2 ┬¦`./front/utils`; ┬¦API reference ┬¦apiFetch normative contract.
close_reason: apiFetch + apiFetchJson + getApiBase + buildApiUrl + getWsBase + buildWsUrl + openWebSocket + getHttpErrorDetail + routes + routeHref + sanitizeMarkdown + sanitizeToolOutput + debounce. 29 tests.

title: Infra: CI pipeline (lint + typecheck + unit + e2e + invariants)
description:
**Goal:** End-to-end CI that runs everything. File: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (or equivalent).

**Jobs:**
1. `lint` ŌĆö ESLint + Prettier check.
2. `typecheck` ŌĆö `tsc --noEmit` (workspace-wide).
3. `test` ŌĆö `pnpm test` (vitest).
4. `invariants` ŌĆö `scripts/check-invariants.sh`.
5. `build` ŌĆö `pnpm build` (asserts all dist outputs exist).
6. `e2e` ŌĆö Playwright with headless chromium. (~2 min budget.)

**Matrix:** Ubuntu (bwrap available) + macOS (direct mode only). Skip bwrap-specific tests on macOS.

**Caching:** pnpm store + Playwright browsers.

**Tests:** CI green on main + on PR sanity tests. `pnpm ci` alias runs the whole flight locally.

## Acceptance Criteria

CI runs lint + typecheck + unit + invariants + build + e2e. Matrix: Ubuntu (bwrap) + macOS (direct only). pnpm ci alias runs full flight locally.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs lint + typecheck + unit + invariants + build + e2e. Matrix: Ubuntu (bwrap) + macOS (direct only). pnpm ci alias runs full flight locally.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: M2: Periodic snapshot cron (10 min / on-idle)
description:
**Goal:** Background task per active sandbox that snapshots every ~10 min (or on idle) for crash-recovery safety. File: `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/periodicSnapshot.ts`.

**Behavior:**
- Per workspaceId/sandbox pair, schedule `setInterval(() => sandbox.snapshot(), 10 * 60 * 1000)`.
- Skip snapshot if no activity in last 10 min (detect via "dirty" marker set on writes).
- On snapshot: update `SandboxHandleStore` with new snapshotId.
- Stopped on sandbox dispose or backend shutdown.

**Accepted risk:** no on-dirty-turn-end snapshot ŌĆö cost amplification risk in chatty sessions. Periodic only.

**Tests:** cron triggers snapshot; dirty skip works; graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cron triggers snapshot at interval. Dirty skip works for idle sessions. Graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.
acceptance_criteria: Cron triggers snapshot at interval. Dirty skip works for idle sessions. Graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: core-M5: post-deploy smoke script ŌĆö health + signup + email arrival
description:
## Goal
A bash + Playwright script that runs against a deployed URL and verifies: /health is 200, /api/v1/config has expected branding, signup ŌåÆ verification email arrives ŌåÆ user can sign in. Runs after every deploy.

## Why it matters
"It deployed" ŌēĀ "it works." This script catches: misconfigured CORS, wrong DATABASE_URL, broken Resend domain config, missing env vars, expired secrets. Run as a final CI step on the production URL after `fly deploy`.

## Acceptance criteria
- `apps/full-app/scripts/deploy-smoke.sh` accepts `--url=https://boring-ui-v2.fly.dev` (default reads from vault deploy URL).
- Steps:
  1. `curl -fsS $URL/health` ŌåÆ expect 200 `{ ok: true }`.
  2. `curl -fsS $URL/api/v1/config` ŌåÆ expect 200 `{ appId: "boring-ui-v2", appName: "...", apiBase: ..., features: {...} }`.
  3. Playwright test: navigate to `$URL/auth/signup`, sign up with `smoke-${timestamp}@${RESEND_TEST_INBOX}` (use Resend's test inbox feature OR maildrop.cc).
  4. Poll Resend's API (`GET https://api.resend.com/emails/<id>`) for the verification email. Wait up to 60s.
  5. Extract verify URL from email HTML; navigate to it; assert success state.
  6. Assert post-signup landing on `/workspace/:id`.
  7. Sign out; sign in with same creds; assert workspace still visible.
- Exits 0 on full success, 1 with diagnostic on any failure.
- Bash + Playwright + Resend SDK (REST). No external services beyond what's already in vault.
- Pino-logged each step with structured events.

## Considerations
- Test user emails routed to Resend's "test mode" or maildrop.cc ŌĆö DON'T use real personal emails (they accumulate clutter and would fail repeated deploys).
- The smoke creates a real workspace each run; cleanup script tears them down via DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id. Or accept clutter in a dedicated `boring-ui-v2-smoke` Neon DB.
- Add to CI as a `post-deploy` job triggered by Fly's `--release-command` succeeding.

## Tests
- This bead IS the test ŌĆö but its CI integration is the meta-acceptance.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Deployment ┬¦Health checks; M5_FLY_CONFIG bead; M7_E2E bead (overlapping but smoke runs against PROD URL, E2E runs against local).
close_reason: shipped in 721982b: added post-deploy smoke TS script + CI workflow + docs + full-app typecheck coverage

title: core-M2: routes ŌĆö workspace settings (metadata GET + PUT) + runtime (GET + retry POST)
description:
## Goal
Settings (metadata-only contract) + runtime endpoints. Critical: `getWorkspaceSettings` returns metadata, NEVER decrypted values.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/settings`: requireWorkspaceMember(); returns array of `{ key, configured, updated_at }`. NEVER decrypted values.
- `PUT /api/v1/workspaces/:id/settings`: requireWorkspaceMember('editor'); body `Record<string, string>` (max 50 keys, each value non-empty, key 1-128 chars). Calls putWorkspaceSettings (encrypts internally). Returns refreshed metadata.
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/runtime`: requireWorkspaceMember(); calls getWorkspaceRuntime (auto-creates row if missing). Returns WorkspaceRuntime.
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/runtime/retry`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); calls retryWorkspaceRuntime; returns runtime or 409 if not in error state.

## Considerations
- Typed accessors (e.g. for GitHub install) live in agent v1.x and use `decryptSetting` / `encryptAndPut` low-level primitives ŌĆö NOT exposed via this generic settings endpoint.
- 50-key + 128-char-key + non-empty-value validation is v1 contract preserved.

## Tests
- GET settings returns metadata only (no value strings).
- PUT > 50 keys ŌåÆ 400 validation_failed.
- PUT key > 128 chars ŌåÆ 400.
- GET runtime on workspace without runtime row ŌåÆ returns auto-created `ready` row.
- POST runtime/retry on workspace not in error ŌåÆ 409.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦API reference ┬¦HTTP surface; ┬¦DB ┬¦Encrypted settings; ┬¦Workspace runtime.
close_reason: Settings metadata + runtime routes shipped: GET/PUT settings with 50-key/128-char validation, GET runtime auto-create, POST retry with 409 guard. 14 tests.

title: core-M2: capabilities contributor API (boot-memoized aggregation)
description:
## Goal
\`app.registerCapabilitiesContributor(name, fn)\` ŌĆö typed extension point. Runs each contributor ONCE at boot, memoizes result; \`GET /api/v1/capabilities\` serves the merged response.

## Acceptance criteria
- Fastify augmentation: \`registerCapabilitiesContributor(name: string, fn: CapabilitiesContributor): void\`.
- \`CapabilitiesContributor = (ctx: { db, config }) => Partial<CapabilitiesResponse> | Promise<Partial<CapabilitiesResponse>>\`.
- Contributors run ONCE at \`app.ready()\` time; results stored in \`app.capabilitiesCache\`.
- Response shape: \`{ core: {...}, agent?: {...}, workspace?: {...}, [contributor]: ... }\` keyed by contributor name. JSON-safe (no \`unknown\`).
- Core registers its own contributor first (\`name: 'core'\`).
- \`core: CoreCapabilities\` shape per CORE.md ┬¦API reference (version, features, auth booleans).
- \`features.emailFlows\` derived from \`config.auth.mail\` presence (not a separate config field).
- **\`auth.github\` is \`false\` in v1** ŌĆö set explicitly in the core contributor since GitHub OAuth deferred. v1.x will flip this to derived-from-config.
- \`auth.emailPassword\`, \`auth.emailVerification\`, \`auth.passwordReset\`, \`auth.magicLink\` derived from config.

## Tests
- Register agent + workspace contributors ŌåÆ response contains all three keys.
- Disable agent contributor ŌåÆ response omits \`agent\` key (no \`null\` placeholder).
- Memoization: contributor fn called once even with multiple GET requests.
- v1 boot: response \`core.auth.github === false\` (asserted explicitly).
- All tests pino tagged \`bead: 'boring-ui-v2-7431'\`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦API reference ┬¦Capabilities contributor API; ┬¦Open questions deferred to v1.x (GitHub OAuth).
close_reason: Capabilities contributor API implemented. registerCapabilitiesContributor(name, fn) on Fastify instance, boot-memoized, GET /api/v1/capabilities. Core contributor registers auth booleans + features. 7 tests.

title: Gov: Audit ŌĆö every user-facing piece follows default+primitives+hook pattern
description:
**Goal:** Ensure every chat-related UI piece ships as a trio: (a) pre-composed default component, (b) primitive(s) it uses, (c) headless hook for custom composition.

**Audit targets:**
| User piece | Default | Primitives | Hook |
|---|---|---|---|
| Chat | `<ChatPanel />` | `<Message />`, `<Composer />`, `<Tool />`, `<Terminal />`, `<CodeBlock />`, `<Reasoning />` | `useAgentChat()` |
| Sessions | `<SessionToolbar />` | (reuses shadcn `Dropdown`) | `useSessions()` |
| Slash commands | built-ins in Composer | `<SlashCommandList />` (if we add) | `useSlashCommands()` / `registerCommand()` |
| Model/thinking | `<ModelPicker />`, `<ThinkingToggle />` | (reuses shadcn `Select`) | model + thinking are inputs to `useAgentChat` |
| UI bridge (client) | ŌĆö (workspace owns display) | ŌĆö | `useUiBridge()` (if needed by workspace) |

**Enforcement:** pre-release audit checklist; fail the milestone gate if any piece is missing one of the three.

**Acceptance:** checklist in `docs/COMPONENT_PATTERNS.md`; audit run at M5 gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

Checklist in `docs/COMPONENT_PATTERNS.md`. Audit run at M5 gate. Every user-facing piece has default + primitives + hook.

title: core-M6: deep /health DB ping + 503 on db_unavailable
description:
## Goal
M2 had basic health; this bead is the hardening: DB ping with 503 + db_unavailable code on failure. Required for PaaS liveness probes.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /health` runs `SELECT 1` against `app.db` with a 2s timeout.
- 200 `{ ok: true }` on success.
- 503 `{ error, code: 'db_unavailable', message, requestId }` on DB unreachable / timeout.
- Health endpoint NEVER throws ŌĆö always returns a status code.

## Tests
- Integration: pause Postgres container; hit /health; assert 503.
- Integration: DB up; assert 200.
- Verify timeout fires within 2-3s (not 30s).

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Deployment ┬¦Health checks; ┬¦Milestones ┬¦M6.

title: M3b: Copy ai-elements into src/front/primitives/
description:
**Goal:** Adopt Vercel's ai-elements shadcn-style ŌĆö copy sources into our tree so we own them. Files: `src/front/primitives/{Message,Composer,Tool,Terminal,CodeBlock,Reasoning}.tsx`.

**Why copy (vs. import as package):** shadcn philosophy ŌĆö shipped code is ours to modify. ai-elements evolves; we freeze our version. Matches our existing shadcn/ui adoption pattern.

**Files to copy + adapt:**
- `Message` (role-based layout)
- `PromptInput` ŌåÆ rename to `Composer`
- `Tool` (generic tool call renderer with expand/collapse)
- `Terminal` (for bash stdout/stderr)
- `CodeBlock` (syntax highlighting for read)
- `Reasoning` (collapsible thinking blocks)

**Each file gets a provenance comment at the top:**
```ts
/**
 * Adapted from @ai-sdk/react's ai-elements (vercel-labs/ai).
 * Source: https://github.com/vercel-labs/ai/tree/main/packages/ai-elements
 * Copied: <date>. We own this file; upstream updates require re-port.
 */
```

**Adaptations:**
- Classnames ŌåÆ CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-message-bg`, etc.) instead of Tailwind utilities only, OR dual-system Tailwind v4 + vars.
- Replace `lucide-react` with already-adopted icon set.

**Tests:** visual smoke (storybook not required in v1) + unit: rendering shapes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 6 primitives copied. Each has provenance comment at top. CSS vars + Tailwind dual-system works.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 primitives copied. Each has provenance comment at top. CSS vars + Tailwind dual-system works.

title: TopBarSlot context bridge: BoringApp provides UserMenu, ChatTopBar consumes
description:
## Context

Today, workspace's `ChatTopBar.tsx` ships a bare avatar bubble with just an `onAvatarClick` callback ŌĆö clicking it does whatever the consuming app wires up (often nothing). Core's `<UserMenu />` is a complete dropdown (avatar trigger ŌåÆ Settings + Sign out, identity from `useUser()`) that's exported but not auto-wired anywhere. Result: every app must remember to pass `topBarRight={<UserMenu />}` to workspace's shell. If they forget, the avatar is silent.

The dep arrow today is `core ŌåÆ workspace/ui-shadcn`. Inverting it (workspace importing UserMenu from core) creates a circular dep. Solution per v7: a React context bridge.

## Scope

1. Create `packages/core/src/front/components/TopBarSlot.tsx`:
   ```ts
   const TopBarSlotContext = createContext<ReactNode | null>(null)
   export function TopBarSlotProvider({ children, slot }: { children: ReactNode; slot: ReactNode }) {
     return <TopBarSlotContext.Provider value={slot}>{children}</TopBarSlotContext.Provider>
   }
   export function useTopBarSlot(): ReactNode | null {
     return useContext(TopBarSlotContext)
   }
   ```
2. Export from core's frontend barrel.
3. In `packages/core/src/front/BoringApp.tsx`, wrap children with `<TopBarSlotProvider slot={<UserMenu />}>`.
4. In `packages/workspace/src/components/chat/ChatTopBar.tsx`:
   - Import `useTopBarSlot` from `@boring/core/front`.
   - Replace the bare avatar default with: `const slot = useTopBarSlot(); const right = topBarRight ?? slot ?? null`.
   - **Remove** the `onAvatarClick` and `userInitial` props from the type and JSX. No deprecation period (per v7 ŌĆö no consumers to migrate, no in-flight users).
5. Search the codebase for `onAvatarClick` / `userInitial` usages and remove. (Likely only `ChatCenteredShell.tsx` forwards them.)
6. Tests:
   - In `BoringApp.test.tsx`: render a child component that uses `useTopBarSlot`, assert it receives a non-null React node containing a button with aria-label "User menu".
   - In `workspace`'s `ChatTopBar.test.tsx`: render inside a `TopBarSlotProvider` with a sentinel slot; assert it appears in the top bar's right position.
7. Manual verification: start `apps/full-app`, click the avatar in the top bar, assert the dropdown opens with Settings + Sign out items.

## Why this exists

The user explicitly flagged this: "we need a user menu drop down... When clicking on avatar => should open the menu... actually the current avatar in workspace should be owned by core, not workspace." This bead is the structural fix. After it lands, no app needs to remember to wire UserMenu ŌĆö they get it for free as long as their app shell uses `BoringApp`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `TopBarSlot` provider/hook live in `packages/core/src/front/components/`.
- `BoringApp` provides `<UserMenu />` as the slot.
- Workspace's `ChatTopBar` consumes it via `useTopBarSlot`.
- `onAvatarClick` and `userInitial` props are gone from the workspace package's types.
- Manual click on the avatar in any app using `BoringApp` opens the menu.

## Considerations

- The `topBarRight` prop on `ChatTopBar` is preserved as an explicit override; the slot is the default.
- Workspace package gets a NEW dep on `@boring/core/front` ŌĆö confirm it's not a circular dep (core depends on `@boring/workspace/ui-shadcn`, but workspace would now depend on `@boring/core/front`. This IS a cycle at the package level). Mitigation: split workspace's `ui-shadcn` exports into a sub-package, OR have core's TopBarSlot expose a `useTopBarSlotRender` hook that workspace calls without importing UserMenu directly. Confirm with v7 spec section "UserMenu via TopBarSlot bridge": "No new dep" ŌĆö that's the constraint.
- If a circular dep IS introduced: pivot to having BoringApp inject UserMenu via a different mechanism (e.g. a portal, or by accepting a render prop).

## Out of scope

- Migrating `ChatCenteredShell`'s avatar handling beyond removing the deprecated props.
- Adding more menu items to UserMenu (Settings + Sign out is enough for v1).

## Spec reference

Decision #9 in v7 spec. Phase 1.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria met: TopBarSlot bridge wires UserMenu from BoringApp into ChatTopBar and removes workspace avatar props

title: Migration: apps/ide ŌåÆ v2
description:
**Goal:** Port apps/ide to v2 stack. **Current:** bwrap backend + pi browser-placement harness. **Target:** bwrap (unchanged) + pi server-placement.

**Step-by-step migration checklist:**

1. **Dependency swap** ŌĆö update `apps/ide/package.json`:
   - Remove: `@boring/ui`, any old pi deps.
   - Add: `@boring/agent@workspace:*`, `@boring/workspace@workspace:*`.

2. **Agent placement flip** ŌĆö in `apps/ide/boring.app.toml`:
   - Change `[agent].placement = "browser"` ŌåÆ `"server"`.
   - Set `[runtime].mode = "local"` (bwrap continues to work).
   - Keep `workspaceId` + `workspaceRoot` as before.

3. **Layout migration to workspace package:**
   - IDE layout (FileTree + Editor + ChatPanel + git panes) ŌåÆ workspace package's `IdeLayout` preset.
   - Delete `apps/ide/src/layouts/*` once preset compatibility verified.

4. **Backend routes** ŌĆö nothing to do: agent package serves `/api/v1/*` endpoints apps/ide consumed.

5. **Chat component swap** ŌĆö replace old `<AiChat />` with `<ChatPanel />` from `@boring/agent`.

6. **Git UI** ŌĆö REMOVE git panes + status bar (workspace plan drops git UI entirely in v1). If users need git ops, they can use the `bash` tool in chat (`git status`, `git commit`).

7. **Env vars (Fly/docker):**
   - Add: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local`, `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID=ide`, `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/app/workspaces`.
   - Keep: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

8. **Test against local dev:** `pnpm --filter apps/ide dev` ŌåÆ chat works, file tree loads, editor opens files, bash tool executes in bwrap sandbox.

9. **E2E smoke:** click through a representative user flow; verify no regressions.

**Blockers:** depends on workspace package v2 IdeLayout preset (tracked in workspace package beads).

**Estimated effort:** ~1 day.

## Acceptance Criteria

apps/ide boots with v2 packages in local (bwrap) mode. FileTree + Editor + Chat all work. No pi-browser artifacts remain. No lightningfs imports. Git UI removed (no status bar, no diff pane). All user-facing flows smoke-tested. Deployed to existing Fly target without infra changes.
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.

title: core-M3: ThemeProvider + tokens.css bridge
description:
## Goal
ThemeProvider applies `data-theme` to `<html>` based on light/dark/system; tokens.css bridges shadcn's tokens to workspace's vars so all primitives respond to theme.

## Acceptance criteria
- `<ThemeProvider>`: respects boring.app.toml `[frontend.theme] default`. Persists choice to localStorage.
- System mode: listens to `prefers-color-scheme` media query.
- `theme.css` exported as `@boring/core/theme.css` ŌĆö defines `:root` + `[data-theme="dark"]` selectors with shadcn token names (`--primary`, `--background`, `--foreground`, etc.).
- Tokens map to `@boring/workspace/ui-shadcn` token names.

## Tests
- Set theme to 'dark' ŌåÆ `<html data-theme="dark">`.
- System mode + media query change ŌåÆ theme follows.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Layer 2 ┬¦ThemeProvider; CORE.md ┬¦Subpath exports ┬¦theme.css.
