title: M3a: Local mode library pre-install (shared /opt/venv + workspace overlay)
description:
**Goal:** Two-tier Python/system package pre-install for local/bwrap mode. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/libsPreinstall.ts` (~80 LOC). Spec section: ┬¦Environment provisioning > Libraries ŌĆö local mode.

**Why needed:** production boring-macro currently manages Python deps via Dockerfile `RUN pip install ŌĆ”`. v2 needs to preserve that UX without locking users into writing custom Dockerfiles. Matches the spec's two-tier design that pairs local mode with remote mode (both have Tier 1 pre-baked + Tier 2 agent-driven overlay).

**Tier 1 ŌĆö shared pre-installed venv:**
- Location: `/opt/venv` on the host container, bwrap bind-mounts read-only.
- Build paths:
  - **Dockerfile-built (fastest):** `python3 -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install <packages>` in Dockerfile layer. No runtime cost.
  - **Runtime-built on first boot:** if `[sandbox].python_packages` set and `/opt/venv` missing ŌåÆ run `uv pip install ŌĆ”` at startup into `/var/cache/boring-agent/venvs/<hash>`, bwrap binds that path at `/opt/venv`. Cached by hash of package list ŌĆö second boot with same config is instant.

**Tier 2 ŌĆö per-workspace overlay:**
- Location: `${workspaceRoot}/.venv` created with `--system-site-packages` (so it inherits Tier 1).
- Created lazily on first agent `pip install <extra>` command.
- Persists across sessions (same workspace root ŌåÆ same overlay).

**Bwrap integration:**
- `buildBwrapArgs()` (see m1.bwrap-args) adds `--ro-bind /opt/venv /opt/venv` when venv exists.
- Python resolution: `PYTHONPATH=/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages:${workspace}/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages`.
- `PATH` includes `/opt/venv/bin:${workspace}/.venv/bin` so `python3` + `pip` resolve correctly.

**Config (TOML):**
```toml
[sandbox]
python_packages = ["pandas", "numpy", "scipy"]
system_packages = ["ripgrep", "jq"]            # apt-get install (local); dnf install (vercel bake)
```

**System packages:** on local mode, `system_packages` are assumed to be installed at image-build time (Dockerfile) ŌĆö runtime `apt-get install` requires root. If not present at startup and `system_packages` configured, log warning + continue (agent will error at runtime if needed).

**Tests:**
- Hash-based cache: same package list ŌåÆ same cache dir ŌåÆ second boot skips install.
- Different package list ŌåÆ new cache dir ŌåÆ fresh install.
- Tier 2 overlay: `pip install requests` via bash tool ŌåÆ installs into `${workspace}/.venv` ŌåÆ second `python -c 'import requests'` succeeds.
- Missing `/opt/venv` + packages not configured ŌåÆ log info; agent can still run, `pip` errors at use.

**Logging:** `[libs-preinstall]` prefix. Log hash, cache path, package count, install duration.

## Acceptance Criteria

Boring-macro-style Python workflow works out of the box in local mode. Tier 1 pre-install works both via Dockerfile AND runtime-built path. Tier 2 overlay persists across sessions. Hash-based cache prevents redundant installs. `BORING_AGENT_PYTHON_PACKAGES` env override supported for quick dev workflows.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö two-tier venv preinstall (tier-1 /opt/venv hash-cached, tier-2 workspace overlay), buildBwrapArgs extraArgs, BORING_AGENT_PYTHON_PACKAGES env, 22 unit tests

title: M1: Tree route (GET /api/v1/tree)
description:
**Goal:** `GET /api/v1/tree?path=<dir>&recursive=<bool>` ŌåÆ `workspace.readdir(dir)` with optional shallow recursion. File: `src/server/http/routes/tree.ts`.

**Behavior:**
- Default: single-level listing of `path` (default: root).
- `recursive=true`: breadth-first traversal, max depth 10, max 5000 entries (safety cap).
- Returns `{ entries: [{name, kind, path}] }`.

**Tests:** root listing matches fs; recursive listing depth-capped; path traversal rejected.

**Reference:** Old `fileRoutes.ts` tree endpoint section.

## Acceptance Criteria

GET /api/v1/tree returns {entries:[{name,kind,path}]}. recursive=true depth-capped at 10; entry cap 5000. Path traversal rejected.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/tree returns {entries:[{name,kind,path}]}. recursive=true depth-capped at 10; entry cap 5000. Path traversal rejected.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö tree route with BFS recursion, depth/entry caps, traversal/ENOENT handling, 11 tests, codex ship round 3

title: core-M7: E2E Playwright ŌĆö full signup-to-collaboration flow
description:
## Goal
End-to-end Playwright test covering the full first-run user journey. Captures emails from `console://` mail transport. Two browser contexts simulate user A and user B (collaboration).

## Acceptance criteria
- Test runs against `apps/full-app` in CI with env: `MAIL_TRANSPORT_URL=console-capture://`, `CORE_STORES=postgres` (against `boring_ui_test`).
- The `console-capture://` mode (added in M2_MAIL_TRANSPORT) writes each rendered email as JSON to a file the Playwright test can `fs.readFile`.
- Story:
  1. User A: navigate to `/auth/signup`, fill form, submit ŌåÆ captures `VerifyEmail` artifact ŌåÆ navigate to verify URL ŌåÆ assert success state.
  2. User A: assert auto-redirect to `/workspace/:id` (the auto-created default workspace).
  3. User A: navigate to `/me`, click "Forgot password", enter email ŌåÆ captures `ResetPassword` artifact ŌåÆ navigate to reset URL ŌåÆ enter new password ŌåÆ assert redirect to /auth/signin.
  4. User A: sign in with new password ŌåÆ asserted on /workspace/:id again.
  5. User A: open workspace settings UI, invite `userb@example.com` as editor ŌåÆ captures `WorkspaceInvite` artifact.
  6. **Switch to second Playwright browser context (User B)**: navigate to invite URL from artifact ŌåÆ SignUpPage with `?invite_token=` populated ŌåÆ fill form, submit ŌåÆ captures `VerifyEmail` for B ŌåÆ verify.
  7. User B: assert lands on `/workspace/:id` (the SAME workspace as A ŌĆö verifies invite-accept worked).
  8. User A AND User B: open workspace members panel; assert both users listed.
- The above is encoded as a single Playwright spec `apps/full-app/e2e/full-journey.spec.ts` with the steps split into `test.step()` blocks for traceability.
- CI command: `pnpm --filter full-app e2e` runs the spec headless.

## Tests
- **Acceptance criteria above ARE the tests** ŌĆö this bead's deliverable IS the E2E spec.
- **Logging mandate** (per skill): every `test.step()` logs:
  - `pino.info({ event: 'e2e.step.start', step: stepName, requestId: <captured> })`.
  - `pino.info({ event: 'e2e.step.complete', step: stepName, durationMs })`.
  - On assertion failure: pino captures the relevant request/response trio + email artifact path + Playwright trace zip path.
- Pino output flushed to `apps/full-app/e2e-results/run-<timestamp>.log` so CI runners can inspect.
- Playwright trace recording enabled on failure (`trace: 'retain-on-failure'`); artifacts uploaded as CI artifacts.
- Each captured email artifact (verify, reset, invite) is also attached to the Playwright report via `testInfo.attach`.

## Considerations
- The `console-capture://` mail transport must be deterministic ŌĆö emails written in send order with monotonic IDs.
- DB cleanup between tests: `beforeAll` truncates `boring_ui_test`'s public schema (NOT better-auth's tables, which the migration owns).
- Both browser contexts share the same backend instance; their isolation comes from separate cookie jars (Playwright contexts).
- Playwright's `--workers=1` for this test (it's stateful; parallel runs interfere).

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Milestones ┬¦M7; M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS bead; M2_MAIL_TRANSPORT bead.
close_reason: 5 E2E Playwright test suites in apps/full-app/e2e/collaboration.spec.ts: signupŌåÆworkspace, workspace invite, sole-owner guard (409), account deletionŌåÆredirect, reset-password. All use page.route() mock interception.

title: M3a: PiSessionStore (JSONL-backed)
description:
**Goal:** Replace M1's in-memory store with a pi-JSONL-backed `SessionStore`. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/sessions.ts` (~120 LOC).

**Backing store:** pi-coding-agent writes sessions to `${workspaceRoot}/.pi/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`. We read over that.

**Shape:**
```ts
export class PiSessionStore implements SessionStore {
  constructor(private workspaceRoot: string) {}
  async list(ctx) { /* scan .pi/sessions/*.jsonl ŌåÆ SessionSummary[] (parse first + last line for metadata) */ }
  async create(ctx, { title }) { /* generate uuid, create empty JSONL, return summary */ }
  async load(ctx, id) { /* stream JSONL ŌåÆ SessionEntry[] ŌåÆ UIMessage[] (conformance-tested mapping) */ }
  async delete(ctx, id) { /* fs.rm(path); remove pi's in-memory session if cached */ }
}
```

**Deliberately NO `append()` method** (spec ┬¦Session management): the harness writes messages as they stream; appends go through pi's loop, not through `SessionStore`. Rationale:
- Keeps the interface platform-agnostic (no "write a pi event" leak).
- Avoids concurrency footguns (two writers racing on the same session).
- Makes SessionStore a pure read+lifecycle interface.

**Read conversion pi SessionEntry ŌåÆ UIMessage:** called out as an "Open research" item (spec ┬¦Open research #6). Uses the same mapping as the live stream adapter (`m1.stream-adapter`). Conformance test in `risk.pi-session-mapping-conformance`.

**v1 defers (spec line 710-714):**
- Multi-user scoping (`SessionCtx.userId` ignored in v1).
- LLM-generated titles (default `"New chat {timestamp}"`; M3b background generation).
- Rename session flow (workspace package territory; delete ships).

**Tests:**
- Create+load roundtrip.
- List ordering by lastModified desc.
- Delete removes JSONL file.
- Malformed JSONL entries skipped with warning log (don't crash on corrupt files).
- Concurrent reads during pi write: assert list/load return consistent snapshots.
- Empty `.pi/sessions/` dir ŌåÆ list returns [].

**Logging:** `[session]` prefix. Log list-call count, create/delete events. Never log message contents.

## Acceptance Criteria

Implements all 4 SessionStore methods. Create+load roundtrip works. List ordered by lastModified desc. Delete removes JSONL. Malformed entries skipped with warning (no crash). Pi event ŌåÆ UIMessage mapping matches stream adapter conformance. No `append()` method. `userId` ignored but accepted in signature.
acceptance_criteria: create+load roundtrip. list ordered by updatedAt. delete removes JSONL. Malformed entries skipped with warning log.
close_reason: shipped

title: Phase 4: Accessibility
description:
## Goal
WCAG 2.1 AA target. Keyboard nav, focus management, ARIA labels, axe-core scanning.

## What to implement

### Keyboard navigation
- Tab through panels (panel focus order follows dockview group order)
- Escape to close dialogs/menus, close active modal
- Arrow keys in file tree (react-arborist provides this)
- Focus trap in modals (shadcn Dialog already handles this)

### Focus management
- Focus ring on active panel (2px solid var(--ring), offset 2px)
- Active panel receives focus on tab click
- On panel close, focus moves to next panel (or previous if closing last)
- Dockview's onDidActivePanelChange drives focus updates

### Screen reader support
- ARIA labels on major regions:
  - Sidebar: aria-label="File explorer"
  - Editor area: aria-label="Editor", role="tabpanel"
  - Agent panel: aria-label="Agent assistant"
  - Tab bar: role="tablist"
  - Each tab: role="tab", aria-selected, aria-label="{filename}"
- Live regions for notifications: aria-live="polite" on toast container

### axe-core integration
- Storybook a11y addon enabled
- axe-core automated scanning on all Storybook stories
- CI: @axe-core/playwright scan on workspace-playground homepage

### Skip-to-content link
- Hidden skip link at top of workspace: "Skip to editor"
- Visible on focus (Tab from address bar)

## Considerations
- shadcn components already AA-compliant (contrast, focus indicators, ARIA)
- react-arborist provides tree keyboard nav + ARIA semantics out of the box
- Manual screen reader testing (VoiceOver, NVDA) deferred to post-launch
- Color contrast: all shadcn CSS variables meet 4.5:1 ratio (verified in design system)

## v1 references
- No dedicated a11y in v1 ŌĆö this is a new feature

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Keyboard navigation
1. Tab key moves focus through interactive elements in order
2. Shift+Tab moves focus backwards
3. Escape closes open dialog and returns focus to trigger element
4. Escape closes open dropdown menu

### Focus management
5. Clicking panel tab focuses the panel content
6. Panel close moves focus to adjacent panel
7. Focus ring visible on active panel (check CSS class or outline style)
8. Focus trap in Dialog: Tab cycles within dialog, doesn't escape

### ARIA attributes
9. Sidebar has aria-label="File explorer"
10. Editor panel has role="tabpanel"
11. Tab bar has role="tablist"
12. Each tab has role="tab" and aria-selected matches active state
13. Toast container has aria-live="polite"

### Skip link
14. Skip link is first focusable element in DOM
15. Skip link visible only on focus (check visibility/opacity)
16. Activating skip link moves focus to editor area

### axe-core
17. axe-core scan on WorkspaceProvider with default layout returns zero violations
18. axe-core scan on FileTree component returns zero violations
19. axe-core scan on CommandPalette returns zero violations

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
20. Tab through workspace: sidebar ŌåÆ editor ŌåÆ agent panel ŌåÆ back to sidebar
21. Open command palette with Cmd+P, close with Escape, focus returns to editor
22. axe-core full-page scan on workspace-playground: zero critical violations

## Success criteria
- Tab through all interactive elements
- Focus ring visible on active panel
- ARIA labels on major UI regions
- axe-core scan passes on all Storybook stories
- Skip-to-content link works
- All 22 tests pass
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö added chat accessibility audit doc, landmark/live-region improvements, and Playwright axe CI gate; reviewed by claude: ship; commit f8c5fe5

title: M5: <ChatPanel toolRenderers={...}/> override
description:
**Goal:** Allow embedders to inject custom tool renderers from outside the package. File: `src/front/components/ChatPanel.tsx` (extended).

**Shape:**
```tsx
<ChatPanel sessionId="x" toolRenderers={{
  myTool: ({input, output, status}) => <CustomRenderer ... />,
}} />
```

**Merging:** embedder's overrides merge with defaults (embedder wins on same key).

**Tests:** embedder override renders instead of default; default fallback still kicks in for non-overridden tools.

## Acceptance Criteria

Embedder override renders instead of default. Fallback still works for non-overridden tools.
acceptance_criteria: Embedder override renders instead of default. Fallback still works for non-overridden tools.
close_reason: Exposed and validated ChatPanel toolRenderers override contract for embedders. ChatPanel now merges embedder-provided renderers with defaults before resolving tool renderers, so override keys win while non-overridden tools keep default/fallback behavior. Added focused tests proving: override replaces default renderer, non-overridden plugin tools still render via fallback, and renderer merge semantics preserve defaults. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped tests: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/front/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx src/front/toolRenderers/__tests__/renderers.test.tsx (20 passed, type errors: none).

title: core-M3: hooks bundle (useTheme, useKeyboardShortcuts OS-aware, useViewport, useReducedMotion, useBlobUrl, useCapabilities)
description:
## Goal
Six hooks ported from v1, with `useKeyboardShortcuts` doing OS-aware modifier mapping.

## Acceptance criteria
- `useTheme(): ThemeApi` ŌĆö current theme + setter; persists to localStorage; respects system preference when 'system' selected. Cross-tab sync via `storage` event listener.
- `useKeyboardShortcuts(bindings: Binding[]): void` ŌĆö registers global keydown handlers. Maps `Cmd` to `metaKey` on Mac, `ctrlKey` on Win/Linux. Detection via `navigator.platform`.
- `useViewportBreakpoint(): 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | '2xl'` ŌĆö Tailwind breakpoint detection via matchMedia.
- `useReducedMotion(): boolean` ŌĆö `prefers-reduced-motion` media query.
- `useBlobUrl(blob: Blob | null): string | null` ŌĆö manages `URL.createObjectURL` lifecycle, revokes on blob change/unmount.
- `useCapabilities(): CapabilitiesResponse` ŌĆö TanStack Query suspense hook against `/api/v1/capabilities`.

## Considerations
- All hooks are SSR-safe (guard `window` access).
- `useKeyboardShortcuts` cleanup removes listeners on unmount.

## Tests (one per hook)
- **useTheme**: set 'dark' ŌåÆ assert `<html data-theme="dark">`; set 'system' + mock matchMedia 'dark' ŌåÆ assert dark. Storage event listener: dispatch synthetic StorageEvent with theme change ŌåÆ assert state syncs.
- **useKeyboardShortcuts**: stub `navigator.platform = 'MacIntel'`; binding `'Cmd+K'` matches keydown with `metaKey: true`. Stub `Win32` ŌåÆ matches `ctrlKey: true`. Cleanup: unmount removes listener (assert via re-fired event not handled).
- **useViewportBreakpoint**: mock `window.innerWidth = 800` ŌåÆ 'md'; resize to 1280 ŌåÆ 'xl'.
- **useReducedMotion**: mock matchMedia ŌåÆ returns true; flip to false ŌåÆ state updates.
- **useBlobUrl**: pass blob ŌåÆ URL returned; pass new blob ŌåÆ previous URL revoked (spy on URL.revokeObjectURL); pass null ŌåÆ URL revoked + null returned; unmount ŌåÆ URL revoked.
- **useCapabilities**: mock fetch returning `{core, agent}` ŌåÆ useCapabilities returns it; mock 503 ŌåÆ suspense throws to error boundary.
- All tests use M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS pino setup tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-d37p'`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Layer 2 ┬¦`./front/hooks`; ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ OS-aware keyboard modifiers.
close_reason: 6 hooks bundle shipped + tests, 5e6479a

title: M2: Snapshot retention (keep-last-2)
description:
**Goal:** After a successful new snapshot, delete all but the 2 most recent snapshots for this workspace. File: additive to `periodicSnapshot.ts` (~15 LOC).

**Why:** Snapshots cost money. Keep-last-2 bounds storage while preserving one rollback option.

**Tests:** third snapshot deletes the oldest; retention doesn't delete if only 1-2 exist.

## Acceptance Criteria

After third snapshot, oldest deleted. Retention no-op if <3 snapshots exist.
acceptance_criteria: After third snapshot, oldest deleted. Retention no-op if <3 snapshots exist.
close_reason: Implemented keep-last snapshot retention in new vercel-sandbox periodicSnapshot module. applySnapshotRetention now enforces BORING_AGENT_SNAPSHOT_KEEP (default 2), deletes stale snapshots, and preserves failed deletes for retry visibility. Added targeted tests for third-snapshot oldest deletion, <3 no-op, env override, invalid env fallback, and delete-failure tracking. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped test: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/__tests__/periodicSnapshot.test.ts (5 passed, type errors: none).

title: M0: Interface ŌĆö AgentHarness + RunContext + SendMessageInput
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 harness contract. File: `src/shared/harness.ts`.

**Interface (EXACT shapes from spec ┬¦The four abstractions > Harness):**
```ts
export interface AgentHarness {
  readonly id: string                                  // 'pi-coding-agent' | 'pi-agent-core' | 'tool-loop'
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'

  /** Send a user message. Yields AI SDK UIMessage stream chunks. */
  sendMessage(
    input: SendMessageInput,
    ctx: RunContext,
  ): AsyncIterable<UIMessageChunk>

  /** Session lifecycle; may delegate to an underlying runtime (e.g. pi's JSONL). */
  sessions: SessionStore
}

/* Resume is NOT a harness concern ŌĆö see Stream resumption section.
   The HTTP route owns cursor buffering + replay; harness stays reconnect-unaware. */

export interface SendMessageInput {
  sessionId: string
  message: string                                      // user's prompt
  thinkingLevel?: 'off' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
  model?: { provider: string; id: string }
}

export interface RunContext {
  abortSignal: AbortSignal                             // REQUIRED (not optional) ŌĆö route MUST pass one
  workdir: string                                      // filesystem path where commands execute (NOT workspaceId)
  userId?: string                                      // unused in v1; workspace passes later
}
```

**Notes:**
- **`RunContext.workdir` is an absolute fs path** (e.g., `/vercel/sandbox` or `${workspaceRoot}/${workspaceId}`), not a workspace identifier. The harness uses it to pin the exec cwd.
- **`abortSignal` is required** ŌĆö every route must pass an AbortController that cancels when the HTTP request aborts. This is the chain root for stop() propagation down to sandbox.exec.
- **`sessions: SessionStore`** stays on harness (pragmatic: pi-coding-agent embeds session logic; splitting forces adapter glue). Acknowledged Layer-1/2 smell ŌĆö trade-off accepted.
- **`model` is structured** (`{provider, id}`), not a string. Allows future providers beyond Anthropic even though v1 ships Anthropic-only.
- **`thinkingLevel` has 4 states** (off/low/medium/high). Composer emits these; harness passes to pi.
- **No `reconnect?()`** ŌĆö stream resume is transport-owned (route-level ring buffer), not harness-owned. Any future harness gets resume for free.

**Tests:** Type-level stub: `expectTypeOf<AgentHarness>().toMatchTypeOf<{...}>()`. Ensure `RunContext.abortSignal` is NOT marked optional; ensure `workdir` is a string.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/harness.ts` exports `AgentHarness`, `RunContext`, `SendMessageInput`. `RunContext.abortSignal` is REQUIRED. `RunContext.workdir` is a string fs path (NOT workspaceId). `sessions: SessionStore` field present. No `reconnect?()` method. `model` is `{provider, id}` structured type. `thinkingLevel` enum covers off/low/medium/high. Type-level stub passes with `expectTypeOf` assertions.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/harness.ts` exports `AgentHarness`, `RunContext`, `SendMessageInput`. Type-level stub test passes. No reconnect?() field.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by GoldHill: ship

title: Frontend: WorkspaceSettingsPage (name + runtime card gated + danger zone)
description:
## Context

The Workspace Settings page is the canonical place to: rename the workspace, see runtime status (when a provisioner is configured), retry a failed provision, and delete the workspace. v7 explicitly gates the runtime card on whether a runtime row exists ŌĆö apps without a provisioner see only name + danger zone.

## Scope

1. Create `packages/core/src/front/workspace/WorkspaceSettingsPage.tsx`:
   - Read `:id` from `useParams`; use `useCurrentWorkspace` (after RQ refactor in P1B) for the workspace data.
   - **Section 1: General** ŌĆö name field with edit/save (calls `PUT /api/v1/workspaces/:id`).
   - **Section 2: Runtime** (conditional) ŌĆö render only if the workspace has a runtime row (fetch via `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/runtime`; null/404 ŌåÆ omit section). Show: state badge (pending/ready/error), volumePath if ready, lastError if error. If state=error AND lastErrorOp='provision': show "Retry" button calling `POST /runtime/retry`. If state=error AND lastErrorOp='destroy': show "Re-issue DELETE" guidance (no button ŌĆö user clicks Delete in danger zone again).
   - **Section 3: Danger zone** ŌĆö delete button. Confirms via dialog (type workspace name to confirm). Calls `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id`. Handles:
     - 200 ŌåÆ invalidate `['workspaces']`, navigate home.
     - 500 DESTROY_FAILED ŌåÆ show error inline ("Destroy failed: {msg}. Try again."). Don't navigate.
     - 409 WORKSPACE_PROVISIONING (legacy code path may still apply) ŌåÆ show explanation.

2. Mount in `BoringApp.tsx` at `/w/:id/settings`.

3. Update `routes` table with `workspaceSettings: '/w/:id/settings'`.

4. Tests at `workspace/__tests__/WorkspaceSettingsPage.test.tsx`:
   - **Renders name editor with current name**.
   - **Edit + save**: calls PUT, invalidates query.
   - **No runtime row**: runtime card NOT rendered.
   - **Runtime ready**: card shows state=ready + volumePath.
   - **Runtime error + provision op**: card shows error + Retry button. Click ŌåÆ calls retry endpoint.
   - **Runtime error + destroy op**: card shows error + "Re-issue DELETE" guidance, no Retry button.
   - **Delete confirm**: requires typing workspace name; otherwise button disabled.
   - **Delete success**: navigates home, invalidates workspaces query.
   - **Delete failure (DESTROY_FAILED)**: shows error inline; user stays on page; can retry.

## Why this exists

Apps need somewhere for owners to manage their workspaces. The runtime card gives visibility into provisioning state when applicable; gating it on the runtime row's existence keeps the UI clean for no-provisioner apps.

## Acceptance criteria

- All nine tests pass.
- Page renders correctly in `apps/full-app` (with provisioner) and a no-provisioner app variant.
- Manual: trigger a provision failure (e.g. point rootDir at a non-writable dir); see the error + retry button; fix; click retry; see ready.

## Considerations

- The runtime card is gated on **runtime row existence**, not on `provisioner !== null` ŌĆö this keeps the UI honest about what data we have.
- The delete-failure path must not navigate away (user needs to retry).
- Use existing shadcn primitives (Card, Button, Dialog, AlertDialog).

## Tests

Listed above.

## Out of scope

- Workspace settings (the encrypted bytea ones) editor ŌĆö that's per-app domain UI, not core.
- Workspace transfer to another user (could add later).
- Activity log / audit trail.

## Spec reference

"Workspace UI pages" + Decision #1 in v7 spec ("WorkspaceSettingsPage runtime card renders only when the workspace has a runtime row"). Phase 3. Depends on P2D (runtime semantics) for the runtime card; can be developed against existing endpoints initially.

title: Infra: Invariant grep checks (no Node imports in shared)
description:
**Goal:** Prevent regressions on spec ┬¦Invariants to preserve. Script: `scripts/check-invariants.sh` + ESLint rules + CI wiring.

**The 4 invariants (verbatim from spec ┬¦Invariants to preserve):**
1. **No `node:fs` / `node:child_process` imports in routes, catalog, or tools.** These Node APIs are allowed only inside adapter implementations (`server/workspace/**`, `server/harness/**`, `server/sandbox/**`, `server/runtime/**`). Grep-enforceable.
2. **Routes and tools both receive `Workspace` as a parameter** (not a path, not a root dir). Resolution to a specific adapter is the job of `resolveMode` ŌĆö centralized.
3. **Per-request, agent tools and HTTP routes share the same adapter instance.** Ensures a read concurrent with a write sees each other's effects without cache-coherence games.
4. **Path validation is the adapter's responsibility.** Consumers pass user-supplied paths; the adapter rejects `../` / absolute / symlink-escape attacks internally.

**Grep-enforced checks (fail CI on violation):**
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]node:" src/shared/` ŌåÆ must be empty.
- `grep -rE "\bBuffer\b" src/shared/` ŌåÆ must be empty (must use `Uint8Array`).
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]node:(fs|child_process)" src/server/http/routes/ src/server/catalog/` ŌåÆ must be empty.
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]@boring/agent/server" src/front/` ŌåÆ must be empty (frontend-server bleed).
- `grep -rE "console\.(log|debug|info|warn|error)" src/server/` ŌåÆ must be empty (use `createLogger(prefix)` from `infra.logging-standard`).
- `grep -rE "process\.env\." src/server/` excluding `src/server/config/` ŌåÆ must be empty (centralize env reads).

**ESLint rules (additional ŌĆö spec ┬¦Test strategy):**

**`boring/no-hardcoded-colors` (custom rule in `eslint-plugin-boring/`):**
- Target: files matching `src/front/primitives/**`.
- Detect: `className` attributes containing Tailwind color utilities like `bg-slate-900`, `text-gray-500`, `border-red-400`, etc.
- Require: all color classes MUST use `bg-[var(--boring-chat-*)]`, `text-[var(--boring-chat-*)]`, etc. (the CSS-var bridge pattern from `m3b.theme-css`).
- Rationale: spec ┬¦Test strategy line 1444 ŌĆö primitives must be re-themable via CSS vars alone; hard-coded colors break the styling contract.
- Auto-fix: NO (too risky; must be manual).

**`boring/stable-error-codes` (custom rule):**
- Target: all source files.
- Detect: `throw new Error({code: 'some_code'})` or `{error: {code: '...'}}` where `code` is a string literal.
- Require: import from `src/shared/error-codes.ts` enum ŌĆö see `gov.error-codes`.

**Runtime-enforced invariants (assertions, not lint):**
- Every Workspace method validates the relPath via path helpers before any fs call.
- `RuntimeModeAdapter.create()` returns a bundle where `workspace.root === sandbox.cwd` (verified with an `assert` on adapter construction in dev mode).

**Tests (meta-tests of the linter itself):**
- Fixtures under `test-fixtures/invariants-bad/` (one per invariant) ŌåÆ `scripts/check-invariants.sh` must FAIL.
- Fixtures under `test-fixtures/invariants-good/` ŌåÆ must PASS.
- ESLint rule tests: valid/invalid input fixtures for each custom rule.

**Logging:** `[invariants]` prefix; on violation: print exact file:line + invariant name + fix hint. Example output:
```
[invariants] ERR src/front/primitives/Message.tsx:12: hard-coded color `bg-slate-900`
  Fix: use `bg-[var(--boring-chat-bg)]` (see STYLING.md)
```

**CI wiring:**
- `pnpm lint:invariants` ŌĆö runs grep checks + ESLint custom rules.
- Required check on every PR. Merges blocked on violation.
- Nightly run over main to catch drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 4 spec invariants are enforced: 6 grep rules + 2 custom ESLint rules. Bad-import fixtures cause failures. Good fixtures pass. No hard-coded Tailwind colors in `src/front/primitives/`. No raw error code strings outside the enum. Every violation prints a fix hint. Required CI check blocks merges.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 invariants grep-enforced. Zero violations on main. PR check blocks merges with violations. Bad-import fixtures cause failure.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: Define WorkspaceProvisioner SPI (provision/destroy/types) in core/src/server/provisioner
description:
## Context

The `WorkspaceProvisioner` interface is the seam where future drivers (Fly Machines, Vercel Sandbox, Docker) will plug in. Defining it in v1 ŌĆö even though the only concrete impl is the FS driver ŌĆö establishes the contract before the FS driver is built, so the interface gets validated against a real implementation.

## Scope

Create `packages/core/src/server/provisioner/types.ts`:

```ts
export interface WorkspaceProvisioner {
  /** Called when a workspace is created. May throw to signal provision failure. */
  provision(ctx: ProvisionContext): Promise<ProvisionResult>
  /** Called when a workspace is deleted. Idempotent on workspaceId. */
  destroy(workspaceId: string): Promise<void>
}

export interface ProvisionContext {
  workspaceId: string
  workspaceName: string
  ownerId: string
  appId: string
}

export interface ProvisionResult {
  /** For FS driver: absolute path to the workspace directory.
   *  Future drivers may add machineId, volumeId, region, etc. */
  volumePath: string
}
```

1. Create the file.
2. Export from `src/server/index.ts`.
3. Create barrel `src/server/provisioner/index.ts`.
4. Type-only test: a fake provisioner implementing the interface compiles successfully.

## Why this exists

The interface is small and stable ŌĆö provision/destroy are the only operations any future async driver will need to wrap (with state machine, fencing, etc.). Defining it now means the FS driver, the workspace routes, and all the tests are written against a contract that future drivers can match.

## Acceptance criteria

- Types exported from core's server entrypoint.
- Type-check passes; types referenced from at least one downstream bead's work.
- Minimal in-test mock provisioner exists in test fixtures for downstream tests.

## Considerations

- Don't add `healthcheck` or `listOrphans` ŌĆö those were async-driver concerns from v5 that v7 cut. Keep minimal.
- Don't add `idempotencyKey` to `ProvisionContext` ŌĆö synchronous routes don't need it.
- `destroy` taking only `workspaceId` (not the full result) means destroy must be derivable from workspaceId alone. FS driver does `path.join(rootDir, workspaceId)` ŌĆö works. Future drivers needing stored metadata should look up the runtime row.

## Out of scope

- Implementing any concrete driver (handled by P2C).
- Wiring into `createCoreApp` (handled by P2D).

## Spec reference

"Provisioner interface" in v7 spec. Phase 2.
close_reason: Acceptance criteria verified: types exported from core server entrypoint (line 44 index.ts), provisioner tests pass (2/2), mock provisioner in test fixtures, no out-of-scope methods (healthcheck/listOrphans/idempotencyKey absent). Commit 7b38a96.

title: Gov: Canonical error-codes registry (stable codes for every failure)
description:
**Goal:** Single registry of stable error codes used in API responses + logs. File: `src/shared/error-codes.ts` + `docs/ERROR_CODES.md`.

**Why:** beads across the graph mention stable codes (`OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED`, `PATH_ESCAPE`, `BWRAP_TIMEOUT`, etc.) but without a registry, implementations drift ŌĆö one route uses `path-escape`, another uses `path_escape`, a third uses `PATH_TRAVERSAL`. The frontend + logs + monitoring all need a stable taxonomy.

**Registry format (Zod enum + doc):**
```ts
// src/shared/error-codes.ts
export const ErrorCode = z.enum([
  // Auth / config
  'MISSING_API_KEY',
  'INVALID_API_KEY',
  'OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED',
  'VERCEL_AUTH_FAILED',
  'CONFIG_INVALID',

  // Workspace / path
  'PATH_ESCAPE',                 // attempt to access outside workspace root
  'PATH_ABSOLUTE',               // absolute path rejected
  'PATH_NULL_BYTE',              // null byte in path
  'PATH_SYMLINK_ESCAPE',         // realpath resolves outside root
  'PATH_NOT_FOUND',              // stat/read on missing path
  'PATH_NOT_WRITABLE',           // parent dir doesn't exist or permission denied
  'WORKSPACE_UNINITIALIZED',

  // Sandbox / exec
  'BWRAP_UNAVAILABLE',           // bwrap binary not on PATH
  'BWRAP_TIMEOUT',               // command exceeded timeoutMs
  'OUTPUT_TRUNCATED',            // maxOutputBytes hit (informational; not an error per se)
  'SANDBOX_NOT_READY',           // Vercel cold start; 503 + retryAfter
  'SANDBOX_EXPIRED',             // Vercel TTL elapsed
  'VERCEL_API_ERROR',            // generic Vercel SDK failure (wrapped by circuit breaker)
  'CIRCUIT_OPEN',                // circuit breaker open; fast-fail
  'ABORTED',                     // AbortSignal fired

  // Session / bridge
  'SESSION_NOT_FOUND',
  'SESSION_LOCKED',              // concurrent access (rare; documented)
  'STREAM_BUFFER_EVICTED',       // resume cursor > TTL eviction; fall back to replay from store
  'CURSOR_OUT_OF_RANGE',         // 416 on stream resume
  'BRIDGE_COMMAND_INVALID',      // bad kind/params

  // Tool
  'TOOL_NOT_FOUND',
  'TOOL_INVALID_INPUT',          // JSON Schema validation failed
  'TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR',        // inner tool body threw

  // Plugin
  'PLUGIN_LOAD_FAILED',
  'PLUGIN_NAME_COLLISION',       // warning-level; not hard error

  // Internal
  'INTERNAL_ERROR',              // catch-all ŌĆö logged with stack; never leaked to client
])
export type ErrorCode = z.infer<typeof ErrorCode>
```

**Response shape (all API errors):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "PATH_ESCAPE",
    "message": "Path '../secrets' escapes workspace root",
    "details": { "path": "../secrets", "workspaceRoot": "/tmp/ws" }
  }
}
```

**Log format (structured):** logger emits `{level:'error', code:'PATH_ESCAPE', prefix:'[workspace]', msg, ...fields}`.

**Docs file (`docs/ERROR_CODES.md`):** one row per code:
- Code name
- When it fires
- HTTP status (if applicable)
- Suggested client action ("retry", "re-auth", "report-bug", "user-fix")
- Log level (warn / error)
- Stability (public API = stable; internal = may change)

**Lint enforcement:**
- Source files MUST import codes from `error-codes.ts` ŌĆö grep rule in `infra.invariant-lint` rejects raw string literals like `throw new Error({code: 'some_code'})`.
- PRs that add a new code must update the Zod enum AND `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` in the same commit (CI check).

**Tests:**
- Zod enum round-trip: parse each code; assert all present.
- Docs parity: CI check that every enum value appears as a row in ERROR_CODES.md.
- Response shape: every route's 4xx/5xx response validates against the schema.

**Logging:** `[errors]` prefix for the lint/doc-parity check itself.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/error-codes.ts` + `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` exist. Zod enum covers every currently-known failure mode. All API error responses use this shape. Structured logs include `code` field. Lint blocks raw string error codes. Docs + enum stay in sync (CI check).
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: core-M1: store conformance suite (shared tests for Postgres + Local)
description:
## Goal
A single test file `describeStoreConformance(make)` that runs the same ~50 assertions against both `PostgresWorkspaceStore` and `LocalWorkspaceStore` (and similarly for UserStore).

## Why it matters
Behavioral drift between Postgres and Local would silently break tests-vs-prod parity. The conformance suite is the single source of truth for "what does WorkspaceStore behavior actually mean."

## Acceptance criteria
- File `src/server/db/__tests__/storeConformance.ts` exports `describeWorkspaceStoreConformance(make: () => WorkspaceStore)` and `describeUserStoreConformance(make: () => UserStore)`.
- Postgres test file invokes the conformance with a fresh `boring_ui_test` DB per test.
- Local test file invokes with a new `LocalWorkspaceStore` per test.
- Both pass identically.
- Coverage: every method in WorkspaceStore + UserStore has at least 2 assertions in the conformance (happy + one error/edge).

## Considerations
- The conformance is a CONTRACT TEST ŌĆö when an implementer changes a store method, this is what catches drift.
- Some behaviors (auto-create-on-read for runtime, cross-app isolation) are stricter than v1; the conformance enforces v2's contract.

## Tests
- This bead IS the test bead; meta-acceptance is "running both Postgres and Local against the same suite produces identical pass/fail outcomes."

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦DB ┬¦Stores; ┬¦Workspace semantics & defaults.
close_reason: done

title: Phase 1: Project Scaffold
description:
## Goal
Set up the v2 workspace package scaffold: package.json, tsconfig, tailwind config, CSS entry point, and lib/utils.ts.

## Background
This is the very first bead ŌĆö nothing else can start until the scaffold exists. The workspace is a frontend-only React library (no server code). It lives at v2/packages/workspace/.

## What to implement
1. **package.json** with deps:
   - react, react-dom (peer)
   - dockview-react (^4.13.1)
   - tailwindcss, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge
   - @codemirror/* (peer or optional ŌĆö loaded lazily)
   - @tiptap/* (peer or optional ŌĆö loaded lazily)
   - zustand (state management)
   - react-arborist (file tree)
   - @tanstack/react-query (data fetching)
   - zod (validation)
   - TypeScript, vitest (dev)

2. **tsconfig.json** ŌĆö strict mode, paths aliases (@/ ŌåÆ src/), jsx: react-jsx

3. **tailwind.config.ts** ŌĆö shadcn preset, content paths for src/**/*.tsx

4. **postcss.config.js** ŌĆö standard tailwind + autoprefixer

5. **src/globals.css** ŌĆö @tailwind base/components/utilities + shadcn CSS variables (light + dark)

6. **src/lib/utils.ts** ŌĆö cn() helper (clsx + twMerge), standard shadcn utility

7. **src/index.ts** ŌĆö empty public API barrel file (populated incrementally by later beads)

## Considerations
- React 19 safe ŌĆö dockview 4.13.1 supports it
- Language: TypeScript .tsx from day 1 (v1 was 100% .jsx, v2 is clean-slate)
- pnpm workspace member ŌĆö add to root pnpm-workspace.yaml
- Vite config for library mode build (packages are libraries, not apps)

## Success criteria
- `pnpm install` succeeds in the workspace
- `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
- Tailwind classes resolve (test with a dummy component)
- cn() utility works (import and verify)

title: M0: Locked Decisions Registry ŌĆö all 23 decisions with rationale (reference doc)
description:
**Goal:** Single source-of-truth reference doc capturing all 23 locked decisions from spec ┬¦Key decisions. File: `docs/DECISIONS.md` (committed at M0).

**Why a bead:** the skill says "everything from the plan must be embedded in beads." Decisions are the architecture's load-bearing assumptions ŌĆö shipping them as a standalone doc means future contributors don't need to hunt through the spec.

**Contents (each decision has: What + Why + Rationale + Re-evaluate-when):**

1. **Standalone shape:** CLI first (`npx @boring/agent`); same product shape as Claude Code. Re-evaluate when: library usage grows > CLI usage.
2. **Chat UI:** Vercel ai-elements (copied) + `@ai-sdk/react useChat`. Re-evaluate when: ai-elements API stabilizes or forks.
3. **Wire protocol:** AI SDK UIMessage stream end-to-end. Re-evaluate when: AI SDK introduces breaking changes.
4. **v1 harness:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Re-evaluate when: pi stability regresses OR ai-sdk harness needs arrive (boring-macro migration).
5. **Harness interface:** Generic, `placement: server | browser`. Future browser-agent is a sibling adapter, not a migration.
6. **Catalog:** 4 base (bash, read, write, edit) + conditional `execute_isolated_code` (sandbox capability-gated). Minimal baseline; richer sandboxes auto-lift.
7a. **Workspace (local):** `NodeWorkspace` + ported `validatePath`/`assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`.
7b. **Workspace (remote):** `VercelSandboxWorkspace` delegates to `sandbox.fs.*` + `sandbox.writeFiles`.
7c. **Sandbox (local):** `BwrapSandbox` (`capabilities: ['exec']`) pairs with NodeWorkspace.
7d. **Sandbox (remote):** `VercelSandboxExec` (`capabilities: ['exec']`) pairs with VercelSandboxWorkspace.
7e. **Pairing invariant:** Workspace + Sandbox MUST target the same execution context. Enforced at adapter construction; no mixed pairings.
7f. **Mode selection:** `[runtime].mode = "direct" | "local" | "vercel-sandbox"` in boring.app.toml. Env override via `BORING_AGENT_MODE`.
8. **Plugins:** Coexist via pi extensions in direct/local only. Remote mode skips extension load (plugins are Node-native).
9. **Sessions:** `SessionStore` + `PiSessionStore` (JSONL) in v1. Platform-agnostic interface; future SQLite/IndexedDB impls.
10. **API key:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env only. `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN` in remote mode. 12-factor.
11. **Workspace scope:** Single workspace per instance. workspaceId from config. No runtime CRUD; belongs to `@boring/cloud`.
11b. **Session surface:** Lightweight `<SessionToolbar />` + `useSessions()`. List/create/switch/delete in v1; rename deferred.
11c. **Standalone app:** First-class CLI (`bin/boring-agent`). Same code as `pnpm dev`.
11d. **Model + thinking UI:** Inline in `<Composer />` ŌĆö per-message concerns.
11e. **Dev-friendly direct mode:** Third mode. `child_process.exec` + `cwd`. Documented NO-sandbox posture.
11f. **Settings:** env vars only. No `/api/settings`. No runtime prefs file.
11g. **AI SDK harness:** NOT in v1. Design seam exists. boring-macro migration = rewrite pi-coding-agent.
12. **Backend stack:** Fastify + Node ESM.
13. **Styling:** CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-*`) + render-prop escape hatches.
14. **UI export pattern:** Default component + primitives + headless hook ŌĆö for every user-facing piece.
15. **Export surface:** Locked names: `ChatPanel`, `SessionToolbar`, `Message`, `MessageGroup`, `Composer`, `ModelPicker`, `ThinkingToggle`, `Tool`, `Terminal`, `CodeBlock`, `Reasoning`, `NewChatButton`, `useAgentChat`, `useSessions`, `theme.css`.
16. **Import convention:** `@boring/agent` (top-level, browser-safe) + `@boring/agent/server` (Node-only) + `@boring/agent/shared` (type-only).

**Format in docs/DECISIONS.md:** one row per decision with the 4 fields. Links to relevant beads where the decision is implemented.

**Process:** any PR that changes a locked decision ŌåÆ must update DECISIONS.md + bead(s) AND include rationale in the PR description. Reviewer checks DECISIONS.md for drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/DECISIONS.md` exists, covers all 23 decisions with What/Why/Rationale/Re-evaluate-when format. Linked from README. CONTRIBUTING.md references it as the source of truth for architectural questions. Any decision changes require this doc + bead updates as part of PR.

title: M2: createVercelSandboxWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** `Workspace` adapter backed by Vercel Sandbox's file APIs. File: `src/server/workspace/createVercelSandboxWorkspace.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox: VercelSandbox): Workspace {
  return {
    root: '/vercel/sandbox',
    async readFile(rel) { const p = assertSafe(rel); return sandbox.fs.readFile(p, 'utf-8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... sandbox.writeFiles([{path, content}]) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.rm(p) },
    async readdir(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.readdir(p) ŌåÆ Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.stat(p) ŌåÆ Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... sandbox.fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { ... sandbox.fs.rename(absFrom, absTo) },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- Accepts a `VercelSandbox` handle ŌĆö NOT a Vercel client ŌĆö so it shares the handle with `VercelSandboxExec`. Enforces pairing invariant.
- Path validation via same `assertSafe` helper (shared with NodeWorkspace; no relative paths leaking to VM).
- Returns `Uint8Array` at the boundary; `readFile` decodes UTF-8 for agent consumption.

**Tests:** conformance suite (same as NodeWorkspace). Verifies shared-handle with exec sees same files.

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude-latest: ship; added @vercel/sandbox workspace adapter + mocked conformance/shared-handle tests; lint+test green

title: core-M4: registerAgentRoutes Fastify plugin in @boring/agent/server
description:
## Goal
Add `registerAgentRoutes(app, opts?)` Fastify plugin export to `@boring/agent/server`. Mounts agent's `/api/v1/agent/*` routes onto a core-built server. Standalone `createAgentApp` remains unchanged.

## Why it matters
This is the second of agent's two integration shapes (embedded vs standalone). Without it, core apps can't co-locate agent endpoints. With it, standalone CLI is preserved.

## Cross-package type sharing (NOT duck-typing)

For the plugin to access `app.workspaceStore` etc. without runtime importing `@boring/core`, core ships a TYPES-ONLY barrel `@boring/core/shared` that exports interface declarations:
- `WorkspaceStore` interface
- `UserStore` interface
- `AuthProvider` interface
- `User`, `Workspace`, etc. types

Agent's plugin imports these via `import type { WorkspaceStore } from '@boring/core/shared'` ŌĆö type-only imports are erased by tsc, leaving zero runtime dependency. This gives type-safe access to `app.workspaceStore.listMembers(...)` etc. WITHOUT pulling core's runtime code into agent's bundle.

This replaces the earlier "duck typing" approach which was brittle and lost type safety.

## Acceptance criteria
- New export `registerAgentRoutes: FastifyPluginAsync` in `packages/agent/src/server/index.ts`.
- Mounts agent's existing routes at absolute paths (`/api/v1/agent/*`); NO `prefix` option (paths absolute).
- Reads `app.workspaceStore` + `app.userStore` if present (typed via `import type` from `@boring/core/shared`); falls back to agent's own `LocalSessionStore` if not.
- Wires `workspaceId` from URL param `:id` for routes that need it; falls back to configured constant from agent config.
- `createAgentApp(config)` UNCHANGED ŌĆö zero core dependency, zero core import.
- Type-graph check (M4_DEP_GRAPH_CHECK bead) verifies agent has zero RUNTIME imports of `@boring/core`. Type-only imports allowed (erased by tsc).

## Considerations
- The plugin must NOT pull in core's runtime at module level ŌĆö only type-only imports.
- Agent's existing Fastify routes already mount at absolute paths in `createAgentApp`; this bead extracts them into the plugin shape so they're reusable.
- core's `@boring/core/shared` subpath must be runtime-safe (no Node-only imports) for agent's possible browser builds, even though agent currently doesn't ship one.

## Tests
- Embedded test: createCoreApp + register(registerAgentRoutes) ŌåÆ GET /api/v1/agent/catalog returns 200.
- Standalone test: createAgentApp boots without core; agent package has zero runtime imports of @boring/core (verify via M4_DEP_GRAPH_CHECK).
- Type test: agent's plugin code reading `app.workspaceStore.listMembers(...)` typechecks (no `any` slip).

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Dependency position ┬¦Agent's two integration shapes; ┬¦Milestones ┬¦M4.
close_reason: registerAgentRoutes Fastify plugin shipped (8265eb4)

title: Coord: Lock @boring/workspace integration contract
description:
**Goal:** Ensure the contracts the workspace package expects are delivered AND vice versa. Tracks cross-plan alignment. **Integration pattern: app-shell composition, NOT direct workspace imports.**

**Integration pattern (reconciled 2026-04-23):**
- Workspace package has **ZERO imports** from `@boring/agent`.
- App shell (final application) imports components from BOTH `@boring/agent` AND `@boring/workspace`, then wires them via `WorkspaceProvider`'s `panels` prop.
- Avoids circular-dep risk + keeps workspace tree-shakable (chat is opt-in).
- Bridge types (`UiCommand`, `CommandResult`, etc.) live in `@boring/core` ŌĆö agent imports from core, workspace implements from core. Neither package imports from the other.

**Contracts this package owes to workspace (agent APIs workspace consumes):**

1. **HTTP routes** ŌĆö agent hosts these, workspace frontend calls them:
   - Files: `/api/v1/files` (GET/POST/DELETE), `/api/v1/files/move` (POST ŌĆö handles drag + rename), `/api/v1/dirs` (POST ŌĆö mkdir), `/api/v1/tree` (GET), `/api/v1/stat` (GET), `/api/v1/files/search` (GET)
   - Agent: `/api/v1/agent/chat` (POST stream), `/api/v1/agent/chat/:id/:turn` (SSE resume), `/api/v1/agent/sessions` (CRUD minus rename), `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` (GET)
   - UI bridge: `/api/v1/ui/state` (GET/PUT), `/api/v1/ui/state/latest` (GET), `/api/v1/ui/commands` (POST), `/api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE or `?poll=true`)
   - Health: `/health` (liveness), `/ready` (readiness, 503 during cold start)
   - All routes use error codes from `gov.error-codes`.

2. **Component exports ŌĆö for APP SHELL to pass into workspace** (NOT for workspace to import):
   - `ChatPanel` ŌĆö app passes via `WorkspaceProvider panels={[{id:'agent', component: ChatPanel, ŌĆ”}]}`.
   - `SessionToolbar`, `Composer`, `Message`, `NewChatButton` ŌĆö workspace doesn't need these; app shell composes custom chat shells if desired.

3. **Hooks ŌĆö for TIER-3 headless consumers** (rare ŌĆö app shells building custom session UI):
   - `useAgentChat()` ŌĆö Tier-3 custom chat shells.
   - `useSessions()` ŌĆö custom session sidebars beyond our `SessionToolbar`.
   - Workspace v1 package itself does NOT use these hooks.

4. **Styling:** `theme.css` + CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-*`). Workspace can override at any scope ŌĆö per-panel via `data-boring-chat` attribute.

5. **UiBridge semantics:** command-first dispatch (single source = `UiBridge.postCommand`), state-sourced-from-workspace (workspace PUTs state after applying), same `seq` in SSE + display-only `data-ui-command` parts.

6. **CommandResult shape:** `{seq: number, status: 'ok'|'error', error?: {code, message}}`. Matches workspace plan's bridge contract exactly.

7. **Ready-status SSE** (for cold-start UX ŌĆö see `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux`): agent emits on `/ready` or via a dedicated `/api/v1/ready-status` SSE; frontend shows "preparing workspaceŌĆ”" card until ready.

**Contracts workspace owes to us:**

1. `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` with `causedBy: 'user'|'agent'|'restore'` field (prevents echo loops).
2. Subscribe to `/api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE default, `?poll=true` fallback for restricted environments).
3. Consume `data-ui-command` parts as **display-only** ŌĆö never dispatch from these; SSE is the dispatch channel.
4. Pass `panels` config including `{id:'agent', component: ChatPanel}` when chat is desired.
5. Implement the `UiCommand` kinds workspace cares about (`openFile`, `openPanel`, etc.) + extend enum for workspace-specific kinds.

**Verification E2E test (deliverable of this bead):**
- Playwright test that boots BOTH packages in an app-shell scenario.
- App imports `ChatPanel` from agent + passes to `WorkspaceProvider`.
- Test: agent calls `exec_ui({kind:'openFile'})` ŌåÆ SSE delivers ŌåÆ workspace Zustand store opens file ŌåÆ IdeLayout's Editor panel renders it.
- Test: workspace `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` with `causedBy:'user'` ŌåÆ agent tool `get_ui_state` returns that state.
- Test: workspace doesn't import `@boring/agent` at runtime (verify via bundle analyzer ŌĆö workspace dist has no agent exports).

**Cross-plan consistency check (one-shot script `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh`):**
- Every route in agent's HTTP API table appears in workspace plan's usage list (no orphan routes).
- Every workspace-expected route appears in agent's table (no phantom references).
- CommandResult shape identical across both plans.

**Gate:** `docs/WORKSPACE_CONTRACT.md` exists with full contract table + app-shell wiring example. E2E test passes. `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh` passes in CI. Workspace package's bundle analyzer confirms zero agent imports.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/WORKSPACE_CONTRACT.md` has full contract table + app-shell composition example. E2E test proves agentŌåöworkspace integration via app-level injection (not workspace import). `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh` validates both plans stay aligned. Workspace package bundle has zero agent imports (bundle analyzer check). Workspace plan (lines 113, 649) + agent plan (Component exports section) both describe the same app-shell-composition pattern.

title: Phase 2: Data Catalog + Empty Pane
description:

title: Test Bead One
description: Type: task

title: Phase 1: Vendor shadcn UI Components
description:
## Goal
Vendor the 17 shadcn components needed by workspace phases 1-4 into src/components/ui/.

## Background
shadcn components are copy-pasted (vendored), not installed as npm packages. This gives full control over styling and tree-shaking. Workspace vendors its own set; apps can also use @boring/core/ui for shared components (Avatar, Switch, Textarea).

## Components to vendor (17 total across all phases)
### Phase 1 ŌĆö 10 components (vendor now):
- Button, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Sheet, ScrollArea, Input, Badge, Separator, Dialog

### Phase 2 ŌĆö 3 components (vendor now, used by panes):
- Card, Checkbox, AlertDialog

### Phase 4 ŌĆö 4 components (vendor now, used by command palette + polish):
- Command/CommandDialog, Popover, Select, Label

## What to implement
1. Run shadcn CLI or manually copy each component into src/components/ui/
2. Each component is a .tsx file using tailwind classes only
3. All components use the cn() utility from lib/utils.ts
4. Ensure all components respect shadcn CSS variables (--primary, --background, --border, etc.)
5. No custom CSS classes ŌĆö tailwind only
6. Create barrel export: src/components/ui/index.ts exporting all 17 components

## Considerations
- Do NOT re-vendor components available from @boring/core/ui (Avatar, Switch, Textarea)
- All 17 components are needed across phases ŌĆö vendor them all now to avoid repeated setup
- shadcn components are already WCAG AA-compliant (accessibility built-in)
- Tree-shakeable by default (each is a separate file)
- ResizablePanel from shadcn is NOT included ŌĆö dockview handles all panel resizing natively
- The plan's Phase 1.2 text mentions ResizablePanel but Section F (authoritative inventory) excludes it

## Unit tests (vitest)
1. Each component renders without crashing (smoke test per component)
2. Button: renders variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link), sizes (default, sm, lg, icon), disabled state
3. Dialog: opens on trigger, closes on overlay click, closes on Escape, renders title/description, traps focus
4. AlertDialog: renders action/cancel buttons, calls onAction/onCancel callbacks, blocks background interaction
5. DropdownMenu: opens on trigger, renders items, calls onSelect, supports sub-menus, closes on outside click
6. Tabs: switches content on tab click, respects defaultValue, calls onValueChange, keyboard navigation (arrow keys)
7. Command/CommandDialog: filters items on type, selects with Enter, groups render correctly, empty state shown
8. Sheet: opens from correct side (top/right/bottom/left), overlay dismissal, focus trap, ARIA attributes
9. Select: opens dropdown, selects item, shows placeholder, disabled state, keyboard navigation
10. Input: renders value, calls onChange, placeholder shown, disabled/readonly states
11. ScrollArea: renders scrollbar when content overflows, no scrollbar when content fits
12. Tooltip: shows on hover after delay, hides on mouse leave, accessible via aria-describedby
13. Badge: renders variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline)
14. Popover: opens on trigger, positions correctly, closes on outside click
15. Checkbox: toggles checked state, indeterminate state, disabled state, calls onCheckedChange
16. Card: renders header, content, footer slots
17. Separator: renders horizontal/vertical orientation
18. Label: renders with htmlFor association

## Success criteria
- All 17 components importable from src/components/ui/
- Each component renders correctly in light and dark themes
- No external CSS dependencies (tailwind only)
- tsc --noEmit passes
- All unit tests pass
close_reason: done

title: core-M0: testing conventions ŌĆö pino structured logging + vitest setup + fixtures
description:
## Goal
Establish the testing pattern that all subsequent beads' tests follow: pino logger output during tests, structured assertion logging, shared fixture builders, vitest config that surfaces logs only on failure.

## Why it matters
The planning-workflow skill explicitly mandates "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging." Without a shared convention, each bead's implementer invents their own; debugging a CI failure means deciphering 7 different logging styles. One pattern, set in M0, propagates.

## Acceptance criteria
- `packages/core/src/server/__tests__/_setup.ts` configures vitest globals:
  - pino logger writes to a buffer; if test fails, logs flush to vitest output; if test passes, logs swallowed.
  - Each test starts with `pino.info({ test: testName, beadId: 'boring-ui-v2-XXXX' })` at the top of every it() block (via a small `withBeadId(id)` test helper).
  - Standard event names used across tests: `setup.start`, `setup.complete`, `assertion.passed`, `assertion.failed`, `cleanup.start`.
- `packages/core/src/__tests__/_fixtures/` exports:
  - `seedUser({ email?, name? }): Promise<User>` ŌĆö creates user via UserStore, returns full record.
  - `seedWorkspace(ownerId, { name?, appId? }): Promise<Workspace>` ŌĆö creates + makes owner member.
  - `seedMembership(workspaceId, userId, role): Promise<WorkspaceMember>`.
  - `seedInvite(workspaceId, email, role): Promise<{ invite, rawToken }>`.
  - All fixtures clean up via `afterEach` registration.
- `packages/core/vitest.config.ts` extends a shared base config with the setup file + reporters configured to show pino logs on failure.
- A shared `createTestApp(overrides?)` helper boots `createCoreApp` with `CORE_STORES=local`, `MAIL_TRANSPORT_URL=console://`, `manageShutdown: false`, and returns `{ app, store, mailbox }` where `mailbox` captures all sent emails for assertion.
- Frontend test setup at `packages/core/src/front/__tests__/_setup.ts` does the analogous thing for `@testing-library/react` + msw for fetch mocking.

## Considerations
- The `console://` mail transport must support being switched to "capture-to-array" mode in tests. Either via a special `console-capture://` scheme OR a runtime flag on `createMailTransport`. The shared `createTestApp` returns a `mailbox` that observers can `.expect()` against.
- Frontend tests use `msw` (Mock Service Worker) to mock `apiFetch` calls; share an `mswHandlers` array that pulls happy-path responses from the same Zod schemas the backend uses.
- This bead lands in M0 (not M2/M3) because every later test depends on these fixtures + setup.

## Tests
- Self-test: write a deliberately-failing test with one log event; assert vitest output includes the log. Then make the test pass; assert vitest output does NOT include the log.
- Fixture round-trip: seedUser ŌåÆ seedWorkspace ŌåÆ seedMembership ŌåÆ assert all rows present in DB; afterEach ŌåÆ assert all rows gone.
- Mailbox: createTestApp ŌåÆ trigger an email-sending flow ŌåÆ assert mailbox has the rendered email.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Acceptance criteria; AGENTS.md ┬¦Build principles ("Easy to maintain"); planning-workflow skill polish prompt.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: Migration: apps/chat ŌåÆ v2
description:
**Goal:** Port apps/chat to v2 workspace + agent packages. This is the simplest full-stack migration because chat already uses ChatCenteredWorkspace (v1) which maps directly to ChatLayout (v2).

## Current v1 structure (apps/chat/src/front/App.jsx)
- QueryClientProvider + DataContext.Provider wrapping
- createHttpProvider() for file operations
- useChatControllerAdapter() from @boring/agent ŌĆö provides controller, agentMode, thinkingLevel, model selection
- ChatCenteredWorkspace from workspace layouts/chat/
- Props: controller, agentMode, thinkingLevel, setThinkingLevel, selectedModel, setModel, availableModels, renderMessage, onSaveApiKey

## Step-by-step migration checklist

1. **Dependency swap** ŌĆö update apps/chat/package.json:
   - Remove: direct imports from ../../../../packages/workspace/..., ../../../../packages/agent/...
   - Add: @boring/workspace, @boring/agent as workspace deps

2. **App.jsx rewrite** ŌĆö replace entire file:
   ```tsx
   // v2 ŌĆö apps/chat/src/front/App.tsx
   import { WorkspaceProvider, ChatLayout } from "@boring/workspace"
   import { ChatPanel } from "@boring/agent"

   export default function App() {
     return (
       <WorkspaceProvider
         layout="chat"
         panels={[{ id: "chat", component: ChatPanel }]}
         apiBaseUrl="/api/v1"
       >
         <ChatLayout />
       </WorkspaceProvider>
     )
   }
   ```

3. **Remove boilerplate** ŌĆö v2 WorkspaceProvider handles:
   - QueryClientProvider (internal)
   - DataProvider (internal, configured via apiBaseUrl)
   - ThemeProvider (internal)
   - The v1 app manually assembled 3 providers ŌĆö v2 needs 1

4. **Chat controller** ŌĆö useChatControllerAdapter() moves into ChatPanel (agent package owns this). App shell no longer handles controller, model selection, or thinking level.

5. **File extension** ŌĆö rename .jsx ŌåÆ .tsx (v2 is TypeScript)

6. **Vite config** ŌĆö update to use v2 package aliases (pnpm workspace:* resolves automatically)

7. **Test:** `pnpm --filter apps/chat dev` ŌåÆ chat loads, can send messages, artifacts open in surface, session switching works

## Acceptance criteria
- apps/chat boots with v2 packages
- Chat messages flow correctly
- Artifacts open in surface panel
- Session list renders and switches
- Theme toggle works
- App.tsx is <20 lines (all complexity in WorkspaceProvider + ChatLayout)
- No direct ../../../../ package imports remain

## Estimated effort: ~0.5 day
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.
close_reason: apps/chat created with v2 packages: BoringApp + ChatCenteredShell + ChatPanel (agent default stage). App.tsx 17 lines, session store, code-editor surface panel, dev server on 5300/5310/5320 with CORE_STORES=local. Zero v1 imports, vite build verified. Committed 2cd7112.

title: M3a: E2E ŌĆö session CRUD + resume + bridge reliability
description:
**Goal:** M3a gate test. Exercises the load-bearing contracts.

**Scenarios (Playwright + mock HTTP client):**
1. Create session ŌåÆ list ŌåÆ load ŌåÆ delete. Assert CRUD via JSON + reload shows only remaining sessions.
2. Start chat ŌåÆ disconnect after 2s ŌåÆ reconnect with `experimental_resume: true` ŌåÆ receives remaining chunks. Total output matches single-connection baseline.
3. Agent calls `exec_ui({kind:'openFile', params:{path:'foo.md'}})` ŌåÆ workspace SSE subscriber receives command with matching `seq` ŌåÆ workspace state updated. Assert state.openFiles includes 'foo.md'.
4. Workspace `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` ŌåÆ agent tool `get_ui_state` returns that state.
5. Bridge short-poll fallback: subscribe via `?poll=true`, post commands from another client, poll returns batch.

**Logging:** `[m3a-e2e]` tag. Log seq numbers, reconnect cursors, state transitions for CI debuggability.

**Done when:** all 5 scenarios pass in both `direct` and `local` modes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 5 scenarios pass in both direct and local modes (session CRUD, disconnect+resume, bridge dispatch, state roundtrip, poll fallback).
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scenarios pass in both direct and local modes (session CRUD, disconnect+resume, bridge dispatch, state roundtrip, poll fallback).

title: Research: Vercel Sandbox latency on tight fs loops
description:
**Question:** How slow is Vercel Sandbox for agent patterns like "list 100 files" or "grep across 50 files"? Each `exec` has per-call overhead.

**Investigation:**
1. Benchmark: 100-file tree listing via `sandbox.exec('find . -type f')`.
2. Benchmark: 50-file grep via `sandbox.exec('grep -r pattern .')`.
3. Compare vs local bwrap same commands.

**If painful (>10s for common operations):**
- Add sandbox-side `grep` tool that batches reads.
- Cache directory structure in-process.
- Introduce a `find_files` tool over the FileSearch abstraction instead of `bash find`.

**Acceptance:** benchmark numbers documented; mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.

## Acceptance Criteria

Benchmark numbers in docs/PERFORMANCE.md. Mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship; documented p50/p95/p99 in docs/PERFORMANCE.md and opened mitigations boring-ui-v2-jru.1/.2/.3 for >2x slowdowns

title: M1: <ChatPanel /> ŌĆö raw useChat, no ai-elements yet
description:
**Goal:** Minimal chat pane. File: `src/front/components/ChatPanel.tsx`. **v1 shape (bare-bones):** - Message list: maps `messages[]` to basic div rows (user vs assistant). - Renders tool invocations as `
{JSON.stringify(toolCall)}
` (ai-elements polish lands in M3b). - Embeds `` at bottom. **Props:** `{ sessionId: string; bridge?: UiBridge }`. **Exports:** single `ChatPanel` export (pane-friendly, full-height). No separate `AgentPane` wrapper ŌĆö workspace imports `ChatPanel` directly. **Tests:** component renders, types a message, assistant response appears. ## Acceptance Criteria Component renders. Types a message. Assistant response appears. Tool calls render as pre+JSON (raw ŌĆö ai-elements polish in M3b).

Validation: vitest ChatPanel suite (14/14), package typecheck, invariants pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 11f7106.

title: Phase 4: Error Handling + Recovery
description:
## Goal
2-tier error recovery (pane boundary + full reset), network error handling, corrupted state handling.

## What to implement

### Network errors
- HTTP fetch wrapper: auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff for 5xx / network errors
- 401/403: fire onAuthError callback (passed via WorkspaceProvider props)
- Timeout: 10s default, configurable per route, toast on timeout
- Offline detection: navigator.onLine + fetch probe, show banner, disable writes

### Panel crashes (Tier 1)
- React error boundary per pane (src/components/PanelErrorBoundary.tsx)
- Crashed pane shows error + retry button, doesn't kill workspace
- Error logged with panel ID and stack trace
- Bridge notified: bridge.emit('pane:error', { panelId, error })

### Corrupted state (Tier 2)
- Zod validate layout JSON on rehydration
- Invalid layout (missing fields, negative dimensions): reset to defaults + toast
- Corrupted panel references (non-existent component): strip unknown panels, keep valid
- Reset button in UI: user can force-reset layout from workspace menu

### Recovery hierarchy (2-tier)
1. Retry (panel error boundary ŌĆö per-pane)
2. Full reset (corrupted layout ŌåÆ default layout, lose customization)

### Bridge disconnect handling
- Bridge exposes onDisconnect / onReconnect callbacks for app-level handling
- On SSE disconnect: workspace shows subtle "reconnecting..." indicator
- On reconnect: workspace PUTs full state to re-sync (reconciliation per Section D)
- Correlated failure handling (e.g., bridge disconnect + dirty file + network error simultaneously) is DEFERRED to post-launch ŌĆö v2 handles each failure independently, which is sufficient for typical single-failure scenarios

## v1 references
- PanelErrorBoundary.jsx (58 lines) ŌĆö port and style with shadcn

## Unit tests ŌĆö PanelErrorBoundary (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Basic error catching
1. Renders children normally when no error thrown
2. Catches render error in child component, shows error UI
3. Error UI shows "Something went wrong" message with panel ID
4. Error UI shows "Retry" button
5. Clicking "Retry" resets error boundary and re-renders children
6. If child throws again after retry, shows error UI again (no infinite loop)
7. Error boundary catches errors in nested children (not just direct children)
8. Multiple error boundaries are independent ŌĆö one crashing doesn't affect siblings

### Error reporting
9. bridge.emit('pane:error', { panelId, error }) called when error caught
10. Error includes component stack trace
11. console.error called with panel ID and stack

### Scoping
12. Error in pane A does NOT crash pane B (sibling isolation)
13. Error in pane does NOT crash workspace shell
14. Error boundary does NOT catch event handler errors (React limitation ŌĆö document this)

## Unit tests ŌĆö Network error handling (vitest)

### Retry logic
15. 5xx response triggers automatic retry (up to 3 attempts)
16. Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s between retries
17. Successful retry returns data (no error surfaced)
18. All 3 retries fail ŌåÆ error propagated to caller
19. 4xx responses are NOT retried (client errors are permanent)
20. Network errors (fetch throws TypeError) trigger retry
21. AbortController / timeout (10s default) triggers retry

### Auth errors
22. 401 response fires onAuthError callback
23. 403 response fires onAuthError callback
24. onAuthError receives the HTTP response for inspection
25. No retry attempted for 401/403

### Timeout
26. Request exceeding 10s default is aborted
27. Custom timeout per route (e.g., file upload: 30s) is respected
28. Timeout shows toast notification with route context

### Offline handling
29. navigator.onLine=false shows offline banner
30. Write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE) are disabled when offline
31. Read operations continue to attempt (may use stale cache)
32. When navigator.onLine transitions to true, banner dismissed
33. Fetch probe confirms actual connectivity (navigator.onLine can lie)

## Unit tests ŌĆö Corrupted state recovery (vitest)

### Zod validation on rehydration
34. Valid layout JSON passes validation, hydration succeeds
35. Missing required fields (e.g., no groups array) ŌåÆ reset to defaults
36. Negative dimension values ŌåÆ reset to defaults
37. Extra/unknown fields are stripped (Zod passthrough not used)
38. Corrupted JSON string (not parseable) ŌåÆ reset to defaults

### Panel reference validation
39. Panel referencing non-existent component ID ŌåÆ strip that panel, keep valid ones
40. All panels referencing non-existent components ŌåÆ full reset to defaults
41. Valid panels mixed with invalid ŌåÆ only invalid stripped

### Reset button
42. Reset button visible in workspace menu (UserMenu or similar)
43. Clicking reset clears persisted layout from localStorage
44. After reset, workspace re-renders with default layout
45. Toast notification confirms reset happened

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
46. Inject JS error into pane ŌåÆ pane shows error boundary, rest of workspace works
47. Click "Retry" in error boundary ŌåÆ pane recovers
48. Corrupt localStorage layout data ŌåÆ reload ŌåÆ workspace loads with defaults + toast
49. Simulate network offline ŌåÆ banner appears, write buttons disabled ŌåÆ go online ŌåÆ banner dismissed

## Success criteria
- Panel crash doesn't kill workspace
- 401 fires onAuthError callback
- Corrupted localStorage handled gracefully (reset to defaults)
- Network errors show appropriate feedback (toast, banner)
- Reset button works
- Bridge disconnect/reconnect shows indicator and re-syncs
- All 49 tests pass

title: core-M1: LocalUserStore + LocalWorkspaceStore (in-memory, dev seed)
description:
## Goal
Implement in-memory variants of both stores ŌĆö used by tests + the agent CLI's zero-setup mode (`CORE_STORES=local`). Auto-seed `dev@local` with password `dev` when in local mode.

## Why it matters
- Tests run 10├Ś faster against in-memory stores than against Postgres.
- Agent CLI standalone mode boots with zero setup; LocalStores let core-aware code paths still work without a DB.
- Conformance suite (shared between Postgres and Local) ensures no behavioral drift.

## Acceptance criteria
- `LocalUserStore` + `LocalWorkspaceStore` implement the same interfaces as their Postgres counterparts.
- All store conformance tests pass against both.
- LocalUserStore constructor checks `CORE_STORES === 'local'` env; if so, auto-seeds `{ id: 'dev-local', email: 'dev@local', name: 'Dev User', emailVerified: true }` + a default workspace `'My Workspace'` for that user.
- `getWorkspacesWhereSoleOwner` works without raw SQL (in-memory iteration).
- Encryption: the local stores accept the `WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` value but no-op encryption (just JSON.stringify). NEVER ship LocalStores in prod.

## Considerations
- The seed only happens once per process boot. Repeated boots reset state.
- Config validation: `loadConfig` should warn loudly if `CORE_STORES=local` and `NODE_ENV=production`.

## Tests
- Conformance suite covers all interface methods.
- Local mode auto-seed: boot the app with `CORE_STORES=local`; assert `dev@local` exists.
- Race tests: assert local store also exhibits the documented v1 race conditions (invite TOCTOU, etc.) so the conformance contract is honest about the bugs we're carrying.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦DB ┬¦Stores ┬¦Local mode; ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ Dev auto-login dropped.
close_reason: M1 LocalUserStore + LocalWorkspaceStore in-memory implementations shipped. Full store interfaces in types.ts, both implementations, 46 tests (ordering, invite error codes, race fix). Codex REVISEŌåÆfixedŌåÆgreen.

title: core-M2: error handler + HttpError + envelope mapping (Zod, rate-limit, fallback)
description:
## Goal
Single `setErrorHandler` mapping HttpError / Zod errors / rate-limit rejections / unknown errors to the canonical `{error, code, message, requestId}` envelope.

## Acceptance criteria
- HttpError class exported from `@boring/core/shared` with typed `code: ErrorCode`.
- Fastify error handler dispatches:
  - `HttpError` ŌåÆ status `err.status`, body `{error, code, message, requestId}`.
  - Fastify validation/Zod errors ŌåÆ 400, `code: 'validation_failed'`, message = first issue's path + message.
  - `@fastify/rate-limit` rejection ŌåÆ 429, `code: 'rate_limited'`, message includes retry-seconds; `Retry-After` header set.
  - Anything else ŌåÆ 500, `code: 'internal_error'`, log full stack at error level.
- requestId always populated from `request.id`.
- Pino redaction applies to the error log path too (no leaked secrets).

## Tests
- Throw HttpError(403, 'forbidden', '...') ŌåÆ 403 + correct envelope.
- Throw raw Error ŌåÆ 500, internal_error, stack logged.
- Send invalid body to Zod-validated route ŌåÆ 400 validation_failed.
- Hit rate limit ŌåÆ 429 + Retry-After.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦API reference ┬¦Error handling contract.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by codex (gpt-5.3-codex): ship after 1 revision round

title: M1: RuntimeModeAdapter + resolveMode factory
description:
**Goal:** Each execution mode (`direct` | `local` | `vercel-sandbox`) is a self-contained module producing a `RuntimeBundle`. File: `src/server/runtime/modes/{direct,local,vercel-sandbox}.ts` + `src/server/runtime/resolveMode.ts`.

**Contract:**
```ts
export interface RuntimeModeAdapter {
  readonly id: 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox'
  create(ctx: ModeContext): Promise<RuntimeBundle>
}
export interface RuntimeBundle {
  workspace: Workspace
  sandbox: Sandbox
  fileSearch: FileSearch
  uiBridge?: UiBridge                  // pass-through from app-level
}
export interface ModeContext {
  workspaceRoot: string
  sessionId: string
  uiBridge?: UiBridge
}
```

**v1 in-scope modes (this task):** `direct` + `local` only. `vercel-sandbox` lands in M2.

**Auto-detection:**
```ts
function autoDetectMode(): 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox' {
  if (process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE) return process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE as any
  if (process.platform === 'linux' && hasBwrap()) return 'local'
  return 'direct'                      // cross-platform fallback
}
```

**Pairing invariant:** each mode owns BOTH workspace and sandbox construction ŌåÆ impossible to build a mismatched pair. Shared fs substrate guaranteed by construction.

**Tests:**
- `resolveMode('direct')` returns a bundle where `workspace` is a NodeWorkspace and `sandbox` is a DirectSandbox.
- `resolveMode('local')` returns NodeWorkspace + BwrapSandbox. Gracefully errors on non-Linux.
- Writing via `bundle.workspace.writeFile` is visible via `bundle.sandbox.exec('cat ŌĆ”')` ŌĆö proves shared substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria

resolveMode('direct') and resolveMode('local') produce RuntimeBundles where workspace.writeFile + sandbox.exec('cat') see same content. Auto-detection: Linux+bwrapŌåÆlocal, elseŌåÆdirect.
