title: [Plugin] Step 1b: File ops bundle wiring (pi factories + custom additions)
description:
Wire `@boring/agent`'s file-ops tool catalog through pi-tools-migration's bundle factories: `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle)` and `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)`. v7.0 simplification: these are HARNESS substrate, not plugin contributions. No dual-registration with filesystemPlugin.

## Context: this is downstream of pi-tools-migration

`packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md` SHIPS FIRST and produces:
  - `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle)` ŌåÆ `[bash, executeIsolatedCode]`
  - `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)` ŌåÆ `[read, write, edit, find, grep, ls]` (pi factories + Operations adapters)
  - `vercelGrepTool` (custom AgentTool for vercel-sandbox mode)
  - DELETION of all 6 hand-rolled tool files

This bead does NOT extract from hand-rolled tools ŌĆö those are deleted by pi-tools-migration. This bead just ensures the bundle factories exist and are wired into createAgentApp per the migration plan.

## Tool name and schema changes

- find_files ŌåÆ find (pi convention)
- grep_files ŌåÆ grep (pi convention)
- New: ls (pi default; useful affordance)
- Schemas: pi's are richer supersets

## v7.0 separation: tools are harness-level

The earlier 'shared bundle, dual registration' arrangement is gone. v7.0:
  - Substrate file tools always-on via createAgentApp (or opt-out with disableDefaultFileTools: true)
  - filesystemPlugin has NO agentTools field ŌĆö it's UI-only (panels + catalog)
  - createWorkspaceAgentApp wraps createAgentApp without disableDefaultFileTools: true. No coordination dance.

## Custom non-pi additions allowed

buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle) returns pi factory tools PLUS any project-specific filesystem tools we add (watch_files, stat, git-aware ops, multi_edit, ŌĆ”). These are substrate alongside pi's defaults; they live in @boring/agent/server/tools/filesystem/. Plugin authors don't see them ŌĆö they flow through the harness automatically.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle): AgentTool[] exported from @boring/agent/server/tools/harness
- [ ] buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle): AgentTool[] exported from @boring/agent/server/tools/filesystem
- [ ] createAgentApp registers both bundles by default
- [ ] disableDefaultFileTools: true opts out of the filesystem bundle (harness bundle remains)
- [ ] find_files/grep_files references gone (renamed to find/grep)
- [ ] ls tool added (pi default)
- [ ] HTTP routes (/api/v1/files, /tree, /files/search) moved to @boring/workspace/server (substrate path stays)
- [ ] No standalone-agent tool registration goes through any plugin path
- [ ] Bundle factories accept and pass through optional custom AgentTool additions

## Tests

- Unit: buildFilesystemAgentTools(stubBundle) returns 6 pi tools + any registered customs; find (not find_files) appears
- Unit: disableDefaultFileTools: true removes filesystem bundle; harness bundle remains
- Integration: createAgentApp default config exposes read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash/executeIsolatedCode

## Dependencies

- pi-tools-migration Phase 0 + Phase 1 complete (existing boring-ui-v2-uhwx.* bead chain)

## Considerations

- This bead is small if pi-tools-migration is already done. Mostly verification + naming + ensuring bundle factories exist as exported symbols at the expected paths.
- Custom non-pi additions are NOT added in this bead ŌĆö that's per-need future work. The bead just ensures the extension point exists.
- v6.x's createFilesystemAgentTools(deps) name is gone; pi-tools-migration prescribes buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle).
- The standalone agent CLI keeps file tools by default ŌĆö that's the point of v7.0's 'harness owns tools.'

title: [Plugin] Step 6-iii: macro e2e suite (10 specs) ACCEPTANCE GATE
description:
Run all 10 existing boring-macro-v2 e2e specs against the post-migration app. THIS IS THE PHASE 1 ACCEPTANCE GATE ŌĆö if any spec fails, Phase 1 is incomplete; if all pass, the plugin model is shippable.

## The 10 specs (verified live)

Located at /home/ubuntu/projects/boring-macro-v2/e2e/:

1. composer-border.spec.ts ŌĆö empty composer border state
2. deck.spec.ts ŌĆö deck/*.md opens DeckPane (path-aware resolver test)
3. catalog-to-chart.spec.ts ŌĆö click series row ŌåÆ chart panel opens
4. catalog.spec.ts ŌĆö workbench data tab shows series
5. split-no-clip.spec.ts ŌĆö surface split layout doesn't clip
6. layout-persistence.spec.ts ŌĆö workbench layout persists across reloads
7. chat-suggestions.spec.ts ŌĆö empty-state suggestions render and submit
8. chart-tabs.spec.ts ŌĆö chart panel tab interactions
9. topbar.spec.ts ŌĆö top bar elements
10. agent.spec.ts ŌĆö chat ŌåÆ LLM ŌåÆ tool calls round-trip

## Why these are the gate

The specs are BEHAVIOR-LEVEL ŌĆö they don't reference deleted props (`data`, `extraPanels`, `withCommandPalette`) by name. Verified codebase scan: only `apps/boring-macro-v2/src/server/index.ts` and `src/web/App.tsx` reference these props. The specs interact with the rendered UI; if migration preserves behavior, specs pass.

If any spec FAILS post-migration, the regression is real and must be fixed before Phase 1 ships.

## Pre-flight check

Before running, verify:
- macro app builds cleanly (pnpm build from apps/boring-macro-v2/)
- TypeScript: pnpm typecheck ŌĆö must be zero errors
- Lint: pnpm lint ŌĆö must pass

## Run

```bash
cd /home/ubuntu/projects/boring-macro-v2
pnpm exec playwright test
```

Or per-spec:
```bash
pnpm exec playwright test e2e/catalog-to-chart.spec.ts --reporter=line
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] composer-border.spec.ts passes
- [ ] deck.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms path-aware resolver (deck/*.md ŌåÆ DeckPane, not generic MarkdownEditor)
- [ ] catalog-to-chart.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms macroSeriesPanel onActivate ŌåÆ chart-canvas open
- [ ] catalog.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms workbench data tab shows series via macroSeriesPanel left-tab
- [ ] split-no-clip.spec.ts passes
- [ ] layout-persistence.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms dockview layout still persists across reload
- [ ] chat-suggestions.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms chatSuggestions prop still works
- [ ] chart-tabs.spec.ts passes
- [ ] topbar.spec.ts passes
- [ ] agent.spec.ts passes ŌĆö confirms LLM tool round-trip (read/write/edit/find_files via filesystemPlugin)

## If any spec fails

1. Run that spec with --headed --debug to inspect.
2. Diagnose: is it a real regression (behavior changed) or a stale selector (test artifact)? Real regression ŌåÆ fix the implementation.
3. DO NOT modify the spec to make it pass unless the user-visible behavior change is explicitly approved (it shouldn't be ŌĆö Phase 1 promises behavior preservation).

## Dependencies

- ALL preceding beads (B1 through B17) ŌĆö this is the final integration test
- Specifically blocks on: B17 (macro app refactor); transitively blocks on the entire Phase 1 chain

## Considerations

- These specs are part of macro's existing test suite; they were passing before the migration. Failures point at the migration, not the specs.
- Run the full suite, not a subset. Some interactions are subtle (e.g., topbar layout depends on workbench presence).
- If any test infrastructure changed in macro (playwright config, CI), reconcile before running.
- Run twice to detect flakes ŌĆö true regressions reproduce.
- This bead's success is the GO/NO-GO signal for closing the epic (j9p7).

title: [Plugin] Step 7: Release notes for 3 breaking changes
description:
Author release notes for the three breaking changes in @boring/workspace introduced by Phase 1. External hosts (today: only boring-macro-v2; tomorrow possibly others) need a clear migration guide.

## The three breaking changes

### 1. CommandPaletteProps ŌĆö removed fileSearchFn / onOpenFile (B13a)

Old:
```tsx
<CommandPalette
  open={open}
  onOpenChange={setOpen}
  fileSearchFn={(q) => myFileSearch(q)}
  onOpenFile={(path) => mySurface.openFile(path)}
/>
```

New: file search comes from CatalogRegistry. WorkspaceProvider mounts CommandPalette without these props; hosts that custom-mount CommandPalette must contribute a catalog.

Migration: drop the props. If the host has a custom file search function, register it as a CatalogConfig.adapter on a Plugin. If the host had an onOpenFile, move that logic to catalog.onSelect.

### 2. ChatCenteredShellProps ŌĆö removed data / extraPanels / withCommandPalette (B14)

Old:
```tsx
<ChatCenteredShell
  data={dataPaneConfig}
  extraPanels={["chart-canvas", "deck"]}
  withCommandPalette={true}    // also dropped
  chatSuggestions={...}
/>
```

New:
```tsx
<ChatCenteredShell chatSuggestions={...} />
```

Migration:
- `data: DataPaneConfig` ŌåÆ register a left-tab Panel via a Plugin (e.g., custom panel like macro's macroSeriesPanel). For simple cases, use `makeStaticDataPlugin({adapter})` ŌĆö the convenience factory exported from @boring/workspace.
- `extraPanels: string[]` ŌåÆ use `allowedPanels?: string[]` (semantically narrower ŌĆö gates which registered panels appear in this shell). Or drop entirely if unrestricted is fine.
- `withCommandPalette: boolean` ŌåÆ CommandPalette is mounted by WorkspaceProvider; gate at provider level if needed.
- `chatSuggestions: ChatSuggestion[]` ŌåÆ KEPT verbatim. No migration.

### 3. WorkbenchLeftPane ŌĆö internal tab API changed (B15)

WorkbenchLeftPane no longer has hardcoded Files / Data tabs. Tabs are read from PanelRegistry (placement: 'left-tab'). Hosts that imported WorkbenchLeftPane directly (rare; usually mounted inside ChatCenteredShell) need to contribute their tab content via plugins.

Migration: register left-tab panels via plugins. excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] now actually removes the Files tab.

## File for release notes

Where the project keeps release notes (TBD by maintainer): possibly `packages/workspace/CHANGELOG.md` or `docs/releases/0.x.md` ŌĆö coordinate with the project's release convention.

Format suggestion:

```md
## @boring/workspace 0.x.0 (Plugin Model Phase 1)

### Breaking changes

#### `<CommandPalette>`: removed `fileSearchFn` and `onOpenFile` props
...

#### `<ChatCenteredShell>`: removed `data`, `extraPanels`, `withCommandPalette` props
...

#### `<WorkbenchLeftPane>`: tabs are now driven by `PanelRegistry`
...

### New
- `Plugin` contract + `definePlugin()` factory
- Registry-driven workbench tabs (placement: 'left-tab' panels)
- `<WorkspaceProvider plugins={...}>` and `createWorkspaceAgentApp({plugins})`
- `excludeDefaults` for opt-out
- `makeStaticDataPlugin` convenience factory
- Polymorphic Recent (catalogs + commands)
- Path-aware file-pattern resolver
- `@boring/workspace/events` package subpath

### Migration

For each of the three breaking changes above, see the migration block. The boring-macro-v2 migration is the canonical worked example ŌĆö see apps/boring-macro-v2/src/{plugin/, web/App.tsx, server/index.ts} in commit <hash> for the diff.
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Release notes file created (per project convention) with the three breaking changes documented
- [ ] Each breaking change has BEFORE / AFTER code blocks
- [ ] Each breaking change has a migration recipe
- [ ] New features section enumerates: Plugin contract, registry-driven tabs, plugins prop, excludeDefaults, makeStaticDataPlugin, polymorphic Recent, path-aware resolver, events subpath export
- [ ] Migration section points at the boring-macro-v2 commit as the canonical example

## Tests

N/A ŌĆö documentation-only bead. Lint: spell-check + markdown-lint pass.

## Dependencies

- B13a (CommandPalette breaking change details)
- B14 (ChatCenteredShell breaking change details)
- B15 (WorkbenchLeftPane breaking change details)
- B17 (macro migration commit available as canonical example)

## Considerations

- Don't ship release notes BEFORE B17 lands ŌĆö the migration example needs to exist for the link to be valid.
- Coordinate with maintainer on the version bump (probably 0.x ŌåÆ 0.x+1, since these are breaking ŌĆö semver pre-1.0 allows minor bumps for breaking).
- If beads-based code review surfaces additional breaking changes during implementation, append them here.

title: [Plugin] Step 2a: Extract validateTool from pluginLoader.ts to @boring/agent/shared (node-clean)
description:
Extract the validateTool function from packages/agent/src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/pluginLoader.ts into packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts so workspace's client bundle can import it without dragging node:* modules into the browser. P0 fix flagged by codex round-2.

## Why this is a P0

pluginLoader.ts (verified line 1-4) imports node:fs/promises, node:path, node:os, node:url at module scope. The plugin model's validateAgentTool re-uses validateTool ŌĆö but if it imports from pluginLoader.ts, the entire node-only module gets pulled into any client bundle that calls definePlugin (which validates contributions at definition time). That breaks the workspace's client bundle.

## Solution

Move just the validateTool function into a new node-clean module:

```ts
// packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts (NEW)
import type { AgentTool } from "./tool"
export function validateTool(tool: unknown): AgentTool | null {
  if (typeof tool !== 'object' || tool === null) return null
  const t = tool as Record<string, unknown>
  if (typeof t.name !== 'string' || t.name.length === 0) return null
  if (typeof t.description !== 'string') return null
  if (typeof t.parameters !== 'object' || t.parameters === null) return null
  if (typeof t.execute !== 'function') return null
  return t as unknown as AgentTool
}
```

Then update pluginLoader.ts to import the extracted version (function body deleted from pluginLoader; just a re-export or direct usage):

```ts
import { validateTool } from "../../../shared/validateTool"
```

@boring/workspace's plugin/validators.ts then re-exports validateTool as validateAgentTool from @boring/agent/shared (which is browser-safe).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts exists, exports validateTool(tool: unknown): AgentTool | null
- [ ] packages/agent/src/shared/validateTool.ts has NO node:* imports (verify with grep -n "node:" ŌĆö must be empty)
- [ ] pluginLoader.ts imports validateTool from ../../../shared/validateTool ŌĆö does NOT define it inline
- [ ] @boring/agent/shared barrel exports validateTool
- [ ] All existing pluginLoader tests still pass

## Tests

- Unit: import validateTool from a Vite-bundled context (simulated browser) without dragging node:* ŌĆö bundle resolves cleanly
- Unit: existing validateTool semantics unchanged (well-formed AgentTool returns the tool; missing field returns null) ŌĆö port the existing test cases
- Integration: pluginLoaderSmoke test still passes against the new import location

## Why this lands BEFORE Step 2b

B4 (Plugin type + definePlugin + validators) needs to import validateAgentTool. If B3 hasn't landed first, the import either (a) leaks node:* into client OR (b) duplicates the validator implementation. Both unacceptable. Hence B3 is a hard prerequisite for B4.

This is a one-file refactor; should land in 30-60 minutes of focused work.

title: [Plugin] Step 2b: Plugin contract + definePlugin + validators
description:
Define the v6.3 Plugin TypeScript contract ŌĆö six fields, all data ŌĆö plus the definePlugin() factory that validates at definition time. Lays the foundation that all subsequent plugin work builds on.

## The contract (locked in v6.3)

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/shared/plugin.ts
import type { ComponentType } from "react"
import type { AgentTool } from "@boring/agent/shared"
import type { ExplorerAdapter, ExplorerRow } from "../components/DataExplorer/types"
import type { PanelConfig } from "../registry/types"

export interface Plugin {
  /** Stable id. Used for late-wins-on-id, debug provenance. */
  id: string
  /** Human-readable label, defaults to id. */
  label?: string
  panels?: PanelConfig[]
  commands?: CommandConfig[]
  catalogs?: CatalogConfig[]
  agentTools?: AgentTool[]    // server-side only
}

// PanelConfig: existing discriminated union in registry/types.ts.
// Plugin model only ADDS 'left-tab' | 'right-tab' to placement
// + pluginId?: string (auto-set by registry; do not assign manually).
// Existing fields (lazy, requiresCapabilities, essential, chromeless,
// source: 'builtin' | 'app') preserved verbatim.

// CommandConfig: already exists; add pluginId?: string field.

// CatalogConfig: NEW.
export interface CatalogConfig {
  id: string
  label: string
  adapter: ExplorerAdapter
  onSelect: (row: ExplorerRow) => void
  pluginId?: string
}
// NOTE: NO recentKind field (cut in v6.1 ŌĆö was dead code after orphan-drop policy).
```

## The factory

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts
export function definePlugin(spec: Plugin): Plugin {
  validatePlugin(spec)   // throws PluginValidationError on bad input
  return Object.assign({}, spec)   // shallow clone, no Object.freeze (over-engineering)
}
```

## Validators (per v6.1 ┬¦"What definePlugin validates")

1. id: non-empty string
2. panels: each id unique within plugin; placement is one of the allowed enum values; if lazy:true, component is a thunk; if !lazy, component is a ComponentType
3. commands: each id unique within plugin; run is a function
4. catalogs: each id unique within plugin; adapter.search is a function; onSelect is a function
5. agentTools: delegate to validateAgentTool (re-export of @boring/agent/shared/validateTool)

Errors throw immediately with shape:
```
PluginValidationError: plugin "boring-macro": catalogs[0].adapter.search must be a function (got: undefined)
```

## Error model ŌĆö ONE class with kind

```ts
export type PluginErrorKind =
  | 'validation'
  | 'duplicate-id'        // two plugins share Plugin.id (throws at registration)
  | 'mount'               // not used in Phase 1 (no onMount); reserved for Phase 2+
  | 'contribution'        // runtime per-contribution failure (catalog.search throws, etc.)

export class PluginError extends Error {
  constructor(public readonly kind: PluginErrorKind, message: string) { super(message) }
}
```

(v5/v5.2 had 5 classes ŌĆö collapsed to one with a kind field per v6 simplification. Less surface, same diagnostic info.)

## Plugin id collision policy (v6.1)

- TWO plugins sharing Plugin.id ŌåÆ throw PluginError({kind: 'duplicate-id'}) at bootstrap registration. Not a composition pattern; an authoring bug.
- TWO contributions sharing a panel/command/catalog id ACROSS plugins ŌåÆ late-wins-on-id with dev-mode console.warn. THIS IS the composition pattern (host overrides default).

## Files

- packages/workspace/src/shared/plugin.ts ŌĆö Plugin + CatalogConfig types
- packages/workspace/src/registry/types.ts ŌĆö extend existing PanelConfig with 'left-tab'/'right-tab' + pluginId; CommandConfig adds pluginId
- packages/workspace/src/plugin/definePlugin.ts ŌĆö factory + PluginError + validators

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Plugin / PanelConfig / CommandConfig / CatalogConfig all type-check
- [ ] definePlugin({id: 'foo'}) returns a Plugin
- [ ] definePlugin({id: ''}) throws PluginValidationError
- [ ] definePlugin with duplicate panel ids within the SAME plugin throws PluginValidationError
- [ ] definePlugin with malformed agentTool (no execute fn) throws PluginValidationError citing the bad index
- [ ] PanelConfig still supports 'left' | 'center' | 'right' | 'bottom' AND new 'left-tab' | 'right-tab'
- [ ] PanelConfig still supports lazy: true via the discriminated union (no regressions)
- [ ] @boring/workspace barrel re-exports definePlugin and Plugin

## Tests

- Unit: definePlugin happy path returns input
- Unit: each validator branch: bad id, bad panel, bad command, bad catalog, bad agentTool ŌĆö all throw
- Unit: error message contains plugin id + contribution kind + bad field
- Unit: PluginError instanceof Error; .kind set correctly

## Dependencies

- B3 (validateTool extraction) ŌĆö required because validateAgentTool re-exports validateTool from @boring/agent/shared. Without B3 first, this would import a node-leaky module into client.

## Considerations

- Validation runs synchronously at definePlugin call time ŌĆö plugin module fails to import on bad input. This is the right time: dev sees errors at module load, not later at registration.
- Cross-PLUGIN id collisions (panel id from plugin A == panel id from plugin B) are NOT validated here; that's a runtime registration concern handled at bootstrap.
- Do NOT freeze the Plugin object (Object.freeze) ŌĆö considered and rejected in v6.1 as over-engineering.

title: [Plugin] Step 2c: CatalogRegistry (new, subscribe-aware)
[Plugin] Step 2c: CommandRegistry + PanelRegistry subscribe-aware retrofit
[Plugin] Step 2 spine: bootstrap() single-pass orchestrator
[Plugin] Step 2d: Path-aware file-pattern resolver upgrade
description:
Replace the basename-only file-pattern matcher in PanelRegistry.resolve + SurfaceShell.openFile with a path-aware micromatch-based resolver, plus a concrete specificity-scoring formula. Required so domain-specific patterns like 'deck/**/*.md' actually beat generic '**/*.md'.

## Why current resolver fails

Verified at packages/workspace/src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts:91 ŌĆö matchGlob() handles only `*suffix` and exact match. SurfaceShell.tsx:98 strips path to filename via filename.split('/').pop() before lookup. Patterns like 'deck/**/*.md' would be stored but never match because the lookup never sees the full path.

This blocks the canonical compositional example in PLUGIN_MODEL.md: opening 'deck/labor/labor.md' should pick macro's DeckPane (specific) over filesystem's MarkdownEditor (generic).

## Design

### Step 1 ŌĆö accept full paths

SurfaceShell.openFile and resolvePanelForPath pass the FULL path to PanelRegistry.resolve(path). Don't pre-strip to basename.

### Step 2 ŌĆö path-aware micromatch

Replace matchGlob with micromatch:
```ts
import micromatch from "micromatch"
function matches(path: string, pattern: string): boolean {
  return micromatch.isMatch(path, pattern, { matchBase: false, dot: true })
}
```

### Step 3 ŌĆö concrete specificity formula

```ts
function specificity(pattern: string): number {
  const segmentCount = pattern.split('/').filter(Boolean).length
  // Count non-wildcard chars (everything that isn't *, ?, !)
  const nonWildcardChars = pattern.replace(/[*?!]/g, '').length
  return segmentCount * 10 + nonWildcardChars
}
```

Higher score wins. Examples:
  '**/*.md'             ŌåÆ segments=2, non-wildcard=3  ('.md') ŌåÆ 23
  'deck/**/*.md'        ŌåÆ segments=3, non-wildcard=8  ('deck/.md') ŌåÆ 38
  'deck/labor/*.md'     ŌåÆ segments=3, non-wildcard=12 ('deck/labor/.md') ŌåÆ 42

deck/**/*.md beats **/*.md. As intended.

### Step 4 ŌĆö preserve source tie-breaker

Current PanelRegistry.resolve has 'app source beats builtin' on equal-specificity ties. PRESERVE this. Order:
  1. Path-aware micromatch filter
  2. Higher specificity wins
  3. source: 'app' beats source: 'builtin'
  4. Registration order, late wins

### Step 5 ŌĆö late-wins via registration order

Tied on all of the above ŌåÆ return the latest-registered panel.

## Files affected

- packages/workspace/src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts:91 ŌĆö replace matchGlob, add specificity scoring, ordered tiebreakers
- packages/workspace/src/components/chat/SurfaceShell.tsx:98 ŌĆö pass full path to resolve, drop filename.split

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PanelRegistry.resolve(path) takes full path, returns best PanelConfig or undefined
- [ ] micromatch is added as a dep of @boring/workspace (check pnpm-lock)
- [ ] Specificity formula matches the documented one (segments ├Ś 10 + non-wildcard chars)
- [ ] source: 'app' beats source: 'builtin' on equal specificity (preserved behavior)
- [ ] Tie-on-everything ŌåÆ registration order, late wins (preserved)
- [ ] SurfaceShell.openFile passes full path (not basename) to resolve

## Tests (CRITICAL ŌĆö make this bullet-proof since the resolver is foundational)

- Unit: matches('deck/labor/labor.md', 'deck/**/*.md') === true
- Unit: matches('notes.md', '**/*.md') === true
- Unit: specificity('deck/**/*.md') > specificity('**/*.md')
- Unit: register {id: 'markdown', patterns: ['**/*.md']} + {id: 'deck', patterns: ['deck/**/*.md']} ŌåÆ resolve('deck/labor/labor.md') returns 'deck'; resolve('notes.md') returns 'markdown'
- Unit: register {id: 'a', source: 'builtin', patterns: ['**/*.ts']} + {id: 'b', source: 'app', patterns: ['**/*.ts']} ŌåÆ resolve('foo.ts') returns 'b' (app wins on tie)
- Unit: register two with identical specificity + source ŌåÆ later registration wins
- Unit: PanelConfig with no filePatterns is never returned by resolve
- Integration: SurfaceShell.openFile('deck/labor/labor.md') opens DeckPane (full e2e through the workbench)
- Integration: opening a *.foo file with no matching panel returns undefined (paves the way for B12's EmptyFilePanel fallback)

## Dependencies

- None hard. (B6 retrofits PanelRegistry but the matcher upgrade is independent of subscribe semantics.) Can run in parallel with B5/B6/B7.

## Considerations

- micromatch is a substantial dependency (~50KB minified). Verify it's already used elsewhere or budget the add.
- The 'matchBase: false' option is what makes us PATH-aware. Don't accidentally enable matchBase or basename-only behavior creeps back.
- Don't lose the source tie-breaker ŌĆö that's an existing user-meaningful behavior. App-supplied panels win over builtins.
- Consider exposing the resolve algorithm via a pure function for unit testing without instantiating PanelRegistry.

title: [Plugin] Step 3: filesystemPlugin (UI-only ŌĆö panels + catalog, no agentTools)
description:
Implement filesystemPlugin as a v7.0 UI-only Plugin: panels + catalog, NO agentTools. File tools belong to the harness (per pi-tools-migration); the plugin is purely the UI surface.

## v7.0 framing

Earlier drafts had filesystemPlugin via a makeFilesystemPlugin(deps) factory carrying agentTools from a shared bundle. v7.0 drops that entirely:
  - File ops tools (read/write/edit/find/grep/ls/bash/executeIsolatedCode) are HARNESS substrate registered by createAgentApp via pi-tools-migration's bundle factories. ALWAYS-on (opt out with disableDefaultFileTools: true).
  - filesystemPlugin contributes ONLY UI: FileTree (left-tab), CodeEditor + MarkdownEditor (center, with filePatterns), Files catalog (cmd palette).
  - No factory; plain module-scope const. No runtime bundle dependency.

## Implementation

```ts
// packages/workspace/src/plugin/defaults/filesystemPlugin.ts
import { definePlugin } from '../definePlugin'
import { FileTreePanel, CodeEditorPanel, MarkdownEditorPanel } from '../../panels'
import { filesCatalog } from './filesystemCatalog'

export const filesystemPlugin = definePlugin({
  id: 'filesystem',
  label: 'Filesystem',
  panels: [
    { id: 'files', title: 'Files', component: FileTreePanel, placement: 'left-tab', source: 'builtin' },
    { id: 'code-editor', title: 'Code', component: CodeEditorPanel, placement: 'center',
      filePatterns: ['**/*.ts','**/*.tsx','**/*.js','**/*.jsx','**/*.py','**/*.rs','**/*.go','**/*.json','**/*.yml','**/*.yaml'],
      source: 'builtin' },
    { id: 'markdown-editor', title: 'Markdown', component: MarkdownEditorPanel, placement: 'center',
      filePatterns: ['**/*.md', '**/*.mdx'], source: 'builtin' },
  ],
  catalogs: [filesCatalog],
  // NO agentTools ŌĆö file tools live with the harness
})
```

## filesCatalog adapter

Wires /api/v1/files/search to a CatalogConfig.adapter (cmd palette search).

## Excludes ONLY UI

excludeDefaults: ['filesystem'] removes:
- Files left-tab from WorkbenchLeftPane
- code-editor / markdown-editor panel registrations (file types fall through to EmptyFilePanel per B12)
- Files catalog from the cmd palette

It does NOT remove:
- LLM file tools (still in @boring/agent's harness substrate)
- /api/v1/files/search route (substrate)
- Any harness-level capability

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] filesystemPlugin exported as a plain const (NOT a factory)
- [ ] No runtime bundle dependency (no deps argument)
- [ ] Plugin shape: id='filesystem', panels (3), catalogs (1), agentTools=undefined
- [ ] Files panel uses 'builtin' source
- [ ] excludeDefaults removes UI; LLM file tools remain

## Tests

- Unit: filesystemPlugin shape (panel ids, catalog id, no agentTools)
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider plugins={[]}> shows Files left-tab
- Integration: <WorkspaceProvider excludeDefaults={['filesystem']}> hides Files left-tab
- Integration: opening 'foo.ts' opens code-editor when default mounted; opens EmptyFilePanel when excluded
- Integration (server): createWorkspaceAgentApp registers harness file tools; cmd palette catalog hits /api/v1/files/search

## Dependencies

- B4 (Plugin contract + definePlugin)
- B5 (CatalogRegistry ŌĆö fan target)
- B6 (PanelRegistry retrofit ŌĆö fan target)
- pi-tools-migration's bundle factories must exist for filesystem agent tools to flow through createAgentApp

## Considerations

- WAY simpler than v6.x's factory shape. Plugin is a static const; module-load is instant.
- Files catalog and Files panel both have id 'files' but in different registries ŌĆö no collision.
- /api/v1/files/search route is SUBSTRATE (always-on), not part of the plugin.
- Component imports (FileTreePanel etc.) come from existing workspace primitives.

title: core-M2: routes ŌĆö workspace invites (create with email send + raw token, list, revoke, accept)
description:
## Goal
Invite endpoints. createInvite gets the raw token from store + sends WorkspaceInvite email with the accept URL. acceptInvite throws 4 specific HttpError codes.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites`: requireWorkspaceMember(); list invites for workspace.
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); body `{ email, role }`; calls `WorkspaceStore.createInvite` ŌåÆ gets `{ invite, rawToken }`. Renders WorkspaceInvite email with `acceptUrl: ${BETTER_AUTH_URL}/api/v1/workspaces/${invite.workspaceId}/invites/${invite.id}/accept?invite_token=${rawToken}`. Sends via mail transport. Response: `{ invite }` (does NOT include rawToken ŌĆö it's already in the email).
  - If mail not configured (no MAIL_FROM): returns `{ invite, warning: 'mail_disabled' }`.
- `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites/:inviteId/accept`: auth-only (NOT requireWorkspaceMember ŌĆö accepter isn't a member yet); reads `?invite_token` query, validates against stored hash, calls `WorkspaceStore.acceptInvite`. On 4 error codes (HttpError thrown by acceptInvite) ŌåÆ 404/410/409/403 with code. Success: 200 `{ workspaceId, role }`.
- `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id/invites/:inviteId`: requireWorkspaceMember('owner'); calls revokeInvite.

## Considerations
- The raw token NEVER persists outside the email body. Server logs MUST NOT include it (redaction list covers `token`).
- `expiresAt` defaults to 7 days from server time at creation.

## Tests
- Owner creates invite ŌåÆ email sent (mock transport asserts), invite row exists, raw token NOT in DB.
- Non-owner POST invites ŌåÆ 403.
- Accept with valid token ŌåÆ user joins workspace.
- Accept with expired token ŌåÆ 410 invite_expired.
- Accept twice ŌåÆ second call 409 invite_already_accepted.
- Accept with wrong-email user ŌåÆ 403 invite_email_mismatch.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦API reference ┬¦HTTP surface; ┬¦DB ┬¦createInvite, acceptInvite.
close_reason: done

title: M2 ŌĆö Remote mode (Vercel Sandbox)
description:
**~1 week.** Prove the core architectural claim: swapping adapters flips from local to remote with zero changes elsewhere.

**Deliverables:**
- `createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox)` ŌĆö 30 LOC adapter against `sandbox.fs.*` + `sandbox.writeFiles` + path validation.
- `createVercelSandboxExec(sandbox)` ŌĆö 30 LOC adapter wrapping `sandbox.runCommand`.
- `SandboxHandleStore` interface + `FileHandleStore` default impl at `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Cloud package injects DB-backed impl. ~60 LOC.
- `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` ŌĆö create-or-get-or-resume-from-snapshot + in-process cache, persists via SandboxHandleStore.
- `vercel-sandbox` `RuntimeModeAdapter` ŌĆö workspace + sandbox share a single Sandbox handle.
- Snapshot-bake flow (src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/bake.ts): creates seed sandbox + pip install + snapshot + caches snapshotId. One-time per `python_packages` hash. ~150 LOC.
- Periodic snapshot cron (every 10 min or on-idle).
- Circuit breaker around Vercel SDK (exponential backoff, fast-fail). ~30 LOC.
- Snapshot retention policy (keep-last-2). ~15 LOC.
- Config: `BORING_AGENT_MODE`, `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_TEAM_ID`.

**Gate:** `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` ŌĆö same chat UI, same tools, files now living in a Firecracker VM. No other code changes. Switching modes mid-development is an env-var flip.

## Success Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip ŌĆö no code changes.
acceptance_criteria: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip ŌĆö no code changes.
close_reason: M2 Remote Mode complete ŌĆö all children shipped: jru.1 (find_files, f318c84), jru.2 (grep_files, b5898ff), jru.3 (tree/stat cache, 04db555). Plus n1o (mode-flip E2E be82e7d), 082 (tarball e640081), 4vl (pricing 790e5af), 96h (cold-start fcc7f65), f4f (fs latency baca373), xzr (leak monitor b5898ff).

title: M2: Add find_files tool over FileSearch (mitigate vercel fs latency)
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec find(100 files) p50 ~297ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x). Implement a first-class find_files tool that uses FileSearch abstraction instead of shelling out to find. Acceptance: chat can enumerate matching files without invoking bash find; docs note this as preferred path for vercel-sandbox mode.
close_reason: shipped in 1cb50cb; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added find_files tool over FileSearch + catalog wiring + tests + vercel-sandbox docs note

title: M2: Add grep_files batched tool to avoid per-file read loops
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec grep(50 files) p50 ~292ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x), and per-file read/stat ops are 180-1000x slower than local node fs. Implement grep_files tool that executes one batched search in runtime adapter (prefer ripgrep where available) and returns structured matches. Acceptance: tool can search 50+ files with one invocation and includes file/path/line metadata for UI rendering.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö grep_files batched tool: sandbox.exec() with rg||grep fallback, structured match output, 14 unit tests passing

title: M2: Add short-lived tree/stat cache for vercel-sandbox workspace
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-fs stat p50 ~299ms vs local node stat ~0.3ms (~996x), readFile/writeFile/mkdir also 200x+ slower. Add bounded in-process cache for readdir/stat results in vercel-sandbox workspace adapter with invalidation on write/rename/unlink/mkdir. Acceptance: repeated stat/readdir in same turn avoid redundant remote calls; cache invalidation correctness covered by unit tests.
close_reason: shipped in 04db555; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added short-lived stat/readdir cache invalidation hook for sandbox.exec + cache correctness tests

description:

title: core-M2: safeRedirect util + integration with better-auth callback paths
description:
## Goal
`safeRedirect(url: string, config: CoreConfig): string` ŌĆö strips CRLF/HTML chars, accepts relative paths, accepts absolute URLs only if origin is in `config.cors.origins`, falls back to `/`.

## Why it matters
Open-redirect + CRLF injection are real vectors for OAuth callback abuse. better-auth doesn't enforce; core wraps every redirect emission point.

## Acceptance criteria
- `safeRedirect(url, config): string` exported from `@boring/core/server`.
- Rejection rules:
  - Contains `[\0\r\n<>"'` + backtick `]` ŌåÆ fallback `/`.
  - Absolute URL with origin NOT in `config.cors.origins` ŌåÆ fallback `/`.
- Acceptance:
  - Relative paths starting with `/` (no scheme).
  - Absolute URLs with origin in allowlist.
- Wired into better-auth callback handlers (any redirect URL passed to `reply.redirect()` runs through this).
- Wired into invite-accept page redirect.

## Considerations
- Trailing-slash handling: `https://app.com` and `https://app.com/` both accepted if origin matches.
- Magic-link `next` query param explicitly handled.

## Tests
- Unit: 12+ cases covering CRLF chars, relative, absolute-allowed, absolute-disallowed, empty string, javascript: URL, data: URL.
- Integration: GitHub OAuth callback with `state` containing a malicious next URL ŌåÆ user redirected to `/`, not the malicious target.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Auth ┬¦Security additions ┬¦Redirect URI sanitization.
close_reason: shipped in scaffold commit 96346a0 ŌĆö safeRedirect util + 24 unit tests covering CRLF, open-redirect, scheme injection. Integration wiring deferred to M2 better-auth bead. Reviewed by codex.

title: core-M4: dependency-graph CI check (agent has zero runtime import of core)
description:
## Goal
CI step that verifies `@boring/agent` has zero RUNTIME imports of `@boring/core`. Type-only imports are allowed (erased by tsc); runtime imports are forbidden.

## Why it matters
The standalone-CLI promise relies on agent being core-free. A regression where someone imports a core symbol (even accidentally) would break `npx @boring/agent` for self-hosters.

## Why not `tsc --listFiles`

`tsc --listFiles` lists files visited by the TS compiler, including type-only files ŌĆö false positives. We need a check on the EMITTED JS, where only runtime imports remain.

## Acceptance criteria
- Script `scripts/check-agent-isolation.ts`:
  1. Run `pnpm --dir packages/agent build` to produce `dist/`.
  2. Static-walk every emitted `.js` and `.mjs` file under `packages/agent/dist/` (use `es-module-lexer` or `acorn` to parse).
  3. Collect every `import` / `require` specifier ŌĆö whether bare module spec or relative path that escapes via node resolution.
  4. Filter for any specifier matching `^@boring/core(/|$)`.
  5. Assert the filtered list is empty.
- Wired into the CI workflow as a separate step running AFTER the agent build job.
- Fails CI with a clear error message naming the offending source file (resolved from the dist sourcemap).

## Considerations
- Type-only imports MUST be stripped by tsup/tsc ŌĆö verify build config has `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` or equivalent so `import type` is genuinely erased.
- `package.json`'s `"sideEffects"` field in agent should NOT include any core-imported file.
- Dynamic imports (`await import('@boring/core/...')`) are also caught by the parser walk.

## Tests
- **Self-test fail**: temporarily add `import { createCoreApp } from '@boring/core/server'` to a non-test file in agent ŌåÆ run isolation check ŌåÆ assert exits non-zero with the file path in the error.
- **Self-test pass**: remove the import ŌåÆ re-run ŌåÆ exits zero.
- **Type-only allowed**: add `import type { WorkspaceStore } from '@boring/core/shared'` to agent's plugin ŌåÆ run check ŌåÆ exits zero (type-only erased from JS output).
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-ka07'`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Dependency position; ┬¦Milestones ┬¦M4; M4_REGISTER_AGENT bead (cross-package type sharing).
close_reason: scripts/check-agent-isolation.ts walks all .js/.mjs in agent dist, extracts import specifiers via regex, asserts zero @boring/core matches. Wired into invariants.yml post-build step. Self-tested with injected import ŌĆö caught at file:line. Commit dddedc4.

title: M1: buildBwrapArgs (port ŌĆö security-critical)
description:
**Goal:** Port the bwrap argument builder. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/buildBwrapArgs.ts`.

**Source:** Old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/adapters/bwrap.ts` lines ~22ŌĆō58.

**SECURITY REVIEW REQUIRED:** This bead MUST be reviewed by a second agent or human before merge. It defines the sandbox boundary ŌĆö subtle flag errors create sandbox escapes.

**Behavior:** Given `{workspaceRoot}`, returns `string[]` of `bwrap` flags creating a sandbox where:
- `/workspace` ŌåÆ bind-mounted from `workspaceRoot` (writable)
- Read-only binds: `/usr`, `/lib`, `/lib64`, `/bin`, `/etc/ssl`, `/etc/ca-certificates`, etc. (see `RO_BIND_DIRS`)
- `--unshare-all --share-net` (proc/user/ipc/uts isolated; keep network)
- `--die-with-parent` ŌĆö so if our Node process dies, sandbox terminates.
- `--chdir /workspace`
- `--setenv HOME /workspace`
- `--proc /proc --dev /dev --tmpfs /tmp`

**Why flag order matters:** bwrap applies binds in order. Read-only binds must come BEFORE `--chdir`. Port verbatim where possible.

**Constants:**
- `BWRAP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30`
- `KILL_GRACE_SECONDS = 5`
- `RO_BIND_DIRS` ŌĆö list of host paths to bind read-only.

## Security invariants
- `--unshare-all` MUST always be present (no exception, no config to disable)
- `--die-with-parent` MUST always be present
- workspaceRoot bind is writable; ALL other binds are read-only
- No user-controlled string appears unescaped in the args array
- workspaceRoot is validated before reaching this function (realpath resolved, no symlink chasing outside allowed root)

## Unit tests (vitest)

### Snapshot + structure
1. Snapshot test: args array for known workspaceRoot matches expected output
2. `--unshare-all` present in every generated args array
3. `--share-net` present (network allowed)
4. `--die-with-parent` present
5. `--bind workspaceRoot /workspace` present exactly once
6. All RO_BIND_DIRS have `--ro-bind` entries
7. RO binds appear before `--chdir` in the array (ordering invariant)
8. `--chdir /workspace` present
9. `--setenv HOME /workspace` present

### Adversarial inputs (security-critical)
10. workspaceRoot with spaces: `/home/user/my project` ŌĆö args correctly quoted/separated
11. workspaceRoot with shell metacharacters: `/tmp/$(whoami)` ŌĆö treated as literal path, no shell expansion
12. workspaceRoot with newlines: `/tmp/foo\nbar` ŌĆö rejected or escaped
13. workspaceRoot with null bytes: `/tmp/foo\x00bar` ŌĆö rejected
14. workspaceRoot as symlink: should be pre-validated by caller (test that buildBwrapArgs does NOT resolve symlinks ŌĆö that is tx7 responsibility)
15. workspaceRoot that does not exist: function still builds args (existence check is caller responsibility), but assert no crash
16. Empty workspaceRoot string: throws or returns error (not silent empty bind)
17. Very long workspaceRoot (>4096 chars): rejected

### Negative tests
18. Removing `--unshare-all` from RO_BIND_DIRS does NOT affect its presence (it is hardcoded, not derived)
19. No `--bind-try` in output (we want hard failures, not silent skips)

## Acceptance criteria
- Snapshot test for args array
- Required flags present (`--unshare-all`, `--die-with-parent`, `--share-net`)
- `--bind` for workspaceRoot present exactly once
- RO binds precede `--chdir`
- All 19 adversarial + structural tests pass
- Security review completed before merge
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot test for args array; required flags present; --bind for workspaceRoot present exactly once; RO binds precede --chdir.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship; commit a98c004; targeted test: pnpm exec vitest run src/server/sandbox/bwrap/__tests__/buildBwrapArgs.test.ts

title: core-M1: schema ŌĆö workspaces table + soft-delete + default-per-user index
description:
## Goal
Define the `workspaces` table matching v1 column-for-column, with the partial unique index that enforces one default workspace per (user, app).

## Why it matters
Workspaces are the central tenancy unit. The default-per-user partial unique index was a v1 footgun (concurrent creates can hit unique violation); we preserve it for v2 with a documented retry-on-violation in createWorkspace.

## Acceptance criteria
- `workspaces` table per CORE.md ┬¦DB Schema overview "Core-owned":
  - `id` uuid primary key
  - `appId` text NOT NULL
  - `name` text NOT NULL (1-100 chars validated at app layer, not DB)
  - `createdBy` text references `users.id`
  - `createdAt` timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
  - `deletedAt` timestamp NULL (soft-delete marker)
  - `isDefault` boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false
  - `machineId` / `volumeId` / `flyRegion` text NULL (legacy v1 fields preserved for agent provisioning)
- Partial unique index `idx_workspaces_default_per_user_app` ON `(createdBy, appId) WHERE isDefault = true` (matches v1 schema.ts:67).
- B-tree index on `(createdBy)` for `WorkspaceStore.list` lookups.

## Considerations
- v1 has a known race on default-workspace creation under unique violation ŌĆö documented as carried-forward known issue (CORE.md ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ Default-workspace promotion). Don't try to fix in this bead; just preserve the index.
- `createdBy` FK has NO cascade ŌĆö see core-M1-schema-workspace-members bead for the user-deletion cascade policy. Workspaces created by a deleted user are NOT auto-deleted.

## Tests
- Integration: insert a default workspace; insert a second default for same (user, app); assert unique violation.
- Insert a default for (userA, appX) AND (userA, appY); assert both succeed (cross-app isolation).

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦DB Schema; v1 schema.ts:42-78; ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ Accepted as known issues.
close_reason: already landed in commit b722851

title: Gov: Single source of truth for env + config schema
description:
**Goal:** One canonical Zod schema for all env vars + config keys. File: `src/shared/config-schema.ts`.

**Contents:**
```ts
export const ConfigSchema = z.object({
  // Runtime
  BORING_AGENT_MODE: z.enum(['direct','local','vercel-sandbox']).optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID: z.string().default('default'),
  BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_AUTH_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),

  // Providers
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: z.string(),

  // Vercel mode
  VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),
  VERCEL_TEAM_ID: z.string().optional(),

  // TOML-sourced (runtime/sandbox sections)
  runtime: z.object({ mode: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  workspace: z.object({ template_path: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  sandbox: z.object({
    python_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    system_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    vercel: z.object({
      runtime: z.enum(['node24','node22','python3.13']).optional(),
      snapshot_id: z.string().optional(),
    }).optional(),
  }).optional(),
})
```

**Precedence rules:**
1. CLI flag
2. Env var
3. `boring.app.toml` / `.boring-agent/config.toml`
4. Default

**Documented in:** `docs/CONFIG.md` with full table.

**Acceptance:** all env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`; invalid configs fail fast with clear errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

All env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`. Invalid configs fail fast. `docs/CONFIG.md` has full table.

title: M1: createNodeWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** Node fs-backed `Workspace` adapter. File: `src/server/workspace/createNodeWorkspace.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createNodeWorkspace(root: string): Workspace {
  return {
    root,
    async readFile(rel) { const p = validatePath(root, rel); await assertRealPath...(root,p); return fs.readFile(p,'utf8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... ensureWritable ... fs.writeFile(p, data) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... fs.rm(p, { recursive: false }) },   // file OR empty dir
    async readdir(rel) { ... fs.readdir(p, { withFileTypes: true }) ŌåÆ Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... fs.stat(p) ŌåÆ Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { validate both; fs.rename(abs(from), abs(to)) },
  }
}
```

**Uses:** path helpers on every call (no exceptions).

**Shared by:** `direct` and `local` modes (both use same host fs).

**Tests:**
- Happy path for all 7 ops.
- Path traversal rejections on every op.
- `rename` within root (move file, rename file, rename dir).
- `unlink` on empty dir succeeds; on non-empty dir rejects.
- `readdir` returns `{name, kind}` ŌĆö no extra fields.

**Done when:** Passes shared `Workspace` conformance suite (in infra epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.

title: core-M2: mail transport layer (resend default, smtp via nodemailer, console fallback)
description:
## Goal
`createMailTransport(url): MailTransport` dispatches by URL scheme. Three implementations: resend (default REST), smtp (nodemailer), console (dev-only).

## Why it matters
Email delivery underpins verification, reset, magic-link, and invites. Resend is the recommended default; SMTP is the escape hatch for self-hosters; console is for dev/test.

## Acceptance criteria
- `MailTransport` interface: `{ send(email: RenderedEmail): Promise<{ id: string }> }`.
- `RenderedEmail = { to: string; subject: string; html: string; text: string }`.
- `createMailTransport(url: string, env: 'production' | 'development' | 'test'): MailTransport`:
  - **`resend://<api-key>` (default recommended)** ŌĆö direct `fetch POST https://api.resend.com/emails` with Bearer auth. NO npm dep on `resend`.
  - **`smtp://`, `smtps://`** ŌĆö nodemailer SMTP transport.
  - **`console://`** ŌĆö logs at INFO level. **Logging policy pinned by env:**
    - `env === 'development'`: log full body `{to, subject, html, text}` (so devs can read template output).
    - `env === 'test'`: log full body AND write to `/tmp/test-mail-<pid>.log` for assertion harnesses.
    - `env === 'production'`: REFUSED ŌĆö `console://` rejected at boot via ConfigValidationError ("console mail transport is not allowed in production").
  - **`console-capture://` (test-only)** ŌĆö same as console:// in test mode but writes JSON-line to a configurable path; M7_E2E uses this. Refused outside test env.
  - Unknown scheme ŌåÆ throws `ConfigValidationError` at boot.
- Each impl handles errors: 4xx / 5xx from Resend ŌåÆ throws `MailDeliveryError`; SMTP timeouts ŌåÆ throws same.
- Retry logic: 3 retries with exponential backoff (500ms, 1s, 2s) for transient failures (5xx, network errors). 4xx errors do NOT retry (e.g. invalid recipient).

## Considerations
- For Resend: `from: config.auth.mail.from` is required. Resend rejects sends without a verified domain.
- The test-mode mailbox observer in `createTestApp` (M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS) is the canonical way to capture emails in tests.
- DO NOT log email body in production logs even via redaction ŌĆö entire body NEVER goes to pino in production mode.

## Tests
- Each transport dispatches correctly given its URL scheme.
- Mock Resend API ŌåÆ `transport.send` returns the message id.
- Bad scheme ŌåÆ throws.
- Retry on 503 ŌåÆ succeeds on second attempt.
- 4xx ŌåÆ no retry, throws immediately.
- `console://` in production env ŌåÆ throws ConfigValidationError at boot.
- `console-capture://` in non-test env ŌåÆ throws.
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-ma63'`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Auth ┬¦Email sending ┬¦Transport.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.3): 4 issues fixed, re-verified, ship

title: Phase 4: Playwright E2E Tests
description:
## Goal
Golden-path E2E tests + bridge protocol E2E test. ~2 min CI.

## What to implement

### Golden paths (Playwright, workspace-playground app)

#### File workflow
1. Click file in FileTree ŌåÆ editor tab opens with correct content
2. Edit file in CodeMirror ŌåÆ dirty indicator (dot) appears on tab
3. Cmd+S ŌåÆ file saved (dirty indicator disappears)
4. Close tab ŌåÆ reopen same file ŌåÆ content matches saved version

#### Layout persistence
5. Drag sidebar wider ŌåÆ reload page ŌåÆ sidebar width restored
6. Open 3 tabs ŌåÆ close middle tab ŌåÆ reload ŌåÆ 2 tabs restored in correct order
7. Collapse sidebar ŌåÆ reload ŌåÆ sidebar still collapsed

#### Panel lifecycle
8. Open file ŌåÆ panel registers in tab bar
9. Close panel via X button ŌåÆ tab removed, panel unmounted
10. Open same file again ŌåÆ panel re-created (not cached)

#### Chat layout
11. Switch chat session ŌåÆ messages update
12. Open artifact from chat ŌåÆ artifact panel opens adjacent

#### Markdown workflow
13. Open .md file ŌåÆ tiptap editor renders rich content
14. Toggle bold ŌåÆ text formatted ŌåÆ save ŌåÆ reload ŌåÆ formatting preserved

### Bridge protocol E2E (~50 LOC, critical)
Proves the full command-based bridge flow end-to-end:

```
Test flow:
1. Playwright opens workspace-playground at localhost
2. Separate fetch() client simulates agent:
   - POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "openFile", params: { path: "/src/main.ts" } }
3. Playwright asserts: new editor tab appears in workspace within 2s
4. Agent client: POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "showNotification", params: { msg: "Hello", level: "info" } }
5. Playwright asserts: toast notification appears
6. Playwright: user closes file tab
7. Agent client: GET /api/v1/ui/state ŌåÆ verify openPanels no longer includes main.ts
8. Playwright: SSE event stream test ŌĆö open EventSource, verify events arrive with v:1 protocol version
```

This test validates:
- Command POST ŌåÆ SSE delivery ŌåÆ workspace execution
- Workspace state ŌåÆ PUT to server ŌåÆ agent reads via GET
- Protocol version field present
- End-to-end latency <2s

### CI integration
- Run on every PR (~2 min target)
- Playwright config: 1 worker (sequential, avoids port conflicts)
- Full suite (chromium + firefox + webkit) on merge to main only
- Retry: 1 retry per test (flake protection)

## Implementation files
- tests/e2e/golden-path.spec.ts ŌĆö scenarios 1-14
- tests/e2e/bridge-protocol.spec.ts ŌĆö scenario 15 (bridge E2E)
- tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts ŌĆö workspace-playground server setup

## Success criteria
- All 15 E2E scenarios pass
- Bridge E2E proves command flow works end-to-end
- No flaky tests (0 flakes over 10 consecutive runs)
- <2 min total runtime on CI
- Test output includes detailed step logging for debugging failures

title: core-M1: integration tests for accepted-bug races (TOCTOU, last-owner, default-promotion)
description:
## Goal
Write integration tests that DOCUMENT the three accepted v1 race conditions as failing (or `.fails()`-marked) tests, with clear comments referencing the v1.1 fix bead. Use a deterministic concurrency harness so failures reproduce reliably in CI.

## Why it matters
Silent known-issues rot. Explicit failing-tests-as-documentation make the bug visible to anyone running the suite. When v1.1 fixes the races, the implementer flips `.fails()` off and the tests pass. Without a deterministic harness, races are flaky in CI ŌĆö sometimes triggering, sometimes not ŌĆö which is worse than no test.

## Acceptance criteria
- Three test files (one per race), each marked `test.fails()` (vitest's "expected to fail").
- **Deterministic concurrency harness** `__tests__/_concurrency.ts`:
  - Provides `runConcurrent<T>(fns: Array<() => Promise<T>>, opts: { barrier: number }): Promise<T[]>`.
  - Uses `Promise.allSettled` + an explicit barrier (all functions wait for the barrier to release before proceeding to the racy operation).
  - Optional `instrument` hook that wraps Drizzle's `transaction()` to inject sleeps at chosen points so the race window is FORCED open in tests (the test harness reaches into Drizzle internals only in test mode).
- **Files**:
  - `inviteAcceptToctou.test.ts` ŌĆö 2 concurrent acceptInvite calls on same invite; both succeed (the race we DON'T fix in v1).
  - `lastOwnerRemoveRace.test.ts` ŌĆö 2 concurrent removeMember on two owners; both can succeed, leaving 0 owners.
  - `defaultWorkspacePromotion.test.ts` ŌĆö soft-delete the default workspace; assert no auto-promotion happens.
- Each test has a comment block linking to the bead ID for the v1.1 fix and to the ┬¦Traps from v1 section in CORE.md.
- Comment includes mitigation note for ops (e.g. "if a workspace ends up with 0 owners, run admin SQL ...").

## Considerations
- `test.fails()` makes the suite pass when the race triggers. If the race accidentally doesn't trigger (e.g. faster CI), the test fails with "expected race did not occur" ŌĆö instrument hook prevents this.
- When v1.1 fixes the races, this bead's tests are flipped to `expect`-style (no `.fails`).

## Tests
- Self-referential: the suite passes when these tests "fail in the documented way."
- Run the suite 100├Ś in a CI loop; assert race triggers each time (no flakiness).
- All test events logged with pino tagged `bead: 'boring-ui-v2-mo58'`. Each race test logs:
  - `race.test.start { race, beadId, v1.1FixBead: '...' }`
  - `race.barrier.released { participants: 2 }`
  - `race.test.outcome { race, observed: 'both_succeeded' | 'one_failed' | 'unexpected', mitigation: '...' }`
- Failure logs explicitly state the operator-recovery SQL.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ Accepted as known issues; M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS bead.
close_reason: shipped in f6dec1c: race-safe invite/member/account-delete semantics + Postgres integration tests
