title: Research: Plugin tool name collision precedence
description:
**Question:** When a pi plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, who wins?

**Investigation:**
1. Write a synthetic plugin that registers a tool named `bash` with behavior "return 'FROM PLUGIN'".
2. Load standard catalog + plugin ŌåÆ invoke `bash` via chat ŌåÆ observe output.
3. Vary registration order: ours-then-plugin vs plugin-then-ours.
4. Try pi's namespace mechanisms if any.

**Document:** exact precedence rule in `docs/PLUGINS.md`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins (plugins after defaults = plugin wins).

**Future:** namespace defaults as `boring.bash` ŌĆö deferred unless collision reports pile up.

**Acceptance:** rule documented with reproducible test.

## Acceptance Criteria

Precedence rule documented with reproducible test.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship. Documented plugin-vs-plugin same-name precedence in PLUGINS.md and added mergeTools unit test proving later plugin wins with warning and no hard error.

title: Phase 3a: Agent Pane Slot
description:
## Goal
Register ChatPanel from @boring/agent as a panel in the workspace registry. Workspace does NOT import from agent ŌĆö the app shell passes the component in.

## What to implement
- ChatPanel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop (app shell provides it)
- Default placement: 'right' (IdeLayout) or center slot (ChatLayout)
- Lazy-loaded with React.lazy + Suspense wrapper
- requiresCapabilities: ['agent.chat'] ŌĆö only available when capability is set

## How it works (no circular dependency)
- App shell imports ChatPanel from @boring/agent
- App shell passes it to WorkspaceProvider panels prop
- Workspace never imports from @boring/agent
- Types (WorkspaceBridge, CommandResult, etc.) in @boring/core

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Registration
1. Panel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop appears in registry
2. Panel has correct default placement (IdeLayout: 'right', ChatLayout: 'center')
3. Panel component lazy-loads (Suspense fallback shown, then component renders)

### Capability gating
4. Panel visible when capabilities include 'agent.chat'
5. Panel hidden when capabilities do NOT include 'agent.chat'
6. Adding 'agent.chat' capability dynamically makes panel appear

### Isolation (no circular dependency)
7. WorkspaceProvider renders without @boring/agent imported (no hard dependency)
8. Passing null/undefined for panels prop works (no agent panel)
9. Custom panel component (not ChatPanel) can be registered the same way

### Placement
10. In IdeLayout: agent panel renders in right group
11. In ChatLayout: agent panel renders in center group

## Success criteria
- Agent pane renders inside IdeLayout right rail
- Agent pane renders inside ChatLayout center
- Pane lazy-loads correctly
- Capability gating works (hidden when 'agent.chat' not in capabilities)
- All 11 tests pass
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by claude: ship

title: M0: Interface ŌĆö SandboxHandleStore (DB injection seam)
description:
**Goal:** Lock the sandbox-handle persistence interface. File: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface SandboxHandleRecord {
  workspaceId: string
  sandboxId: string
  snapshotId?: string
  createdAt: string
  lastUsedAt: string
}
export interface SandboxHandleStore {
  get(workspaceId: string): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord | null>
  put(record: SandboxHandleRecord): Promise<void>
  delete(workspaceId: string): Promise<void>
  list(): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord[]>
}
```

**Why:** Agent has no DB dependency. Default impl (`FileHandleStore`) writes `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Future `@boring/cloud` injects a DB-backed impl via `createAgentApp({ sandboxHandleStore })`. Same pattern as `SessionStore`.

**Used by:** `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` (M2).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.

**Design note ŌĆö no M1 impl by design:** Bwrap sandboxes in M1 are ephemeral (no handle to persist ŌĆö each sandbox is a fresh process). First implementation is M2: FileHandleStore, which persists Vercel sandbox handles for snapshot resume.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö reviewed by Claude Code: ship

title: Phase 3a: Chat Layout Integration
description:
## Goal
Integrate ChatLayout with agent pane, artifact surface (nested DockviewShell), session list UI, and chat hooks.

## What to implement

### ArtifactSurfacePane (src/panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx)
- Renders its own nested DockviewShell inside ChatLayout's surface group
- storageKey: 'boring-ui-v2:surface' (own persistence, independent of outer shell)
- allowedPanels: ['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty'] (guard against outer-shell panels)
- Only rendered when artifacts exist (conditional mount)
- Sync suppression flag prevents feedback loops between outer and inner state

Nested shell pseudo-code (from plan Section E):
```typescript
// panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx ŌĆö renders inside ChatLayout's surface group
function ArtifactSurfacePane({ artifacts, onSelectArtifact, onCloseArtifact }) {
  return (
    <DockviewShell
      layout={{
        groups: [{ id: 'artifacts', position: 'center', dynamic: true, placeholder: 'empty' }]
      }}
      storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"    // own persistence, independent of outer shell
      allowedPanels={['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty']}
    />
  )
}
// Filters outer panel registry to allowed IDs. Has own API, own state, own lifecycle.
```

### Nested DockviewShell ŌĆö isolation model (ninth pass)
Nesting works naturally because dockview instances are independent. No formal isolation protocol needed for v2.

1. **Persistence**: Each DockviewShell with unique storageKey creates its own Zustand persist partition. Root uses boring-ui-v2:layout, nested surface uses boring-ui-v2:surface.
2. **API scoping**: useDockviewApi() returns the API for the nearest ancestor DockviewShell. Panes inside nested shell interact with the correct instance.
3. **No panel ID namespacing**: panel IDs are plain strings. If two shells have panels with same ID, they're independent (different dockview instances).
4. **No bridge routing**: bridge commands always target root shell. Nested shell panels managed directly by the component rendering the nested DockviewShell.

### Chat hooks (simplified from v1's 8 ŌåÆ 2)
- **useArtifactPanels()** (~50 lines): open/close/track artifact panels in surface dockview
- **useArtifactRouting()** (~30 lines): optional convenience mapping tool names to panel types

**Eliminated by bridge** (no longer needed ŌĆö these were v1 coupling mechanisms):
| v1 hook | Replacement |
|---------|-------------|
| useShellStatePublisher | ŌåÆ store subscriptions |
| useShellPersistence | ŌåÆ Zustand persist |
| useSessionState/Store/ServerSessions | ŌåÆ agent owns via bridge props |
| useToolBridge | ŌåÆ agent calls bridge.openPanel() explicitly |

### Session list UI (workspace renders, agent owns state)

Session list ownership boundary (from plan Resolved Questions):
```
@boring/agent (owns state):
  sessions: Session[]
  activeSessionId: string
  switchSession(id: string): void
  createSession(): Session
  deleteSession(id: string): void

@boring/workspace (owns UI):
  <SessionList
    sessions={agent.sessions}
    activeId={agent.activeSessionId}
    onSwitch={agent.switchSession}
    onCreate={agent.createSession}
    onDelete={agent.deleteSession}
  />
```

- SessionList component: renders { id, title, updatedAt }[] from agent
- Calls agent's switchSession/createSession/deleteSession
- Active session indicator
- Context menu: rename, delete
- Workspace doesn't know what a "session" contains ŌĆö just renders the list with titles and handles the chrome

### NavRail integration
- NavRail props: { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen }
- App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern)

### Tool ŌåÆ artifact routing simplification
v1 approach: toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) ŌĆö workspace infers panel type from tool results.
v2 approach: agent explicitly calls bridge.openPanel() ŌĆö workspace no longer infers. useArtifactRouting() is an optional convenience (~30 lines) that maps common tool names to panel types for backward compatibility.

## v1 references
- ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) ŌĆö nav rail + chat + surface
- SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) ŌĆö artifact surface dockview (nested)
- NavRail.jsx (133 lines) ŌĆö session switcher
- toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) ŌĆö toolŌåÆartifact routing (simplified in v2)
- SurfaceDockview pattern: filtered registry via allowedPanels, gets layout via props, uses callbacks for state flow

## Considerations
- Nested DockviewShell: independent instance, own API, own state, own lifecycle
- useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API (nearest ancestor)
- No panel ID namespacing needed (different dockview instances)
- Bridge commands target root shell only (no multi-shell routing in v1)
- If multi-shell routing needed post-launch (agent opening artifacts in surface from outside), add shell parameter to bridge commands then

## Unit tests (layouts/chat-integration.test.tsx)

### ArtifactSurfacePane
- Test: renders nested DockviewShell with storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"
- Test: allowedPanels filters registry (only code-editor, markdown-editor, csv-viewer, empty)
- Test: outer-shell panel IDs not available in nested shell
- Test: conditional mount ŌĆö not rendered when no artifacts
- Test: sync suppression ŌĆö inner state changes don't feedback to outer

### Chat hooks
- Test: useArtifactPanels() opens artifact panel in surface dockview
- Test: useArtifactPanels() closes artifact panel
- Test: useArtifactPanels() tracks open artifact panels
- Test: useArtifactRouting() maps tool name to correct panel type
- Test: useArtifactRouting() returns undefined for unknown tool

### Session list
- Test: SessionList renders list of { id, title, updatedAt }
- Test: clicking session calls onSwitch(id)
- Test: create button calls onCreate
- Test: context menu delete calls onDelete(id)
- Test: active session has indicator
- Test: empty session list shows "No sessions" state

### NavRail
- Test: NavRail renders with sessions list
- Test: onNewChat callback fires on button click
- Test: onToggleSurface toggles surface open state
- Test: surfaceOpen prop reflects in toggle button state

### Nested shell independence
- Test: useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API
- Test: bridge commands target root shell, not nested
- Test: nested shell has independent persistence (different storageKey)

## E2E tests (Playwright)
- Test: ChatLayout session switch ŌåÆ correct session displayed
- Test: ChatLayout artifact open ŌåÆ surface appears with correct content
- Test: agent message in chat ŌåÆ artifact renders in surface
- Test: nested shell resize independent of outer shell

## Success criteria
- ChatLayout renders with agent chat in center, artifacts in surface
- Artifact surface opens/closes correctly (conditional mount)
- Session list displays and switches sessions
- Nested DockviewShell has independent persistence
- allowedPanels guard prevents outer panels from rendering in surface
- Two chat hooks work correctly (artifact panels + routing)
close_reason: done

title: M0: Interface ŌĆö Sandbox + SandboxCapability + ExecResult
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 sandbox contract. File: `src/shared/sandbox.ts`.

**Interface (exact shapes from spec ┬¦The four abstractions > Sandbox):**
```ts
export type SandboxCapability = 'exec' | 'isolated-code'

export interface Sandbox {
  readonly id: string                          // 'bwrap' | 'direct' | 'vercel-sandbox' | 'boxlite' | ŌĆ”
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'
  readonly capabilities: readonly SandboxCapability[]

  init(ctx: { workspace: Workspace; sessionId: string }): Promise<void>

  // Always available (capability 'exec'):
  exec(cmd: string, opts?: ExecOptions): Promise<ExecResult>

  // Optional ŌĆö present iff capabilities includes 'isolated-code':
  executeIsolatedCode?(input: IsolatedCodeInput): Promise<IsolatedCodeOutput>

  dispose?(): Promise<void>
}

export interface ExecOptions {
  cwd?: string
  env?: Record<string, string>
  signal?: AbortSignal
  timeoutMs?: number                           // hard timeout; kills the child process
  maxOutputBytes?: number                      // per-stream cap; output beyond this is truncated
  onHeartbeat?: (elapsedMs: number) => void    // called every 2s during active exec ŌĆö for <Tool /> ticking UI
}

export interface ExecResult {
  stdout: Uint8Array                            // RAW BYTES, binary-safe ŌĆö server adapter decodes per encoding
  stderr: Uint8Array
  exitCode: number
  durationMs: number
  truncated: boolean                            // true if maxOutputBytes was hit
  stdoutEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'           // best-effort classification for UI rendering
  stderrEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'
}

// ONLY meaningful when capabilities includes 'isolated-code' (future M6+):
export interface IsolatedCodeInput {
  code: string
  language: 'python' | 'shell'
  image?: string                                // e.g. 'python:3.12-slim' ŌĆö sandbox-defined set
  packages?: string[]                           // installed before run (pip/npm/etc, sandbox decides)
  sandboxId?: string                            // reuse a prior pooled VM (TTL-based)
  vmSize?: 'xxs' | 'xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l'
}

export interface IsolatedCodeOutput {
  sandboxId: string                             // for reuse
  stdout: string
  stderr: string
  exitCode: number
}
```

**Invariants:**
- `Uint8Array` (NOT `Buffer` ŌĆö Node-only type leak forbidden in shared contracts).
- Every impl enforces `timeoutMs` (default 30s in adapters) and `maxOutputBytes` (default 1 MB) ŌĆö bytes beyond cap get dropped and `truncated: true`.
- `capabilities` declares support statically. v1 sandboxes declare `['exec']`. Future ones add `'isolated-code'` which unlocks the 5th catalog tool.
- `Sandbox` takes a `Workspace` in `init()` so exec `cwd` defaults to workspace root. Prevents escape-via-cwd.
- `onHeartbeat(elapsedMs)` fires every 2s while a command is still running. Stream adapter uses this to emit `data-status` heartbeat chunks.

**Tests:** Type-level stub + conformance-test requirement noted for infra.conformance-tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions` (with `onHeartbeat`), `ExecResult` (stdout/stderr as `Uint8Array`), `IsolatedCodeInput/Output`. Type-level stub passes. Conformance test harness imports this interface.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions`, `ExecResult`. `stdout`/`stderr` are `Uint8Array` (not `Buffer`). Type-level stub passes.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö cross-reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.3-codex): ship

title: M1: createPiCodingAgentHarness
description:
**Goal:** Wrap `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` as our `AgentHarness`. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/createHarness.ts`.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/runtime.ts` ŌĆö IMPORTANT the old code uses `pi-agent-core`; v2 uses `pi-coding-agent` (which wraps pi-agent-core + plugins + sessions). API surface differs.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createPiCodingAgentHarness(opts: { tools: AgentTool[] }): AgentHarness {
  return {
    id: 'pi-coding-agent', placement: 'server',
    sessions: new PiSessionStore(),   // in-memory in M1; JSONL-backed in M3a
    async *sendMessage(input, ctx) {
      const session = createAgentSession({ tools: opts.tools })   // pi API
      const piStream = session.runTurn({
        sessionId: input.sessionId, message: input.message,
        model: input.model, thinkingLevel: input.thinkingLevel,
        abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
      })
      yield* piEventsToUIMessageChunks(piStream)                   // see m1.stream-adapter
    },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- **Pi built-ins are skipped.** We pass only our `tools: [...]`. Verified by M1 acceptance test (`m1.test.pi-builtin-opt-out`).
- TypeBox ŌåÆ JSON Schema adaptation at the tool boundary: our tools declare JSON Schema; pi receives TypeBox; adapter converts one-way.
- Model registry adaptation: our `'sonnet' | 'haiku' | 'opus'` ŌåÆ pi's model IDs.

**Tests:** one round-trip: send a message with a no-op tool, assert at least one chunk arrives; assert no pi default tool (like pi's bash) is registered.

## Acceptance Criteria

createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.
acceptance_criteria: createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.

title: M3b: Heartbeat events during long tool execution
description:
**Goal:** Every 2s during an in-flight tool call, emit a synthetic `data-status` UIMessage part so `<Tool />` shows elapsed time. Files: stream adapter (~10 LOC) + Tool renderer (~10 LOC).

**Server-side:** wrap the tool execution promise; every 2s if not yet resolved, emit `{type:'data-status', data:{toolCallId, elapsedMs}}`. Flush on resolve/reject.

**Client-side:** `<Tool>` component displays "RunningŌĆ” (Ns)" updating from latest status part.

**Why:** long `bash` calls feel frozen without heartbeats; users ctrl-C unnecessarily.

**Tests:** a 6s bash command emits 3 heartbeats; UI shows ticking counter; resolve clears heartbeat.

## Acceptance Criteria

6s bash emits 3 heartbeats. UI shows ticking counter. Resolve clears heartbeat.
acceptance_criteria: 6s bash emits 3 heartbeats. UI shows ticking counter. Resolve clears heartbeat.

title: Phase 4: Bombadil Property-Based Testing
description:
## Goal
Property-based exploration using Antithesis Bombadil. Random UI exploration validating invariants.

## Properties (invariants)
- Layout has no overlapping panels (no negative widths/heights)
- Essential panels cannot be closed
- Sidebar collapse/expand is reversible
- Layout serialization round-trips: toJSON ŌåÆ fromJSON ŌåÆ toJSON is idempotent
- No React error boundary triggers during normal exploration
- Bridge state matches visible UI (open panels list = actual rendered panels)
- Active file in bridge = file shown in editor

## Actions (random sequences)
- Open/close panels, resize sashes, collapse/expand sidebar
- Click files in tree, switch tabs, trigger shortcuts
- Send bridge commands (openFile, openPanel, closePanel, showNotification)
- Toggle theme, switch layouts

## CI integration
- Nightly run: 10-30 min exploration
- Reports to Slack/GitHub issue if failures found
- Not blocking PRs (too slow)

## Success criteria
- Bombadil finds no property violations
- At least 7 invariants validated
- Runs for 30 min without crash

title: Acceptance Criteria
description: - Item one

title: Phase 4: Theme System
description:
## Goal
Light/dark mode via shadcn CSS variables. CodeMirror + dockview theme synced. ThemeProvider + useTheme() hook.

## Background
Workspace's ThemeProvider wraps core's useTheme() and extends it to sync CM6 and dockview CSS variables. Apps use workspace's useTheme() (re-exported), not core's directly. This avoids theme divergence between code editors and workspace chrome.

## What to implement
### theme/ThemeProvider.tsx + useTheme.ts
- ThemeProvider wraps core's useTheme, extends to sync CM6 + dockview CSS vars
- useTheme(): { theme: 'light' | 'dark', setTheme, toggleTheme } hook
- Persisted to boring-ui-v2:preferences (separate from layout, never reset)
- System preference detection (prefers-color-scheme)
- ThemeProvider adds data-theme attribute to workspace container element

### theme/codemirror-theme.ts
- CM6 theme reading shadcn CSS variables at runtime (--background, --foreground, --muted, etc.)
- Auto-updates on theme change (EditorView.theme reconfigured via compartment)
- Maps shadcn variables to CM6 theme spec: { "&": {}, ".cm-content": {}, ".cm-gutters": {}, etc. }

### Dockview theme sync
- dockview-overrides.css maps --dv-* variables to shadcn vars:
  - --dv-activegroup-visiblepanel-tab-background-color ŌåÆ var(--background)
  - --dv-tabs-container-scrollbar-color ŌåÆ var(--muted)
  - etc. (~15 variable mappings)
- Auto-follows theme toggle, no JS needed (pure CSS variable cascade)

## v1 references
- useTheme.jsx ŌĆö light/dark toggle, localStorage persistence, system preference detection

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### ThemeProvider
1. Renders children with default theme (system preference)
2. useTheme().theme returns 'light' when prefers-color-scheme: light
3. useTheme().theme returns 'dark' when prefers-color-scheme: dark
4. setTheme('dark') updates theme and applies data-theme="dark" to container
5. toggleTheme() switches from light to dark and vice versa
6. Theme persists to boring-ui-v2:preferences localStorage key
7. On mount, reads persisted theme (overrides system preference)
8. Clearing localStorage reverts to system preference

### CodeMirror theme sync
9. CM6 theme compartment reconfigured when theme changes
10. Dark mode: CM6 gutters use --muted CSS variable (verify computed style)
11. Light mode: CM6 background matches --background CSS variable

### Dockview theme sync
12. dockview-overrides.css loaded (verify CSS rules exist in document.styleSheets)
13. Theme toggle changes dockview tab background (via CSS variable cascade)

## Success criteria
- Toggle between light/dark mode
- CodeMirror theme matches workspace theme
- Dockview chrome matches workspace theme
- Theme persists across reloads (preferences key)
- System preference respected as default
- All 13 tests pass
close_reason: Theme system shipped: toggleTheme, data-theme, system preference detection, CM6 compartment reconfiguration, 13 tests

title: core-M2: workspace-route authorization audit (parameterized integration test) ŌĆö BLOCKER
description:
## Goal
Parameterized integration test that, for EVERY route matching `/api/v1/workspaces/:id/**`, asserts:
1. preHandler `requireWorkspaceMember` is REGISTERED on the route (not just behavior).
2. 401 Unauthorized for unauthenticated callers.
3. 403 with `code: 'not_member'` for authenticated non-members.
4. 2xx (or 4xx-but-not-403) for authenticated members.

## Why it matters
v1 had a known vulnerability where any signed-in user could read/update/delete any workspace by guessing UUIDs. This is the SHIP-BLOCKER ensuring v1's vulnerability is closed AND that future routes added without the hook FAIL CI.

## Acceptance criteria
- Test file `__tests__/membershipAudit.test.ts`.
- **Layer 1 (registration check)**: enumerate every route via Fastify's `app.printRoutes({ method: 'GET', commonPrefix: false })` (and other methods). For each route matching `/api/v1/workspaces/:id/**`:
  - Assert the route's `preHandler` config includes `requireWorkspaceMember` (function reference identity check via `app.routes` inspector). This catches "forgot to add preHandler" at a layer above behavior tests.
- **Layer 2 (401 unauthenticated)**: for each route, send request without session cookie; assert 401 with `code: 'unauthorized'`.
- **Layer 3 (403 non-member)**: setup user A as workspace owner; user B authenticated but not a member; B calls each route; assert 403 with `code: 'not_member'`.
- **Layer 4 (2xx member)**: A calls each route with appropriate payload; assert NOT 403.
- **Layer 5 (cross-app isolation)**: user A's workspace W1 in appX; user A signed into appY; A calls W1 route; assert 404 or 403 (NOT a 200 leak).
- Self-discovering: adding a new route without the hook breaks Layer 1 without manual test additions.

## Considerations
- `printRoutes()` filtering alone is NOT sufficient ŌĆö it can miss routes with custom matchers. Layer 1 augments by also walking `app.findRoute(method, path)` for each known workspace path pattern.
- Some routes need different payloads (POST needs body); test fixtures cover the common shapes via a route-spec table.

## Tests
- The test bead IS the test ŌĆö but it has its own assertions to verify it works:
  - **Self-test 1**: temporarily delete `requireWorkspaceMember` from one route in the source; run the audit; assert the audit FAILS with a clear message naming the unguarded route.
  - **Self-test 2**: assert all 4 layers pass against the green main.
- All test events logged with pino: `audit.layer.start { layer, route, method }`, `audit.layer.complete`, `audit.layer.fail { reason, route, method }`.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Auth ┬¦requireWorkspaceMember ┬¦M2 blocker; ┬¦Traps from v1 ŌåÆ Workspace-route authorization; M0_TESTING_CONVENTIONS bead.
close_reason: 5-layer auth audit implemented and self-tested: L1 structural (preHandler registration), L2 unauthenticated 401, L3 non-member 403, L4 member 2xx, L5 cross-app isolation. 44 tests covering all 14 workspace-scoped routes. Self-test confirmed: removing a preHandler fails L1+L3 with the route name.

title: core-M0: workspace dep + ui-shadcn import smoke
description:

Add `@boring/workspace` as a workspace dependency and verify that core can import shadcn primitives from `@boring/workspace/ui-shadcn` (the new dep direction `core ŌåÆ workspace`).

## Why it matters
Core depends on workspace for its sign-in page, user menu, workspace switcher (per the inverted dep graph: `agent ŌåÉ workspace ŌåÉ core`). If this import doesn't resolve cleanly at the package boundary, half of M3 is blocked. We catch it on day 1.

- `pnpm --dir packages/core build` emits a bundle that does NOT inline workspace's primitives (verify via build size + `external` config).

## Considerations
- Workspace's exports must include `./ui-shadcn` ŌĆö verify in `packages/workspace/package.json` first; if missing, this bead blocks until workspace publishes the subpath.
- `@boring/workspace` itself depends on `@boring/agent` (per the new dep graph). Pulling in workspace transitively pulls in agent. Bundle-size note for the bundle-size gate later (M7).

title: M1: Path helpers + realpath validation (port)
description:
**Goal:** Port + adapt the path validation primitives. File: `src/server/workspace/paths.ts`.

**Functions to port (from old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` ~80 LOC):**
- `validatePath(workspaceRoot, relPath): string` ŌĆö rejects `..`, absolute paths, null bytes; returns joined absolute path.
- `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace(workspaceRoot, absPath): Promise<void>` ŌĆö `realpath()` on the resolved path; rejects if symlink escapes root.
- `ensureExistingWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` ŌĆö validate + realpath + existence check.
- `ensureWritableWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` ŌĆö validate + dir-exists check on parent.

**Why port, not rewrite:** battle-hardened, already fuzzed in production. Copy verbatim where possible; only adapt imports.

**Security invariants:**
- Rejects `..` segments before resolve.
- Rejects absolute paths.
- Rejects null bytes.
- Follows symlinks via `realpath` and checks final resolved path starts with `realpath(workspaceRoot)`.

**Tests:** path traversal attack corpus (~20 vectors: `../../etc/passwd`, `foo/../../../bar`, `/etc/passwd`, `foo\x00bar`, symlink-to-outside). Every vector must throw.

**Logging:** on reject, log the vector + reason (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`) for debugging forensics.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 20+ path-traversal vectors in `infra.path-traversal-corpus` rejected. Helpers throw with categorized reason codes (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`).
acceptance_criteria: All 20+ path-traversal vectors in `infra.path-traversal-corpus` rejected. Helpers throw with categorized reason codes (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`).

title: UI-shadcn subpath: ship Vercel-grade styled ChatPanel via @boring/agent/ui-shadcn
description:

**Source strategy:** vendor ai-elements via their CLI. DO NOT hand-roll primitives.
```
cd packages/agent
npx ai-elements@1.9.0 add conversation message response reasoning tool code-block prompt-input loader
```
These drop into src/front-shadcn/components/ui/. We own them afterward (add VENDORED.md attribution).

**Architecture:**
@boring/agent/ui-shadcn/ChatPanel.tsx
  - useAgentChat() ŌĆö unchanged, same hook as bare ChatPanel
  - <Conversation><ConversationContent>
      - <Message role=...>

          - <Reasoning> collapsible (for reasoning parts)

/home/ubuntu/projects/boring-ui/packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/ChatStage.jsx shows the Stage+empty-state+scroll pattern. We do NOT port the full Stage+Wings shell ŌĆö just the chat column. But reading ChatStage is useful for the empty state + scroll behavior.

3. feat(agent): wire ChatPanel to ai-elements primitives
   - Replace placeholder ChatPanel with full implementation using vendored primitives
   - Identical public API to bare ChatPanel (same sessionId, bridge, toolRenderers, extraCommands, onSessionReset props)

   - Text parts ŌåÆ <Response/>; reasoning ŌåÆ <Reasoning/>; user/assistant ŌåÆ <Message role=/>

4. feat(agent): shadcn example app

5. feat(agent): prebuilt CSS fallback

6. docs(agent): ui-shadcn README + gallery

   - Update packages/agent/README.md with a 'Two UI flavors' section + screenshots
   - Cross-review verdict required before close

**Non-goals this bead:**
- Stage+Wings shell (NavRail/BrowseDrawer/SurfaceShell) ŌĆö separate future bead

**Ship criteria:**

title: core-M2: loadConfig + ConfigValidationError + boring.app.toml parser
description:
## Goal
`loadConfig({ tomlPath?, env?, allowMissingSecrets? })` reads `boring.app.toml` + env vars, Zod-validates, returns typed `CoreConfig` or throws `ConfigValidationError`.

## Why it matters
Boot starts here. A misconfigured environment must fail loudly with actionable errors, not silent defaults.

## Acceptance criteria
- `loadConfig` exported from `@boring/core/server`.
- Reads TOML via `@iarna/toml` or similar; defaults to `./boring.app.toml`.
- Reads env from `process.env` by default; overridable for tests.
- Zod schema for `CoreConfig` exhaustively covers every field declared in CORE.md ┬¦Config (cors, bodyLimit, logLevel, encryption, auth, features).
- `allowMissingSecrets: true` fills placeholders for `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`, `DATABASE_URL`, `WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `MAIL_*` and logs `config:insecure-defaults` warn at boot. Refused if `NODE_ENV === 'production'`.
- Throws `ConfigValidationError extends Error` with `.issues: ZodIssue[]` on any validation failure.
- `validateConfig(unknown)` for runtime validation of programmatic configs (e.g. tests passing config objects).
- `buildRuntimeConfigPayload(config)` returns the redacted client-facing subset for `GET /api/v1/config`.

## Considerations
- Mail transport URL parser inside `loadConfig`: validates scheme is one of `smtp://`, `smtps://`, `resend://`, `console://`. Unknown scheme ŌåÆ ConfigValidationError.
- `cors.origins` parsed from comma-separated `CORS_ORIGINS` env var. Empty in dev = `['http://localhost:3000', 'http://localhost:5173']` auto-allow.
- `auth.sessionCookieSecure` derived from `BETTER_AUTH_URL` scheme (https ŌåÆ true) unless `SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE` overrides.

## Tests
- Valid config loads + matches snapshot.
- Missing `DATABASE_URL` in prod ŌåÆ throws ConfigValidationError.
- Same with `allowMissingSecrets: true` ŌåÆ loads + warns.
- Bad mail transport URL (`mailto://`) ŌåÆ throws.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Config; ┬¦Loader API.
close_reason: shipped in scaffold commit 96346a0 ŌĆö loadConfig + validateConfig + buildRuntimeConfigPayload + ConfigValidationError + Zod schema + 19 tests. Reviewed by codex.

title: core-M1: schema ŌĆö better-auth tables (users, sessions, accounts, verification_tokens)
description:
## Goal
Generate and commit the better-auth-owned tables via better-auth's Drizzle adapter generator. These are the foundational tables every core-owned FK references.

## Why it matters
better-auth manages its own schema ŌĆö we don't hand-write `users`, `sessions`, `accounts`, `verification_tokens`. The adapter generator is authoritative; deviation breaks better-auth's runtime.

## How this avoids a circular dep with M2

This bead is in M1 (schema), M2 has the full `createAuth(config, db)` wiring. To run `@better-auth/cli generate`, we need an instance ŌĆö but only the SCHEMA-relevant parts need to be configured (database adapter + plugin list). The credentials/secrets/oauth/mail are NOT needed to generate the schema.

M1 ships a `packages/core/src/server/auth/schema-config.ts` with a MINIMAL configuration: just `database: drizzleAdapter(db, {provider: 'pg'})` + `emailAndPassword: { enabled: true }` + `magicLink` plugin enabled (so verification_tokens table is generated). NO secrets, NO mail callbacks, NO OAuth ŌĆö those don't affect the table shape. The CLI run uses this minimal config; M2's `createAuth(config, db)` reuses the same module + extends it with secrets/callbacks.

## Acceptance criteria
- `packages/core/src/server/auth/schema-config.ts` exports a minimal `betterAuth` config used by the CLI.
- `pnpm --dir packages/core auth:generate-schema` script runs `npx @better-auth/cli generate --config src/server/auth/schema-config.ts --output drizzle/`.
- Migration SQL committed to `packages/core/drizzle/`.
- `users` table has columns: `id` (uuid), `email`, `name`, `email_verified`, `image`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- `sessions` has rotation columns + `userId` with `ON DELETE CASCADE` to `users.id`.
- `accounts` has the OAuth-account-linking shape better-auth defines + `ON DELETE CASCADE`.
- `verification_tokens` has the magic-link / email-verification token shape.
- `pnpm --dir packages/core drizzle:migrate` against `boring_ui_test` creates all 4 tables.

## Considerations
- The minimal schema-config is a SUBSET of what M2's createAuth wires; both modules share the same `database` + `plugins` declarations. M2 imports and extends it (spread + add fields).
- If a future better-auth plugin requires new columns, this bead adds the plugin to schema-config.ts AND M2's createAuth gets the same plugin with full config (secrets/callbacks).
- DO NOT hand-edit generated tables; if better-auth's generator disagrees with what we wrote, the generator wins.

## Tests
- Integration: drop+recreate `boring_ui_test`; run `drizzle:migrate`; assert all 4 tables exist with expected columns via `pg_dump --schema-only`.
- Unit: import schema-config.ts in a vitest test; assert it can be passed to `betterAuth({})` without throwing.

## Refs

title: Migrate from hand-rolled tools to pi factories + Operations
description:
# Pi tools migration

## Background

`@boring/agent` currently ships six hand-rolled tool implementations (`bash`, `read`, `write`, `edit`, `find_files`, `grep_files`) that shadow pi-coding-agent's built-in tools via `customTools`. ~1100 LOC of duplicated logic across 6 files plus 600 LOC of tests. Two cosmetic bugs leak ~700 chars into every system prompt (double-dash on `promptSnippet`, near-duplicate per-tool guidelines).

Pi-coding-agent was designed to support exactly our use case. Issue [pi-mono#564](https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564) explicitly added the `XxxOperations` seam "for remote delegation" with an SSH example as its acceptance criteria.

## Design principles (locked)

Every bead under this epic obeys three principles. When in doubt, default to the lower-numbered principle.

### Principle 1 ŌĆö Leverage pi's model-facing surface verbatim

Tool **names**, **schemas**, **prompt snippets**, and **guideline phrasing** all come from pi unchanged. The model sees a stock pi agent.

- Use pi's tool names (`find`, `grep`, `read`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `ls`) ŌĆö never our own variants.
- Use pi's TypeBox schemas as authored ŌĆö never re-declare them.
- Use pi's `promptSnippet` wording ŌĆö never wrap with our own bullets.
- Future pi tool releases flow into our catalog with zero shape work on our side.

Motivation: cross-agent conventions. Models trained on pi/Claude Code/Cursor naming and schemas perform better with consistent surface area across agents. Diverging is a tax we pay every turn.

### Principle 2 ŌĆö Replace internal behavior via Operations / spawnHooks

When we need different *behavior* (sandboxed exec, remote VM, audit logging), plug it in through pi's `XxxOperations` seam ŌĆö never by shadowing pi's tool with our own definition.

Consequence: every line of code we own is in **Operations adapters, spawnHooks, or shared resource bundles** ŌĆö never in tool definitions themselves. Adding a new sandbox provider (Modal, Docker, Apple container) is "implement six Operations interfaces" ŌĆö not "rewrite six tools."

### Principle 3 ŌĆö Add custom AgentTools only when pi cannot be made to work

Custom AgentTools (registered via `customTools` through `tool-adapter.ts`) exist for **two cases only**:

1. **Pi has no equivalent.** Example: `executeIsolatedCodeTool`.
2. **Pi's tool architecture cannot accommodate our backend.** Example: `vercelGrepTool` ŌĆö pi's `GrepOperations` cannot redirect the rg spawn (only `isDirectory` + `readFile` for context lines), so vercel-sandbox needs a custom tool. **Even then we mirror pi's grep schema 1:1** so the model's behavior is identical across modes (Principle 1 still applies to the schema).

Any new addition to `customTools` must justify itself against these two cases.

## Phases

- **Phase 0 ŌĆö Tool file cleanup.** Pure refactor on the existing six files: extract shared primitives, drop dead code, fix inconsistencies. Stands alone even if Phase 1 is deferred. Beads `uhwx.1` through `uhwx.4`.

- **Phase 1.0 ŌĆö Streaming Sandbox.exec.** Precursor: extend our `Sandbox.exec` with `onStdout`/`onStderr` callbacks so pi's `BashOperations.exec` can stream output through our adapters. Bead `uhwx.16`.

- **Phase 1 ŌĆö Adopt pi's factory tools per-mode via Operations.** Replace the hand-rolled six with pi's factories. Mode-by-mode rollout (direct ŌåÆ local ŌåÆ vercel-sandbox) for independent rollback. Beads `uhwx.5` through `uhwx.15`.

## Net result

After both phases: ~570 LOC of code we own vs ~1830 today. **75% reduction** on this surface. Plus pi's polish for free (process-tree kill on bash abort, macOS path-fuzzy-match, auto-fd/rg-download, smart truncation, .gitignore-aware globber).

## Alignment with PLUGIN_MODEL.md

`filesystemPlugin` becomes a factory `(bundle: RuntimeBundle) => Plugin` whose `agentTools` field is constructed via `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)`. PLUGIN_MODEL's `Plugin` contract is unchanged.

## References

- Plan: `packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md`
- Original spec: `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` (decisions #4, #6)
- Companion plan (lands AFTER this): `packages/workspace/docs/plans/PLUGIN_MODEL.md`
- Pi RFC for Operations: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564
- Pi SSH extension reference: `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` in pi-coding-agent
- Pi sandbox extension reference: `examples/extensions/sandbox/index.ts`

title: [Phase 0.1] Extract _shared.ts with helpers + types + constants
description:
# Phase 0.1 ŌĆö Extract `_shared.ts` foundation

## Why

Six tool files in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/` duplicate the same helpers, types, and constants. This bead extracts them into one shared module so subsequent refactors (Phase 0.2+) can consume from a single source.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/_shared.ts` exporting:

```ts
export function makeError(message: string): ToolResult
export function bytesWritten(content: string): number
export function nowIso(): string
export function normalizeLimit(raw: unknown, opts: { default: number; max: number }): { limit: number; error?: string }

export const decoder: TextDecoder        // single shared `new TextDecoder("utf-8", { fatal: false })`
export function decode(bytes: Uint8Array): string

export type FileChangeOp = "write" | "edit" | "unlink" | "rename" | "mkdir"
export interface FileChangeMetadata {
  op: FileChangeOp
  path: string
  oldPath?: string
  timestamp: string
  size?: number
  /** Distinguishes file:created from file:changed in the workspace bridge.
   *  Universal field ŌĆö set even by edit ops, where it is always true. */
  existsBefore?: boolean
}

export const DEFAULT_TOOL_LIMIT = 200
export const MAX_TOOL_LIMIT = 5_000
export const MAX_PATTERN_LENGTH = 256
```

Plus co-located unit tests in `__tests__/_shared.test.ts` covering each helper's happy path + edge cases.

## Acceptance criteria

- `_shared.ts` exists with all exports above.
- `_shared.test.ts` green.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- No tool file consumes any of these symbols yet (that lands in Phase 0.2).

## References

- Plan: `packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md` ┬¦ "Phase 0 - audit findings".
