Package-wide tsc is blocked by pre-existing Sandbox.provider/optional-init errors from committed 8eb1c93; tracked as boring-ui-v2-uhwx.20 and currently coordinated with StormyFinch reservations.

title: [Phase 1.6] Flip direct mode in createAgentApp to use new bundle factories
description:
# Phase 1.6 ŌĆö Flip `direct` mode in createAgentApp

## Why

First mode flip. Direct mode is the simplest (no sandboxing) and the playground default ŌĆö easiest to smoke-test. If anything goes wrong, blast radius is local-dev only.

## What to change

In `packages/agent/src/server/createAgentApp.ts`:

```diff
- const tools = mergeTools({
-   standardTools: standardCatalog(runtimeBundle),
-   extraTools: opts.extraTools,
-   pluginTools,
-   logger: app.log,
- })
+ const tools = (
+   resolvedMode === "direct"
+     ? [
+         ...buildHarnessAgentTools(runtimeBundle),
+         ...(opts.disableDefaultFileTools ? [] : buildFilesystemAgentTools(runtimeBundle)),
+         ...(opts.extraTools ?? []),
+         ...flattenPluginTools(pluginTools),
+       ]
+     : mergeTools({
+         standardTools: standardCatalog(runtimeBundle),
+         extraTools: opts.extraTools,
+         pluginTools,
+         logger: app.log,
+       })
+ )
```

Add `disableDefaultFileTools?: boolean` to `CreateAgentAppOptions` (default false). Document.

## Test plan

### Unit (Vitest)

`packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/createAgentApp.direct-flip.test.ts` ŌĆö new tests:

1. **`returns 6 file tools + 1 bash + 0 isolated when in direct mode`** ŌĆö boot `createAgentApp({ mode: "direct" })` with a stub workspace + sandbox without `isolated-code` capability; assert `harness.getTools().map(t => t.name)` is `["bash", "read", "write", "edit", "find", "grep", "ls"]` (or subset based on bundle implementation).
2. **`disableDefaultFileTools omits all file ops`** ŌĆö same boot with `disableDefaultFileTools: true`; assert tools is `["bash"]` (only harness).
3. **`extraTools are appended after bundles`** ŌĆö boot with `extraTools: [customTool]`; assert customTool is last.
4. **`pluginTools merge as today`** ŌĆö load a fake plugin, assert its tools appear in the array.
5. **`other modes still use standardCatalog`** ŌĆö boot with `mode: "vercel-sandbox"` (mocked); assert tools still include hand-rolled implementations (not flipped yet).

Each test logs: `[direct-flip-test] mode=<m> disableDefaultFileTools=<b> tools=<names>`

### Integration / smoke

- Run `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-modes/direct.mts` (built in `uhwx.17`) ŌĆö 6 tool exercises against a real direct-mode agent app. Each exercise logs the prompt, the tool-input chunks, the tool-output chunks, and the final assertion result.
- Run the playground at `pnpm --filter @boring/example-playground dev` and walk the 7 manual checks listed under "Acceptance criteria".

### System-prompt regression

`packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/system-prompt-size.regression.test.ts` (added in uhwx.17, but the assertion can be co-landed here too):
- Pre-flip: capture baseline size.
- Post-flip: assert size dropped by Ōēź 700 chars.
- Assert no double-dash bullets in tool descriptions.
- Assert no `Prefer the X tool when` lines.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [ ] All new + existing tests pass.
- [ ] Manual smoke (playground at `pnpm --filter @boring/example-playground dev`):
  - [ ] Send: "list workspace files" ŌåÆ `find` tool fires, returns Ōēź 1 file.
  - [ ] Send: "read package.json" ŌåÆ `read` tool fires, returns the file.
  - [ ] Send: "create a file foo.txt with content hi" ŌåÆ `write` tool fires, file exists on disk.
  - [ ] Send: "edit foo.txt to say hello" ŌåÆ `edit` tool fires, content updated.
  - [ ] Send: "find all uses of useEffect" ŌåÆ `grep` tool fires, returns matches.
  - [ ] Send: "run ls" ŌåÆ `bash` tool fires, returns stdout.
  - [ ] Send: "list this directory" ŌåÆ `ls` tool fires, returns entries.
- [ ] System prompt at `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/system-prompt`:
  - [ ] No double-dash bullets in tool descriptions.
  - [ ] No 6 redundant `Prefer the X tool when...` lines.
  - [ ] Tool names are `bash`, `read`, `write`, `edit`, `find`, `grep`, `ls`.
  - [ ] Total prompt size dropped at least ~700 chars vs pre-1.6 baseline.

## Rollback runbook

If smoke fails, revert this PR. Outcome:
- `createAgentApp.ts` returns to its pre-PR shape (still calls `standardCatalog`).
- `standardCatalog.ts` and `src/server/catalog/tools/*.ts` are still present and compile (Phase 0 cleanup leaves them intact).
- Bundle factories (`buildHarnessAgentTools`, `buildFilesystemAgentTools`) remain present but unused.
- Tests in `__tests__/createAgentApp.direct-flip.test.ts` start failing (assert flipped behavior); skip them or revert the test file too.
- Local mode + vercel-sandbox mode are NOT affected (they were not flipped yet).
- No data migration needed ŌĆö sessions are not affected by tool registration changes.

Re-attempt: identify the failure, fix in the bundle factory or Operations adapter (likely `uhwx.5` boundFs or `uhwx.7`), re-PR.

## Depends on

- 1.3 (buildHarnessAgentTools)
- 1.4 (buildFilesystemAgentTools)

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 2 - Flip direct mode".
- Plan ┬¦ "Test strategy".

title: [Phase 1.7] Flip local mode (adds bwrap spawnHook)
description:
# Phase 1.7 ŌĆö Flip `local` mode (bwrap)

## Why

Second mode flip. Local mode adds the bwrap spawn-hook on top of the direct-mode design. Mostly identical code paths to direct; the only addition is `bashOptionsForMode` returns `{ operations: createLocalBashOperations(), spawnHook: bwrapSpawnHook(root) }` for local.

## What to change

Extend the branching in `createAgentApp.ts` from 1.6 to also handle `mode === "local"`. Other modes still on hand-rolled.

## Test plan

### Unit (Vitest)

Add `packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/createAgentApp.local-flip.test.ts`:

1. **`local mode produces bash with bwrap spawnHook`** ŌĆö boot `createAgentApp({ mode: "local" })`; introspect the bash tool (or hook the spawn) and assert the resulting command is wrapped in `bwrap ${bwrapArgs(root)} -- bash -lc ...`.
2. **`local mode produces same 6 file tools as direct`** ŌĆö assert tool names match direct's 7 (bash + 6 file ops).
3. **`local mode boundFs still rejects path escapes`** ŌĆö pass `read("/etc/passwd")` through; expect rejection (regression check on uhwx.5 path-bounding).

Each test logs: `[local-flip-test] step=<n> ...`

### Integration / smoke

- Run `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-modes/local.mts` (built in `uhwx.17`).
- Manually verify via local boot: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local pnpm --filter @boring/agent dev`. Required: bwrap installed (`apt install bubblewrap` on Linux).

### System checks

- During a `bash` exec via the local-mode agent, run `ps aux | grep bwrap` on the host; expect to see a `bwrap` process tree wrapping the actual command.
- Verify a `bash echo "$$" && pwd` reports the bwrap-namespaced PID and the workspace path.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [ ] New + existing tests pass.
- [ ] Manual smoke with `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local` (requires bwrap installed):
  - [ ] All 7 tool exercises from `uhwx.10` succeed.
  - [ ] `ps aux | grep bwrap` shows bwrap in the process tree during a bash exec.
  - [ ] `read("/etc/passwd")` is rejected (boundFs working under local mode).
  - [ ] Streaming bash output works (regression check on uhwx.16): `bash "for i in {1..50}; do echo line-$i; sleep 0.05; done"` shows progressive output, not one chunk at end.

## Rollback runbook

If smoke fails, revert this PR. Outcome:
- `createAgentApp.ts` for `mode === "local"` returns to using `standardCatalog`.
- Direct mode (uhwx.10) is NOT affected.
- vercel-sandbox mode is NOT affected.
- No data migration needed.
- The bwrapSpawnHook helper added for this PR can stay (unused) or be reverted along with the rest.

Re-attempt path: bwrap-related issues are typically about flag mismatch; `BwrapSandbox` already encodes the canonical flag set; cross-check `bwrapArgs(root)` against `packages/agent/src/server/sandbox/bwrap/createBwrapSandbox.ts`.

## Depends on

- 1.6 (uhwx.10) direct mode flipped first

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 3 - Flip local mode".
- Pi sandbox extension: `examples/extensions/sandbox/index.ts:132-198`.
- Existing bwrap impl for flag reference: `packages/agent/src/server/sandbox/bwrap/createBwrapSandbox.ts`.

title: [Phase 1.8] Flip vercel-sandbox mode (uses vercelGrepTool + vercel*Ops)
description:
# Phase 1.8 ŌĆö Flip `vercel-sandbox` mode

## Why

Third mode flip. Highest-risk because every tool routes Operations to the remote VM. Lifts the pattern verbatim from pi's `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` (the canonical reference per pi-mono#564). After this step, `standardCatalog.ts` is unused and can be deleted in 1.9.

The vercel-sandbox path is **empirically validated** end-to-end (see smoke script at `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` + bead 1.0/1.16). Streaming bash, abort, fs roundtrip ŌĆö all known-working on the real SDK.

## What to change

Extend the branching in `createAgentApp.ts` from 1.7 to also handle `mode === "vercel-sandbox"`. After this step the `standardCatalog(...)` branch is unreachable.

Update `packages/agent/src/ui-shadcn/workspaceToolRenderers.tsx:30` to handle both pi-built tools (which carry pi's render via the `ToolDefinition` shape) and our `vercelGrepTool` (a plain `AgentTool` rendered via the generic fallback). Both should look visually similar ŌĆö write a custom renderer keyed on `grep` to bridge the gap if the default differs.

## Test plan

### Unit (Vitest)

`packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/createAgentApp.vercel-flip.test.ts`:

1. **`vercel-sandbox produces vercelGrepTool, not pi grep`** ŌĆö assert `tools.find(t => t.name === "grep")` is the custom AgentTool, not pi's factory output.
2. **`vercel-sandbox produces 6 file tools (no pi grep)`** ŌĆö assert names are `["bash", "read", "write", "edit", "find", "grep", "ls"]` (grep is ours; rest are pi's).
3. **`vercel-sandbox forwards bash exec to sandbox.exec with streaming`** ŌĆö mock `sandbox.exec`, send a bash turn, assert it was called with `onStdout` callback (regression check on uhwx.16).
4. **`vercel-sandbox writeFile lands in sandbox.writeFiles`** ŌĆö mock the sandbox, send a write turn, assert `sandbox.writeFiles` was called with the path + content.

Each test logs `[vercel-flip-test] step=<n> ...`.

### Integration / smoke

- Run `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-modes/vercel-sandbox.mts` (built in `uhwx.17`) ŌĆö exercises all 7 tools against a real Vercel sandbox.
- Run the existing `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` as a low-level streaming regression check.

### Renderer parity check

- Open the playground in vercel-sandbox mode (`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox`), trigger `find` and `grep` turns, screenshot the tool cards.
- Open the playground in direct mode, trigger same turns.
- Compare visually: titles, layout, output formatting should match. If they do not, add a custom renderer for `grep` to bridge the gap.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [ ] New + existing tests pass.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` regression check passes (streaming + abort + fs).
- [ ] Manual smoke deploy to vercel-sandbox (`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox`, secrets from vault):
  - [ ] All 7 tool exercises from uhwx.10 succeed.
  - [ ] Each tool call routes to `sandbox.*` (verify via sandbox-side log).
  - [ ] `vercelGrepTool` returns matches from inside the VM (not from the agent host filesystem). Sanity test: `grep "package.json"` should NOT find this repo's `package.json` because the VM has its own filesystem.
  - [ ] Bash STREAMS to the chat UI as bytes arrive (regression check on uhwx.16): run `for i in {1..30}; do echo line-$i; sleep 0.1; done` and verify text content grows over the 3s run, not one chunk at end.
  - [ ] Abort kills bash within ~50ms (smoke proved 12ms is achievable on the SDK level).
- [ ] Playground tool-card visual parity between modes (find + grep look the same).

## Rollback runbook

If smoke fails, revert this PR. Outcome:
- `createAgentApp.ts` for `mode === "vercel-sandbox"` returns to using `standardCatalog`.
- Direct + local modes are NOT affected (already shipped in 1.6 + 1.7).
- The `vercelGrepTool` (uhwx.9), `vercel*Ops` adapters (uhwx.6), and bundle factories (uhwx.7/8) remain present but unused for this mode.
- The renderer changes for `grep` can stay ŌĆö they are forward-compatible.
- Vault secrets stay in place.

Common failure modes + fix paths:
- **Streaming regression**: `vercelBashOps` not forwarding `onStdout` correctly ŌĆö re-check `Writable` wiring against `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`.
- **Path resolution**: pi resolves model input against cwd, then forwards absolute path; if `vercelReadOps` rejects valid paths, debug `vercelReadOps`'s path translation.
- **fd not in VM**: vercel sandboxes ship `fd` and `rg` by default; if missing, the `vercelFindOps` adapter must `npm install -g fd-find` or similar at sandbox boot.

## Depends on

- 1.7 (uhwx.11) local mode already flipped.
- 1.5 (uhwx.9) vercelGrepTool exists.
- 1.6 (uhwx.6) vercel*Ops adapters exist.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 4 - Flip vercel-sandbox mode".
- Smoke script: `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` (proves the underlying SDK behavior; this bead is the integration step).
- Pi reference: `examples/extensions/ssh.ts:81-220`.
- Pi RFC: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564.

title: [Phase 1.9] Delete hand-rolled tools, standardCatalog, slim tool-adapter
description:
# Phase 1.9 ŌĆö Delete hand-rolled tools + standardCatalog + slim tool-adapter

## Principle alignment

This bead codifies **Principle 1 ŌĆö leverage pi's surface verbatim**. After it lands, no source file in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/` defines a tool with a name that pi already ships (bash/read/write/edit/find/grep/ls). Future contributors who try to "add our own read tool" hit a `git log -p` showing this deletion plus the principle write-up in the epic; subsequently uhwx.18's CI invariants block accidental re-introduction.

## Why

After all three modes have been flipped (1.6 + 1.7 + 1.8) and the E2E suite (uhwx.17) is green and stable for Ōēź 3 days, the hand-rolled tools and `standardCatalog.ts` are unused. Time to remove them.

`tool-adapter.ts` no longer needs the double-dash `promptSnippet` (`- \`${name}\` ŌĆö ${description}`) or the per-tool `promptGuidelines` (`Prefer the \`${name}\` tool when...`) ŌĆö those were workarounds for advertising our shadowed tools to the model. Pi's factory tools carry their own snippets. **Removing the per-tool guidelines is a direct application of the "no guideline bullets per tool" anti-pattern (Principle 1).**

## What to delete

- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/bashTool.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/readTool.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/writeTool.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/editTool.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/findFilesTool.ts`
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/grepFilesTool.ts`
- All co-located test files for the above.
- `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/standardCatalog.ts`
- The double-dash `promptSnippet` and per-tool `promptGuidelines` lines in `packages/agent/src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/tool-adapter.ts`.

**Per AGENTS.md Rule 1 (No File Deletion without permission):** this bead's closure IS the explicit permission. Reference this bead ID in the commit message (`refs uhwx.13`) so the deletion is traceable.

## What stays

- `_shared.ts` ŌĆö used by `vercelGrepTool` + `executeIsolatedCodeTool`.
- `vercelGrepTool.ts` ŌĆö only justified custom tool by Principle 3 case 2.
- `executeIsolatedCodeTool.ts` ŌĆö only justified custom tool by Principle 3 case 1.
- `tool-adapter.ts` ŌĆö slimmed to ~30 LOC. Maps our `AgentTool` shape to pi's `ToolDefinition` for genuinely-custom tools (vercelGrep, executeIsolatedCode, plugin tools, host-supplied `extraTools`). After slimming, it must NOT add per-tool guideline bullets or wrap snippets ŌĆö preserve the `promptSnippet: tool.description` (no decoration) line forever.
- `operations/`, `harness/`, `filesystem/` directories from 1.1-1.4.

## Test plan

### Unit (Vitest) ŌĆö these tests already exist; this bead only ensures they survive the deletion

1. `tool-adapter.test.ts` ŌĆö adapt a custom `AgentTool` and assert `promptSnippet` is NOT prefixed with backticks/dash and `promptGuidelines` is NOT auto-populated per-tool.
2. Existing tests for `vercelGrepTool`, `executeIsolatedCodeTool`, and bundle factories continue to pass unchanged.

### Smoke (regression)

- All three smoke scripts under `scripts/smoke-modes/` (built in uhwx.17) still pass.
- `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` still passes.
- System-prompt-size regression test still asserts the size budget.

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [ ] Full test suite green (E2E suite from uhwx.17 included).
- [ ] `bash scripts/check-invariants.sh packages/agent` passes.
- [ ] A grep over `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/` shows only `_shared.ts`, `vercelGrepTool.ts`, `executeIsolatedCodeTool.ts`, and their tests.
- [ ] A grep over `packages/agent/src/` for `Prefer the \`` (the per-tool guideline pattern) returns zero hits.
- [ ] `tool-adapter.ts` final shape verified: no `- \`${tool.name}\``, no `promptGuidelines: [...]`.
- [ ] System-prompt-size regression test (uhwx.17) still passes (size remains low).
- [ ] Commit message references `uhwx.13` for traceability of file deletions.

## Rollback runbook

If post-deletion tests fail, revert this PR. Outcome:
- All deleted files come back via git revert.
- `standardCatalog.ts` is restored (uses hand-rolled tools).
- `tool-adapter.ts` re-grows the double-dash snippet + per-tool guidelines.
- Mode flips (1.6/1.7/1.8) remain in place ŌĆö they call the bundle factories, NOT `standardCatalog`. The hand-rolled tools come back unused unless `createAgentApp.ts` is also reverted to call `standardCatalog`.

Common failure modes + fix paths:
- **Test mock breakage**: a test file imports from a deleted tool file. Fix: delete the test or re-point to the bundle factory.
- **Plugin tool collision**: a plugin tool was hard-coded to use a deleted `createBashTool` import. Fix: plugin migrates to its own AgentTool.
- **Renderer reference to old name**: covered by uhwx.14 rename, but if it slips into this bead's smoke, fix here.

## Depends on

- 1.6, 1.7, 1.8 (all three mode flips landed and stable)
- 1.8.5 (uhwx.17) E2E suite green and stable for Ōēź 3 days

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Design principles" ŌĆö this bead is the enforcement point.
- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 5".
- AGENTS.md Rule 1 (file deletion needs permission ŌĆö this bead IS that permission).

title: [Phase 1.10] Rename find_files->find, grep_files->grep across renderers + docs + macro
description:
# Phase 1.10 ŌĆö Rename find_files ŌåÆ find, grep_files ŌåÆ grep across the codebase

## Why

Pi's tool names are `find` and `grep`. Ours have been `find_files` and `grep_files` (off-spec drift). After 1.6+ ships, the model sees pi's names in the catalog every turn ŌĆö but our tool renderers, test mocks, and docs still reference the old names.

This bead aligns all callsites. Doc-only for some files; test-mock changes for others.

## What to update

`grep -rn "find_files\\|grep_files" packages/` returns these expected sites:

- `packages/agent/src/ui-shadcn/workspaceToolRenderers.tsx:30` ŌĆö renderer map keyed by tool name. Rename keys.
- `packages/workspace/src/components/chat/workspaceToolRenderers.tsx` if a separate file exists.
- Any test mocks asserting old names.
- `packages/workspace/docs/plans/PLUGIN_MODEL.md` lines 1145, 1550-1554 ŌĆö doc-only update.
- Boring-macro-v2 if it asserts tool names anywhere (`grep -rn "find_files\\|grep_files" /home/ubuntu/projects/boring-macro-v2/src/`).
- README.md / AGENTS.md mentions if any.

## PLUGIN_MODEL.md note

Co-land or follow-up: PLUGIN_MODEL.md needs a one-paragraph note that some default plugins (e.g. `filesystemPlugin`) are factory functions `(bundle: RuntimeBundle) => Plugin` rather than static module exports. The `Plugin` interface contract is unchanged ŌĆö `agentTools: AgentTool[]` stays a plain array; the array is just constructed at plugin instantiation. See plan ┬¦ "Alignment with PLUGIN_MODEL.md".

## Acceptance criteria

- `grep -rn "find_files\\|grep_files" packages/ apps/` returns zero hits (modulo intentional historical references in CHANGELOG).
- Tool-renderer test: every tool name produced by `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)` (in any mode) has a renderer entry.
- Manual smoke: vercel-sandbox grep results render identically to pi-default grep results visually.

## Depends on

- 1.9 (the standardCatalog still using old names is gone)

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 6 - Tool-name renames downstream".
- Plan ┬¦ "Alignment with PLUGIN_MODEL.md".

title: [Phase 1.11] Optional: add ls tool + finalize spec/README/CHANGELOG docs
description:
# Phase 1.11 ŌĆö Optional: add `ls` + finalize docs

## Why

`agent-package-spec.md` decision #6 said "Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred." With pi's `createLsTool` factory, adding ls is a 5-LOC addition per mode in `buildFilesystemAgentTools`. It gives the model an explicit "list directory" affordance and reduces `bash ls` usage. **Optional** ŌĆö independent decision.

## What to add (if proceeding)

In `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)`:
- direct + local: `createLsTool(cwd, { operations: ops.ls })`
- vercel-sandbox: `createLsTool(cwd, { operations: vercelLsOps(bundle.sandbox) })`

Add a renderer entry in `workspaceToolRenderers.tsx`.

## Final docs

- Update `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` decisions #4 and #6 with post-migration reality (or mark them as superseded by `pi-tools-migration.md`).
- Update `packages/agent/README.md` if it documents tool names.
- Add a brief CHANGELOG entry summarizing the migration.

## Acceptance criteria (if ls added)

- `ls` appears in `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle).map(t => t.name)` for every mode.
- Manual smoke: model uses `ls` instead of `bash ls` for directory listings.
- System-prompt size remains within budget (each new tool adds ~50-100 chars).

## Depends on

- 1.10 (renames done)

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 7 - Optional ls + final cleanup".
- Spec decisions #4 + #6.

title: [Phase 1.0] Add streaming onStdout/onStderr to Sandbox.exec (gates bash migration)
description:
# Phase 1.0 ŌĆö Add streaming callbacks to Sandbox.exec

## Status: design empirically verified 2026-04-28

Smoke test (`packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`) ran against a real Vercel sandbox and confirmed:

- `runCommand({ stdout: Writable })` produced **80 incremental `write()` calls** for a 100-line/1.3s command ŌĆö first chunk at +421ms, evenly spread until end.
- `AbortController.abort()` propagation: `runCommand` throws "This operation was aborted" within 12ms.
- `writeFiles` + cat roundtrip works.

The SDK exposes streaming via two APIs: `Command.logs()` async-generator AND `runCommand({ stdout, stderr: Writable })` pipe-to-Writable. The `Writable` path is simpler and is what this bead implements.

Implementation can proceed as designed ŌĆö no upstream PR or workaround needed.

## Why

Pi's `BashOperations.exec` requires `onData(buf)` callback invoked incrementally as bytes arrive. Our current `Sandbox.exec` (per `packages/agent/src/shared/sandbox.ts:5-36`) is buffered ŌĆö returns `Promise<ExecResult>` with full `stdout`/`stderr` collected only after the command exits. Plugging pi's bash tool into our buffered exec without streaming means:

- pi's mid-command truncation logic cannot fire ŌĆö gigabytes can buffer before pi sees the first byte.
- memory blowup risk on unbounded-output commands (`find / -type f` style), especially in `vercel-sandbox` where the buffer accumulates in the VM.
- live progressive output rendering in the chat UI is permanently blocked.

This bead extends our shared interface + all three adapters BEFORE any tool migration begins, so pi's bash plugs in cleanly via a thin Operations wrapper.

## What to change

### 1. Shared interface (`packages/agent/src/shared/sandbox.ts`)

```diff
 export interface ExecOptions {
   cwd?: string
   env?: Record<string, string>
   signal?: AbortSignal
   timeoutMs?: number
   maxOutputBytes?: number
   onHeartbeat?: (elapsedMs: number) => void
+  /** Streamed stdout chunks. Invoked incrementally as bytes arrive. */
+  onStdout?: (chunk: Uint8Array) => void
+  /** Streamed stderr chunks. Invoked incrementally as bytes arrive. */
+  onStderr?: (chunk: Uint8Array) => void
 }
```

Keep buffered `stdout`/`stderr` in `ExecResult` for backward compat ŌĆö non-pi consumers still see the full collected output. Adapter logic both streams (calls callbacks) AND collects (populates the buffered fields). The `maxOutputBytes` cap applies as bytes arrive; the buffer never holds more than the cap.

### 2. `DirectSandbox` adapter

Switch from `child_process.exec` (buffered) to `child_process.spawn`. Wire `child.stdout.on("data", chunk => { onStdout?.(chunk); collected.stdout.push(chunk) })` and same for stderr. After exit, `Buffer.concat` to populate `ExecResult.stdout`/`stderr`.

### 3. `BwrapSandbox` adapter

Same pattern ŌĆö wrap the bwrap-spawn child with the streaming chunk handlers.

### 4. `VercelSandboxExec` adapter (`createVercelSandboxExec.ts`)

Replace the buffered `command.stdout() + command.stderr()` calls (lines 91-97) with the `runCommand({ stdout: writable, stderr: writable })` pattern verified by the smoke script:

```ts
import { Writable } from "node:stream"

const stdoutChunks: Uint8Array[] = []
const stderrChunks: Uint8Array[] = []
let totalBytes = 0
let truncated = false

const stdoutWritable = new Writable({
  write(chunk, _enc, cb) {
    const buf = Buffer.from(chunk)
    if (totalBytes + buf.length <= maxOutputBytes) {
      onStdout?.(buf)
      stdoutChunks.push(buf)
      totalBytes += buf.length
    } else {
      truncated = true
    }
    cb()
  }
})
// similar for stderrWritable
const result = await sandbox.runCommand({
  cmd: "bash", args: ["-c", command], cwd, env,
  stdout: stdoutWritable, stderr: stderrWritable, signal,
})
return {
  stdout: Buffer.concat(stdoutChunks),
  stderr: Buffer.concat(stderrChunks),
  exitCode: result.exitCode,
  truncated,
  durationMs: ...,
}
```

## Tests

`__tests__/streaming-exec.test.ts` (new, per adapter):

- Run `seq 1 100` and assert `onStdout` is called at least twice (not just once at end).
- Run `seq 1 100` and assert `ExecResult.stdout` (the buffered field) contains the full output regardless of streaming.
- Run a command that exceeds `maxOutputBytes`; assert truncation fires and `ExecResult.truncated === true`; assert no chunk after the cap reaches the callback.
- Run a command and abort mid-stream; assert `onStdout` stops being called after abort fires (smoke confirmed 12ms cutoff for VercelSandboxExec).
- Run a command without passing `onStdout`/`onStderr` (backward-compat); assert `ExecResult` is identical to today.

For `VercelSandboxExec` use the smoke script as a reference implementation. Vitest tests can mock the SDK; the smoke is the integration guarantee.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New + existing tests pass.
- A grep for `command.stdout()\\|command.stderr()` in `packages/agent/src/server/sandbox/` returns zero hits (replaced by streaming).
- Smoke script `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` still passes after changes (regression guard).
- Documented in CHANGELOG: "Sandbox.exec now supports optional onStdout/onStderr callbacks for streaming output."

## Estimated scope

~4-6 hours total. Smoke confirmed the design works against the real SDK, so implementation is mechanical: ~1h shared interface + DirectSandbox, ~1h BwrapSandbox, ~2h VercelSandboxExec (Writable wiring), ~1-2h tests.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Migration plan - Step 1.0".
- R5 in plan (output truncation + bash streaming) ŌĆö marked verified.
- Smoke script: `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`.
- SDK reference: `node_modules/.pnpm/@vercel+sandbox@1.10.0/.../dist/command.d.ts` (`Command.logs()`) and `dist/sandbox.d.ts` (`RunCommandParams.stdout/stderr: Writable`).
- Pi BashOperations type: `node_modules/.pnpm/@mariozechner+pi-coding-agent@*/.../dist/core/tools/bash.d.ts:18-34`.
close_reason: shipped ŌĆö commit 749f2a3. All 3 adapters streaming. 150 sandbox tests pass. Reviewed by Codex: reviseŌåÆfixŌåÆship.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: VERIFIED 2026-04-28: smoke test against real Vercel sandbox (script at packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts) produced 80 incremental Writable.write() calls for a 100-line/1.3s command. First chunk at +421ms, abort latency 12ms, fs roundtrip OK. Streaming design is empirically valid; implementation proceeds as bead describes.

title: [Phase 1.8.5] E2E test suite (per-mode smoke + Playwright + size regression)
description:
# Phase 1.8.5 ŌĆö E2E test suite for the migrated agent surface

## Why

After all three mode flips ship (1.6 + 1.7 + 1.8), we need a comprehensive regression net before deleting the hand-rolled tools (1.9). Today's coverage is a mix of unit tests (mocked sandbox) + the manual smoke script (`smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`). That leaves a gap: **no automated test confirms an actual chat turn end-to-end produces correct tool-call chunks against pi-built tools across all three modes**. Without this, 1.9 deletes the rollback target on faith.

This bead builds that net.

## What to build

### 1. Per-mode smoke harness (`packages/agent/scripts/smoke-modes/`)

Three runnable scripts, each spinning up an agent app in one mode and exercising the canonical tool surface:

- `direct.mts` ŌĆö `createAgentApp({ mode: "direct" })`, send 6 prompts (one per tool), assert tool-output chunks contain expected substrings.
- `local.mts` ŌĆö same but `mode: "local"`. Skip if bwrap is missing (CI flag).
- `vercel-sandbox.mts` ŌĆö same but `mode: "vercel-sandbox"`, requires `secret/agent/vercel` creds.

Each script logs every step with timestamps so failures are easy to debug:

```
[2026-04-28T18:23:01.234Z] [smoke-direct] creating agent app with mode=direct
[2026-04-28T18:23:01.456Z] [smoke-direct] send turn 1: "list workspace files"
[2026-04-28T18:23:01.789Z] [smoke-direct] received tool-input-available: name=find input={"pattern":"**/*"}
[2026-04-28T18:23:02.123Z] [smoke-direct] received tool-output-available: chunk has 47 lines
[2026-04-28T18:23:02.234Z] [smoke-direct] ASSERT pass: output contains "package.json"
```

### 2. Playwright e2e against the playground (`packages/agent/examples/playground/e2e/`)

Two specs:
- `tool-rendering.spec.ts` ŌĆö for each tool name, send a prompt that triggers it and assert the tool card renders in the chat UI with the expected name + status (running / success / error).
- `streaming-bash.spec.ts` ŌĆö send a long-output bash command (`for i in {1..50}; do echo line-$i; sleep 0.05; done`); assert the chat UI shows progressive output (live text content grows over the 2.5s run, NOT one chunk at end).

Run via `pnpm --filter @boring/example-playground exec playwright test`.

### 3. Integration test for the system-prompt size budget

`packages/agent/src/server/__tests__/system-prompt-size.regression.test.ts`:
- Boot a `createAgentApp({ mode: "direct" })` with no skills, no AGENTS.md, minimal config.
- Send a message to materialize the pi session.
- Fetch `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/system-prompt`.
- Assert prompt length < `BASELINE - 700` (i.e., the per-tool guideline + double-dash savings actually persist).
- Assert no `- \`(bash|read|write|edit|find|grep)\``" pattern (the double-dash bug).
- Assert no `Prefer the .* tool when` lines (the per-tool guideline pattern).

### 4. CI integration

Add to CI workflow:
- Always: integration test suite (per-mode smoke scripts in `direct` mode + Playwright + system-prompt-size).
- On vault-secret-available: vercel-sandbox smoke too. Skip otherwise.

## Tests this bead defines

| Suite | Test count | What it covers |
|---|---:|---|
| `smoke-modes/direct.mts` | 6 prompts ├Ś 1 turn | every tool fires, output asserts |
| `smoke-modes/local.mts` | 6 prompts ├Ś 1 turn | bwrap path; gated on bwrap binary |
| `smoke-modes/vercel-sandbox.mts` | 6 prompts ├Ś 1 turn | remote VM path; gated on creds |
| `tool-rendering.spec.ts` | 6 tools ├Ś 1 spec | UI renderer keyed on each name |
| `streaming-bash.spec.ts` | 1 spec | progressive output renders |
| `system-prompt-size.regression.test.ts` | 1 test | size budget + no leaked patterns |

**Detailed logging requirement:** every script logs to stdout AND emits structured JSON (`{ timestamp, mode, step, status, ... }`) to a per-run log file under `packages/agent/test-results/<run-id>/`. On CI failure the JSON files are uploaded as artifacts so anyone debugging can replay step-by-step.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All three smoke scripts exit 0 against their respective modes (vercel-sandbox gated on vault creds).
- [ ] Playwright suite passes locally and in CI.
- [ ] System-prompt-size regression test asserts the ~700-char drop holds.
- [ ] CI workflow runs the full suite on every PR.
- [ ] Failing tests upload the per-run JSON log as a CI artifact.
- [ ] README under `examples/playground/e2e/` documents how to run the suite locally.

## Depends on

- 1.10 (uhwx.10) direct mode flipped ŌĆö smoke can target it.
- 1.11 (uhwx.11) local mode flipped ŌĆö smoke can target it.
- 1.12 (uhwx.12) vercel-sandbox mode flipped ŌĆö smoke can target it.

## Why this gates `uhwx.13` (delete hand-rolled)

Before we delete the hand-rolled tools (the rollback target), we need a green E2E suite that proves the pi-built tools work correctly across all three modes. This bead is the rollback insurance ŌĆö once it is green and stable for Ōēź 3 days, `uhwx.13` can claim.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Test strategy".
- Existing smoke: `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` (the reference for per-mode smoke style).
- AGENTS.md "Critical architectural invariants" ŌĆö items 4 (path-bounding) and 5 (paired adapters) must be exercised by these tests.

title: [Phase 1.9.5] CI invariants ŌĆö lock in migration outcome (forbidden-patterns + renderer coverage + schema parity)
description:
# Phase 1.9.5 ŌĆö CI invariants for the migrated tool surface

## Why

Once the hand-rolled tools are deleted (1.9), nothing in CI prevents a future contributor from re-introducing them. The same kind of grep-enforced guard the project already uses (per AGENTS.md "Critical architectural invariants" ŌĆö `bash scripts/check-invariants.sh`) should be extended to lock in the migration outcome.

This is the "make the change un-undoable accidentally" bead.

## What to add

### 1. Forbidden-pattern grep rules

Add to `scripts/check-invariants.sh` (or equivalent invariant runner):

```bash
# Forbid hand-rolled tool re-introduction
forbid_pattern \
  "Hand-rolled tool re-introduction (use pi factories)" \
  "Pi-tools-migration locked in: bash/read/write/edit/find/grep are pi factories. Use Operations adapters." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src" -g "!**/operations/**" \
  -e "function createBashTool|function createReadTool|function createWriteTool|function createEditTool|function createFindFilesTool|function createGrepFilesTool"

# Forbid old tool names in code (post-rename)
forbid_pattern \
  "Old tool name find_files / grep_files" \
  "Renamed in step 1.10: use pi names \`find\` / \`grep\`." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src" \
  -e "find_files|grep_files"

# Forbid the per-tool guideline pattern in tool-adapter.ts
forbid_pattern \
  "Per-tool guideline bullets violate Principle 1" \
  "Pi-built tools self-advertise. Do not add \`Prefer the X tool when...\` guidelines." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src/server/harness" \
  -e "Prefer the .* tool when"

# Forbid the double-dash promptSnippet pattern
forbid_pattern \
  "Double-dash promptSnippet leaks into system prompt" \
  "promptSnippet should be the description verbatim (no leading dash + backticks)." \
  "$ROOT_DIR/src/server/harness" \
  -e "promptSnippet:.*- \`\\\$\\{tool\\.name\\}\`"
```

### 2. Tool-renderer coverage check

Add a script `scripts/check-tool-renderer-coverage.sh` (or a vitest test in `__tests__/`) that:
- Boots `createAgentApp({ mode: "direct" })`.
- Lists registered tool names.
- Asserts every tool name has a renderer entry in `packages/agent/src/ui-shadcn/workspaceToolRenderers.tsx` (and `packages/workspace/...` if separate).

This catches the case where someone adds a new pi tool to the bundle but forgets the renderer (model uses the tool, UI shows "unknown tool").

### 3. Snapshot test for vercelGrepTool schema parity

Add `packages/agent/src/server/tools/__tests__/grep-schema-parity.test.ts`:
- Compares `JSON.stringify(vercelGrepTool(mockSandbox).parameters)` to `JSON.stringify(createGrepTool(mockCwd).parameters)`.
- Fails if pi changes its schema and we have not updated ours. **Principle 1 enforcement gate.**

### 4. Bead ŌåÆ CI doc

Update `AGENTS.md` "Critical architectural invariants" to add a 9th: "Pi-tools-migration: bash/read/write/edit/find/grep flow through pi factories. Custom AgentTools require justification against Principle 3 (epic uhwx)." Or add as 10th if 9 is taken.

## Tests this bead defines

| Test | What it asserts |
|---|---|
| `check-invariants.sh` (extended) | No hand-rolled `createXxxTool` factories. No `find_files`/`grep_files`. No per-tool guideline pattern. No double-dash promptSnippet. |
| `check-tool-renderer-coverage.sh` | Every registered tool name has a UI renderer entry. |
| `grep-schema-parity.test.ts` | `vercelGrepTool.parameters === createGrepTool().parameters` (deep equal). |

**Detailed logging:** each invariant violation prints `[invariants] ERR <file>:<line>: <message>\n  Invariant: <name>\n  Fix: <hint>` (matches existing pattern in `check-invariants.sh`). Schema parity test on failure prints a unified diff.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `bash scripts/check-invariants.sh packages/agent` exits 0 on a clean tree.
- [ ] Running it on a synthetic violation (e.g., I add `function createBashTool()` to a tool file) exits non-zero with a clear message.
- [ ] Tool-renderer coverage check passes.
- [ ] Schema parity test passes.
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated with the invariant.
- [ ] CI workflow runs `check-invariants.sh` on every PR.

## Depends on

- 1.10 (uhwx.14) tool-name rename done ŌĆö invariant for `find_files`/`grep_files` only makes sense after.

## Why this gates `uhwx.13` (delete hand-rolled)

The CI invariants should land BEFORE the hand-rolled deletion lands, so the grep-check that "no `function createBashTool` in `tools/`" is already passing on a clean tree (the file is still there but the invariant ignores it via the path glob). After 1.9 deletes the file, the invariant catches re-introductions.

Order: 1.18 lands ŌåÆ 1.13 deletes the hand-rolled ŌåÆ invariants now actively guard the deletion.

## References

- AGENTS.md ┬¦ "Critical architectural invariants" + the existing `scripts/check-invariants.sh` for the pattern.
- Plan ┬¦ "Design principles" ŌĆö Principle 1 anti-patterns are what these invariants enforce.

title: [Phase 1.10.5] Update agent-package-spec.md decisions #4 #6 + CHANGELOG + README
description:
# Phase 1.10.5 ŌĆö Update agent-package-spec.md decision records

## Why

The original spec at `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` locks two decisions that this migration explicitly supersedes:

- **Decision #4 (line 631):** "v1 harness: @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent ŌĆö Ships loop + plugin API; we pass our own `tools: [...]` to skip pi defaults."
- **Decision #6 (line 633):** "Catalog: 4 base tools: `bash, read, write, edit`. +1 conditional `execute_isolated_code` if sandbox declares `isolated-code` capability. Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred."

After this migration, both are wrong:
- We do NOT pass our own tools ŌĆö we use pi's factories with Operations adapters.
- We DO have dedicated find/grep tools (six tools, not four).

Future contributors reading the spec will be confused unless we update the decision records.

## What to change

In `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md`:

### 1. Update decisions #4 and #6 in place

```diff
- | 4 | v1 harness | `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` | Ships loop + plugin API; we pass our own `tools: [...]` to skip pi's defaults. |
+ | 4 | v1 harness | `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` | Pi's factory tools + `XxxOperations` adapters per mode. Our backends plug in via Operations; pi owns tool definitions, schemas, prompt snippets. See `pi-tools-migration.md` (epic uhwx). |

- | 6 | Catalog | 4 base tools: `bash, read, write, edit`. +1 conditional `execute_isolated_code` ... Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred. |
+ | 6 | Catalog | Standard tools (via pi factories): `bash, read, write, edit, find, grep, ls`. Custom tools (via `customTools`): `execute_isolated_code` (capability-gated), `vercelGrepTool` (vercel-sandbox only ŌĆö pi's GrepOperations cannot redirect rg spawn). See `pi-tools-migration.md` "Design principles" for the full rationale. |
```

### 2. Add a "Supersedes" note to the spec

At the top of the spec, add:

```markdown
> **Note (2026-Q2):** Decisions #4 and #6 below describe the v1-original
> design. The pi-tools-migration (`docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md`,
> epic uhwx) supersedes these. The migration leverages pi's tool
> factories rather than supplying our own, and adds dedicated find/grep
> tools rather than routing them via bash. The old decisions remain
> in this table for historical reference.
```

### 3. Update the LOC budget estimate (line 490)

```diff
- | 4 tool factories (bash ŌåÆ sandbox; read/write/edit ŌåÆ workspace) | ~200 |
+ | Operations adapters (bash + 6 file ops, mode-specific) + bundle factories | ~250 |
+ | Custom tools (vercelGrepTool, executeIsolatedCodeTool) | ~250 |
```

### 4. Update the package-layout reference (line 540+)

Change `src/server/catalog/tools/{bashTool,readTool,...}.ts` block to reflect the new shape: `src/server/tools/{operations,filesystem,harness}/`. Match the actual post-migration tree.

### 5. Update README if relevant

If `packages/agent/README.md` documents tool names or the "we pass tools to pi" model, update it. Likely a one-paragraph change.

### 6. CHANGELOG entry

Add to `packages/agent/CHANGELOG.md` (create if missing):

```markdown
## [Unreleased] ŌĆö Pi tools migration (epic uhwx)

### Changed (breaking)
- Tool names: `find_files` ŌåÆ `find`, `grep_files` ŌåÆ `grep` (pi conventions).
- Tool schemas: read uses `offset`/`limit` (was `lineOffset`/`lineCount`); edit takes `edits[]` array (was `oldString`/`newString`/`replaceAll`); write drops `createDirs` (always recursive); bash gains optional `timeout`. Grep gains `ignoreCase`/`literal`/`context`.
- File ops registered through `buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle)` instead of `standardCatalog`. Standalone path: default-on with `disableDefaultFileTools` opt-out. Workspace path: via `filesystemPlugin`.

### Added
- Streaming bash output via `Sandbox.exec({ onStdout, onStderr })` callbacks.
- Genericized `Sandbox` interface (`SandboxResources`, `vendorHints`, `placement: "remote"`) for future Modal/Docker/Apple-container backends.

### Removed
- Six hand-rolled tool implementations under `src/server/catalog/tools/`.
- Per-tool guideline bullets in tool-adapter (system prompt -~700 chars).
- Double-dash `promptSnippet` bug (system prompt cosmetic fix).
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spec lines 631 and 633 updated.
- [ ] "Supersedes" note added at top of spec.
- [ ] LOC budget table updated.
- [ ] Package-layout reference updated to match post-migration tree.
- [ ] README updated if it mentions tools.
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry written.
- [ ] No remaining "we pass our own tools" or "Grep/find/ls done via bash; dedicated tools deferred" references in any doc under `packages/agent/`.

## Depends on

- 1.14 (uhwx.14) renames done ŌĆö CHANGELOG can reference final names.

## References

- Spec: `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` decisions #4, #6.
- Migration plan: `packages/agent/docs/plans/pi-tools-migration.md`.
- AGENTS.md "Where to Find What" table for the doc-owner convention.

title: [Phase 0.2] Refactor 6 tool files to use _shared.ts
description:
# Phase 0.2 ŌĆö Refactor all 6 tool files to use _shared.ts

## Why

Eliminate duplicated `makeError`/`bytesWritten`/`nowIso`/`normalizeLimit`/`decoder`/`FileChangeMetadata` declarations across `bashTool.ts`, `readTool.ts`, `writeTool.ts`, `editTool.ts`, `findFilesTool.ts`, `grepFilesTool.ts`. Reduces LOC ~30-40% per file with no behavior change.

## What to change

For each of the six tool files in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/`:

1. Replace local `function makeError(...)` with `import { makeError } from "./_shared"`.
2. Replace local `bytesWritten`/`nowIso` with `_shared` imports (edit + write tools only).
3. Replace local `normalizeLimit` with `_shared` import (find + grep tools only).
4. Replace local `TextDecoder` instances + `decode` helper with `_shared` imports (bash + grep tools).
5. Replace local `FileChangeMetadata` declarations with `_shared` import. Use the canonical `existsBefore?` field (universal ŌĆö see Phase 0.3 for the universal-emit decision).
6. Replace local constants (`DEFAULT_LIMIT`, `MAX_LIMIT`, `MAX_*_LENGTH`) with `_shared` imports.

Keep all existing tests passing. Each file should shrink by 10-25 LOC.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- All 6 tool tests pass unchanged.
- A `grep -rn "function makeError" packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/" returns zero hits except in `_shared.ts`.
- Same for `function bytesWritten`, `function nowIso`, `function normalizeLimit`.
- No `new TextDecoder` outside `_shared.ts` in this directory.
- No `interface FileChangeMetadata` outside `_shared.ts` in this directory.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Phase 0 - audit findings - Cross-file duplication" lists items 1-7 covered here.

title: [Phase 0.3] Standardize schemas, abort checks, error phrasing
description:
# Phase 0.3 ŌĆö Standardize schemas + error phrasing

## Why

The six tool files diverge in small, fixable ways: only `findFilesTool` + `grepFilesTool` use `additionalProperties: false`; only `grepFilesTool` has param descriptions; `bashTool` skips the abort check at start; `editTool` does not emit `existsBefore` even though the field belongs there universally. These divergences cause subtle differences in model behavior + workspace bridge events. This bead aligns them.

## What to change

In each of the six tool files in `packages/agent/src/server/catalog/tools/`:

1. **Add `additionalProperties: false`** to every JSON-schema `parameters` block. This tightens the model-facing contract ŌĆö small breaking change worth flagging in the PR description.
2. **Add `if (ctx.abortSignal.aborted) return makeError("X aborted")` at the top of every `execute`**, including bashTool which currently relies only on `sandbox.exec`'s signal forwarding.
3. **Standardize error phrasing**: `"<name> failed: <msg>"` for execution failures, `"<name> aborted"` for aborts. No `"unknown <X> failure"` variants.
4. **Standardize param-typing approach**: every `execute(input, ctx)` body starts with `const params = input as Record<string, unknown>`. Drop the local `XxxParams` interfaces in `editTool` + `writeTool`.
5. **Universal `existsBefore` emission**: `editTool` starts emitting `existsBefore: true` on its `FileChangeMetadata` so the workspace bridge sees a consistent shape. Workspace bridge already accepts the field; this just makes it always-present.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- All 6 tool tests pass; update tests for any outputs that change shape (mainly `editTool` for the new `existsBefore` field).
- A grep for `additionalProperties` in this directory shows it on all 6 schemas.
- A grep for `signal.aborted` shows the abort-at-start check in all 6 files.
- Workspace bridge / sessionChangesTracker tests still green.

## Notes

The `toolCallId` sanitization in `writeTool.ts:75-79` (regex `[^A-Za-z0-9_-]`) stays as defense-in-depth with a one-line comment explaining the intent. Pi guarantees UUIDs today but keeping the regex is essentially free.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Phase 0 - audit findings - Inconsistencies" lists items 8-14.
- Plan ┬¦ "Phase 0 standardization decisions" makes the universal-existsBefore + additionalProperties decisions explicit.
notes: Blocked by mixed same-file changes with in-progress boring-ui-v2-uhwx.4. uhwx.3 gates pass on combined tree, but commit would include uhwx.4 hunks (read TODO removal, edit helper consolidation). Coordinate/split before commit.

title: [Phase 1.1] boundFs Operations adapter (read/write/edit/ls + symlink-safe bounding)
description:
# Phase 1.1 ŌĆö boundFs Operations adapter (with symlink resolution)

## Why

Pi's standard tools take **absolute paths** in their `XxxOperations` callbacks (pi resolves model input via `resolveToCwd(filePath, cwd)` before dispatch ŌĆö see pi's `dist/core/tools/path-utils.js`). Pi does NO path-bounding ŌĆö the Operations adapter is 100% responsible for refusing paths outside the workspace. This bead builds that adapter for `direct` and `local` modes (where the workspace is the host fs).

`agent-package-spec.md` decision #7a says path-bounding is a workspace responsibility via `validatePath` / `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`. We move the bounding from inside our hand-rolled tool into a thin Operations wrapper around pi's default fs primitives.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/bound.ts` exporting:

```ts
import type { ReadOperations, WriteOperations, EditOperations, LsOperations } from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"
import { realpath, readFile as fsReadFile, writeFile as fsWriteFile, mkdir as fsMkdir, access, readdir, stat } from "node:fs/promises"
import { constants } from "node:fs"
import { isUnderWorkspace, assertRealPathWithinWorkspace } from "../../workspace/paths"

export interface BoundFs {
  read: ReadOperations
  write: WriteOperations
  edit: EditOperations
  ls: LsOperations
}

export function boundFs(workspaceRoot: string): BoundFs { ... }
```

Each method:
1. Calls `fs.realpath(absPath)` to resolve symlinks.
2. Throws `Error("path \"${rel}\" is outside workspace")` if resolved path is not under `workspaceRoot`.
3. Delegates to the underlying `node:fs` primitive on the happy path.

Port `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace` and `isUnderWorkspace` helpers from `packages/agent/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` ŌĆö they already exist for our hand-rolled tools. Reuse them.

## Tests

`packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/__tests__/bound.test.ts` covering:

- happy path: `read("/workspace/foo.txt")` succeeds.
- absolute escape: `read("/etc/passwd")` rejects with the standard message.
- relative escape via `..`: pi resolves to absolute before calling Operations, so this test must construct an absolute path that points outside the root.
- symlink escape: create a file in the workspace that symlinks to `/etc/passwd`; assert `read` is rejected. **Critical** ŌĆö pi's `resolveToCwd` does NOT resolve symlinks; we must.
- error shape: thrown error reaches the model as a useful text message (not "ENOENT") via pi's tool-invocation error path. Verify by running through `createReadTool(cwd, { operations: boundFs(root).read })` and asserting the resulting `ToolResult`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- `boundFs(root).read.readFile("/etc/passwd")` rejects.
- Symlink escape test passes.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Phase 1 ŌĆö Operations adapters" + R7 (path-bounding error message) + R8 (symlink resolution).
- Spec line 390 (`assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`).

title: [Phase 1.2] vercel*Ops Operations adapters (bash/read/write/edit/find/ls)
description:
# Phase 1.2 ŌĆö vercel*Ops Operations adapters

## Why

For `vercel-sandbox` mode, every file/exec operation must route to the remote VM, not the agent host. Pi's `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` (the canonical reference per pi-mono#564) is the template: replace the SSH backend with our `Sandbox` interface.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/vercel.ts` exporting:

```ts
import type { Sandbox } from "../../../shared/sandbox"
import type { BashOperations, ReadOperations, WriteOperations, EditOperations, FindOperations, LsOperations } from "@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent"

export function vercelBashOps(sandbox: Sandbox): BashOperations
export function vercelReadOps(sandbox: Sandbox): ReadOperations
export function vercelWriteOps(sandbox: Sandbox): WriteOperations
export function vercelEditOps(sandbox: Sandbox): EditOperations
export function vercelFindOps(sandbox: Sandbox): FindOperations
export function vercelLsOps(sandbox: Sandbox): LsOperations
```

### `vercelBashOps`

Pi calls `exec(command, cwd, { onData, signal, timeout, env })` and expects `Promise<{ exitCode: number | null }>`. After bead `uhwx.16` lands, our `Sandbox.exec` accepts `onStdout`/`onStderr` callbacks; this adapter forwards them to pi's `onData`:

```ts
export function vercelBashOps(sandbox: Sandbox): BashOperations {
  return {
    exec(command, cwd, { onData, signal, timeout, env }) {
      return sandbox.exec(command, {
        cwd, env, signal,
        timeoutMs: timeout && timeout * 1000,
        onStdout: (chunk) => onData(Buffer.from(chunk)),
        onStderr: (chunk) => onData(Buffer.from(chunk)),
      }).then((result) => ({ exitCode: result.exitCode }))
    },
  }
}
```

**Streaming is empirically verified** (see `uhwx.16` and `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`): 80 incremental Writable.write() calls over a 1.3s/100-line command. No further investigation needed.

### `vercelReadOps` / `vercelWriteOps` / `vercelEditOps`

Forward `(absPath, ...)` to `sandbox.fs.read` / `sandbox.writeFiles` / `sandbox.fs.access`. Document the path-translation contract: pi resolves the model's input via `resolveToCwd(filePath, cwd)` before calling Operations; our adapter receives the resolved absolute path. The VM uses `/vercel/sandbox/...` as cwd ŌĆö absolute paths the model emits already match.

### `vercelFindOps`

`glob(pattern, cwd, { ignore, limit })` runs `fd --glob --no-require-git --max-results <limit> <pattern> <cwd>` inside the VM via `sandbox.exec`, parses stdout into a `string[]`. Honor `ignore` patterns (default + caller-supplied) by appending `--exclude` flags.

### `vercelLsOps`

`exists(absPath)`, `stat(absPath)`, `readdir(absPath)` ŌåÆ forward to sandbox.fs methods.

## Tests

`packages/agent/src/server/tools/operations/__tests__/vercel.test.ts` mocking `Sandbox` and asserting:
- Each adapter forwards args correctly.
- `vercelBashOps` propagates abort + timeout (smoke confirmed 12ms abort latency).
- `vercelFindOps` parses fd output correctly (incl. truncation).
- Error responses propagate with usable messages.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- All six adapters compile against pi's `XxxOperations` types.
- `scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts` regression check still passes.

## Depends on

- 1.0 (uhwx.16) ŌĆö Sandbox.exec needs `onStdout`/`onStderr` callbacks before vercelBashOps can wire pi's `onData`.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Phase 1 ŌĆö vercel-sandbox mode".
- Smoke script: `packages/agent/scripts/smoke-vercel-sandbox.mts`.
- Pi reference: `examples/extensions/ssh.ts` lines 47-112.
- Pi RFC: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564.

title: [Phase 1.3] buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle) factory (bash + isolated-code, mode-aware)
description:
# Phase 1.3 ŌĆö buildHarnessAgentTools bundle factory

## Why

Bash + execute_isolated_code stay harness-level (per PLUGIN_MODEL.md non-goal line 122). They are NOT part of the filesystemPlugin path. `createAgentApp` always registers them directly. The bash Operations are mode-specific (direct: pi default; local: pi default + bwrap spawnHook; vercel-sandbox: vercel ops). This bead consolidates that switch into one factory.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/harness/index.ts` exporting:

```ts
export function buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle: RuntimeBundle): AgentTool[]
```

Internal `bashOptionsForMode(bundle: RuntimeBundle): BashToolOptions` returns:
- `direct`: `{ operations: createLocalBashOperations() }`
- `local`: `{ operations: createLocalBashOperations(), spawnHook: bwrapSpawnHook(bundle.workspace.root) }`
- `vercel-sandbox`: `{ operations: vercelBashOps(bundle.sandbox) }`

`bwrapSpawnHook(root)` is a tiny (~15 LOC) helper that returns a `BashSpawnHook` rewriting `command` to `bwrap ${bwrapArgs(root)} -- bash -lc ${shellEscape(command)}`. Reuse our existing `BwrapSandbox` flag-set as `bwrapArgs`.

The bundle returns:
1. `createBashTool(bundle.workspace.root, bashOptionsForMode(bundle))`
2. If `bundle.sandbox.capabilities.includes("isolated-code")`: also `executeIsolatedCodeTool(bundle.sandbox)` (existing tool, unchanged).

## Tests

`__tests__/harness.test.ts`:
- direct mode: returned array contains a tool named `bash`. The bash tool's execute matches pi's default behavior on a mock `cwd`.
- local mode: bash tool exists; spawning it routes through bwrap (assert via spawn-hook spy).
- vercel-sandbox: bash tool exists; spawning it forwards to a mock sandbox.exec.
- isolated-code: present when `sandbox.capabilities` includes `"isolated-code"`, absent otherwise.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- `buildHarnessAgentTools(bundle).map(t => t.name)` returns the right names per mode.

## Depends on

- 1.2 (vercel*Ops) for the vercel-sandbox branch.

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "buildHarnessAgentTools".
- PLUGIN_MODEL.md line 122 (bash + isolated stay harness-level).
- Pi sandbox extension reference: `examples/extensions/sandbox/index.ts:132-198` (spawnHook pattern).

title: [Phase 1.4] buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle) factory (read/write/edit/find/grep/ls, mode-aware)
description:
# Phase 1.4 ŌĆö buildFilesystemAgentTools bundle factory

## Why

The six file-ops tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `find`, `grep`, `ls`) are workspace-level. They flow through both the standalone path (`createAgentApp` default-on, opt-out via `disableDefaultFileTools`) and the workspace path (`filesystemPlugin.agentTools` per PLUGIN_MODEL.md). One factory function, called by both consumers, with mode-specific Operations injected.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/filesystem/index.ts` exporting:

```ts
export function buildFilesystemAgentTools(bundle: RuntimeBundle): AgentTool[]
```

Per-mode behavior:

- **direct + local**: workspace IS the host fs; pi defaults are correct.
  ```ts
  const ops = boundFs(cwd)
  return [
    createReadTool(cwd, { operations: ops.read }),
    createWriteTool(cwd, { operations: ops.write }),
    createEditTool(cwd, { operations: ops.edit }),
    createFindTool(cwd),                     // pi default fd, .gitignore-aware
    createGrepTool(cwd),                     // pi default rg
    createLsTool(cwd, { operations: ops.ls }),
  ]
  ```
- **vercel-sandbox**: route Operations to the VM. Grep is a custom `vercelGrepTool` (1.5) because pi's `GrepOperations` cannot redirect the actual rg spawn.
  ```ts
  return [
    createReadTool(cwd, { operations: vercelReadOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createWriteTool(cwd, { operations: vercelWriteOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createEditTool(cwd, { operations: vercelEditOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    createFindTool(cwd, { operations: vercelFindOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
    vercelGrepTool(bundle.sandbox),          // custom AgentTool
    createLsTool(cwd, { operations: vercelLsOps(bundle.sandbox) }),
  ]
  ```

## Tests

`__tests__/filesystem.test.ts`:
- direct + local: returned tools have names `[read, write, edit, find, grep, ls]`.
- vercel-sandbox: same names but `grep` is the custom `vercelGrepTool` instance, not pi's factory output.
- Switching modes returns different objects (no stale closure bugs).

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- New tests pass.
- A unit assertion: in vercel-sandbox mode, `tools.find(t => t.name === "grep")` is `vercelGrepTool`, not pi's.

## Depends on

- 1.1 (boundFs)
- 1.2 (vercel*Ops)
- 1.5 (vercelGrepTool)

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "buildFilesystemAgentTools".
- PLUGIN_MODEL.md lines 558-575 (filesystemPlugin pattern).
- Pi RFC: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/564.

title: [Phase 1.5] vercelGrepTool ŌĆö custom AgentTool with pi-grep-compatible schema
description:
# Phase 1.5 ŌĆö vercelGrepTool (pi-grep-schema-compatible custom AgentTool)

## Principle alignment

This tool is the canonical example of **Principle 3 case 2** ("pi's tool architecture cannot accommodate our backend"). Justification, in three lines, that the PR description must reproduce verbatim:

1. Pi's `GrepOperations` interface only exposes `isDirectory` + `readFile` (for context lines). The rg spawn is hardcoded local in `grep.js:144`. There is no Operations seam to redirect the search.
2. Issue pi-mono#3782 made dedicated grep a default tool because bare `bash grep` over `node_modules` burned ~98k tokens per session. Dropping grep is not an option.
3. Therefore vercel-sandbox needs a custom AgentTool that spawns rg inside the VM via `sandbox.exec`. **No other custom tool is justified by this case.**

Even though we're writing a custom tool, **Principle 1 still applies to the schema**: `vercelGrepTool.parameters` MUST be byte-for-byte identical to pi's `createGrepTool(cwd).parameters`. The model's grep behavior is identical across modes; only the underlying execution differs.

## What to build

New file `packages/agent/src/server/tools/vercelGrepTool.ts`:

```ts
export function vercelGrepTool(sandbox: Sandbox): AgentTool
```

### Schema (mirrors pi's `dist/core/tools/grep.d.ts` exactly)

```ts
{
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    pattern: { type: "string", description: "Regex (or literal if literal=true)." },
    path: { type: "string", description: "Sub-tree to search; defaults to workspace root." },
    glob: { type: "string", description: "File glob filter, e.g. \"*.ts\"." },
    ignoreCase: { type: "boolean" },
    literal: { type: "boolean", description: "Treat pattern as literal string, not regex." },
    context: { type: "integer", minimum: 0 },
    limit: { type: "integer", minimum: 1, maximum: 5000 },
  },
  required: ["pattern"],
  additionalProperties: false,
}
```

### Execute logic

Mirror pi's rg invocation in `dist/core/tools/grep.js:130-180`:
- Build args: `--no-heading -n -m <limit>`. Add `-i` for `ignoreCase`. Add `-F` for `literal`. Add `-C <n>` for `context`. Add `-g <glob>` for glob filter. Add `--smart-case` if neither flag set.
- Run `rg ${args} ${pattern} ${path ?? cwd}` via `sandbox.exec(...)`.
- Parse stdout into matches: `<file>:<lineNo>:<text>` (or `<file>-<lineNo>-<text>` for context lines). Truncate at `limit`.
- Return `ToolResult` with `details: { matches, truncated, ... }` matching pi's `GrepToolDetails` shape so a renderer keyed on tool name `grep` handles both pi-built and our-built versions identically.

## Tests

`__tests__/vercelGrepTool.test.ts`:
- **Schema parity test**: snapshot assertion that `JSON.stringify(vercelGrepTool(mockSandbox).parameters) === JSON.stringify(createGrepTool(cwd).parameters)`. **This is the Principle 1 enforcement gate** ŌĆö if pi changes its schema, our test fails and we update.
- Forward each schema field correctly to rg args.
- Parse output (mock `sandbox.exec` returning recorded rg stdout).
- Honor `limit` truncation.
- Surface ripgrep exit-code-1 (no matches found) as success with empty matches, not as an error.
- Honor `ctx.abortSignal`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean.
- Tests pass.
- **Schema snapshot equals pi's grep schema exactly** (Principle 1).
- `details` shape matches pi's `GrepToolDetails` (renderer parity).
- Manual visual check: a renderer keyed on `grep` works identically for pi-built (direct/local) and ours (vercel-sandbox).

## References

- Plan ┬¦ "Design principles" Principles 1 + 3.
- Plan ┬¦ "vercelGrepTool design" + R3.
- Pi grep impl: `node_modules/.pnpm/@mariozechner+pi-coding-agent@0.67.68_*/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/tools/grep.js`.
- Pi RFC for default grep: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/issues/3782.
close_reason: Done ŌĆö see commit d35660c; Claude review verdict: ship

title: M4: Name-collision precedence (document + runtime enforcement)
description:
**Goal:** When a plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, which wins? File: `src/server/catalog/mergeTools.ts`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins. Plugin tools registered AFTER defaults ŌåÆ plugin overrides. Document this.

**Future consideration:** namespace ours (`fs.bash`) ŌĆö deferred.

**Log a warning:** on collision: `[catalog] Tool "bash" overridden by plugin <name>`.

**Tests:** register a conflicting tool; assert which wins; assert warning logged.

## Acceptance Criteria

Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.
acceptance_criteria: Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.
close_reason: Implemented runtime collision enforcement via catalog/mergeTools: last-registered wins across standard -> extraTools -> plugin tools, with plugin override warning format '[catalog] Tool "<name>" overridden by plugin <name>'. Wired createAgentApp to use mergeTools and plugin-name labeling. Added mergeTools tests for merge order + collision override/warn behavior and documented precedence in docs/PLUGINS.md. Validation: vitest (mergeTools + createAgentApp), package typecheck, invariants all pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 9a9118d.

title: core-M0: Dockerfile.reference (multi-stage, pnpm workspace, node:20-slim)
description:
## Goal
Commit `packages/core/Dockerfile.reference` ŌĆö the multi-stage Dockerfile that child apps extend for their PaaS deploys.

## Why it matters
M5_FULL_APP and M5_FLY_CONFIG both reference "extending core's reference Dockerfile" but no bead actually creates the file. Without it, every child app reinvents the same multi-stage build.

## Acceptance criteria
- File `packages/core/Dockerfile.reference` committed.
- Multi-stage build:
  - Stage 1 `base`: `node:20-slim` + corepack + pnpm.
  - Stage 2 `deps`: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (cached layer).
  - Stage 3 `build`: `pnpm -r build` against monorepo workspace.
  - Stage 4 `runtime`: `node:20-slim`, copy `dist/`, expose 3000, CMD invokes the child app's main.js.
- `EXPOSE 3000` + `HEALTHCHECK` directive hitting `/health` every 30s.
- Pinned base-image SHA (security best practice).
- Documented headers explaining each stage.
- `packages/core/README.md` (or CORE.md ┬¦Deployment) references the file path so child apps know where to find it.

## Considerations
- Don't include the child app's name in the reference ŌĆö the CMD line is the bit child apps override (e.g. `CMD ["node", "apps/full-app/dist/server/main.js"]`).
- Use distroless or alpine? `node:20-slim` is the safe default ŌĆö alpine has musl quirks with native deps; distroless lacks shell for debugging.
- Image size target: <200MB after build.

## Tests
- `docker build -f packages/core/Dockerfile.reference .` succeeds.
- Image scans clean against `trivy` (CI step).
- `docker run --rm <image> --version` returns Node version.

## Refs
CORE.md ┬¦Deployment ┬¦Reference Dockerfile.
close_reason: M0 Dockerfile.reference shipped. 4-stage multi-stage build with pinned node:20-slim SHA, HEALTHCHECK, EXPOSE 3000. Codex SHIP.

title: Refactor WorkspaceAuthProvider to React Query
description:
## Context

`packages/core/src/front/WorkspaceAuthProvider.tsx` is hand-rolled with `useState`/`useEffect` + raw `apiFetchJson`. Everything else in core uses React Query. This means: no auto-refetch on workspace mutations from `WorkspaceSwitcher`, no shared cache with the workspaces list, no devtools visibility, manual cancel-on-unmount via `cancelled` flag.

## Scope

1. Refactor `WorkspaceAuthProvider` to use React Query:
   ```ts
   const workspacesQuery = useQuery({ queryKey: ['workspaces'], queryFn: fetchWorkspaces })
   const detailQuery = useQuery({
     queryKey: ['workspace', resolvedId],
     queryFn: () => fetchWorkspace(resolvedId),
     enabled: !!resolvedId,
   })
   ```
   No `refetchInterval` ŌĆö v7 spec confirms states resolve synchronously, no transient state to poll.
2. `useCurrentWorkspace` and `useWorkspaceRole` hooks pull from these queries.
3. `WorkspaceSwitcher`'s mutations (create, update name, delete) invalidate `['workspaces']` AND `['workspace', id]`.
4. Update tests at `packages/core/src/front/__tests__/WorkspaceAuthProvider.test.tsx`:
   - Hook returns workspace from cache when present.
   - Mutation invalidates and refetches.
   - Loading/error states surface correctly.
5. Update consumers (any code using `useCurrentWorkspace`) to handle React Query's loading state if needed.

## Why this exists

Consistency: the rest of the codebase uses RQ, so the user-facing behavior of the workspace context should match (no stale data after a switcher mutation). Also gives us devtools visibility.

## Acceptance criteria

- `WorkspaceAuthProvider` uses `useQuery` and `useQueryClient`.
- After `WorkspaceSwitcher` create/update/delete, the active workspace data refreshes without a manual reload.
- All existing `WorkspaceAuthProvider` tests pass; new test confirms invalidate-on-mutation.

## Considerations

- The queries need to live INSIDE the `<QueryClientProvider>` boundary set up by `BoringApp`.
- The "no current workspace" state (user has zero workspaces) must still work cleanly.
- The route param `:id` resolution from `useParams()` interacts with `enabled` ŌĆö handle the empty string case.

## Out of scope

- Adding `refetchInterval` (no transient runtime states in v7 ŌĆö they resolve sync).
- Changing the public API of `useCurrentWorkspace` / `useWorkspaceRole`.

## Spec reference

Decision #7 in v7 spec. Phase 1.

title: Phase 4: Storybook Visual Regression
description:
## Goal
Storybook stories for all standalone components and dockview chrome. Screenshot comparison via Chromatic or Percy.

## What to implement
### Stories for standalone components
- FileTree (various sizes: 10, 100, 1000 files)
- CodeEditor (JS, Python, JSON, large file, read-only)
- MarkdownEditor (rich content, toolbar states)
- DataCatalog (empty, multiple sources)

### Stories for dockview chrome
- PanelChrome (with/without icon, dirty state, close button)
- TabBar (multiple tabs, active tab, overflow)
- GroupChrome (locked, collapsible)

### Stories for panes in isolation (mocked data)
- Each pane with TestWorkspaceProvider + mock data

### Visual regression
- Screenshot comparison via Chromatic (preferred) or Percy
- Catches: CSS regressions, theme inconsistencies, responsive breakpoints
- Run on every PR (~3 min)

## Success criteria
- All components have stories
- Light + dark mode variants
- Visual regression catches theme/CSS changes

title: M3b: E2E ŌĆö chat feels like a real chat in both modes
description:
**Goal:** Polish gate ŌĆö chat experience is smooth in both `local` and `vercel-sandbox` modes.

**Scenarios (Playwright):**
1. Send "list files" ŌåÆ bash tool card renders via `<Tool>` with terminal output.
2. Send "edit foo.md to fix a typo" ŌåÆ DiffView renders unified diff.
3. Trigger `/help` ŌåÆ command list shows.
4. Trigger `/reset` ŌåÆ new session created, old session appears in toolbar.
5. Type `/model haiku` ŌåÆ switch confirmed.
6. Run a 6s bash ŌåÆ heartbeat ticker updates in the `<Tool>` card.

title: Infra: E2E logging harness (browser console + server logs ŌåÆ artifacts)
description:
**Goal:** Playwright fixture + reporter that captures ALL logs (browser console, network, server stdout) per test into structured artifact files. File: `e2e/fixtures/loggingHarness.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Why needed:** the user explicitly asked for "great, detailed logging so we can be sure that everything is working perfectly after implementation." E2E failures in CI with no log context = impossible to debug.

**Captures per test:**
- **Browser console:** all `console.log/warn/error` + page errors.
- **Network:** all requests/responses (method, URL, status, duration, bytes). Redact Authorization headers.
- **Server stdout/stderr:** piped from spawned backend process.
- **Test timeline:** structured events (`test.start`, `page.goto`, `assertion.fail`, etc.).
- **DOM snapshot on failure:** HTML dump for layout debugging.
- **Screenshot on failure:** native Playwright feature + our metadata overlay.

**Artifact layout (on failure):**
```
e2e-artifacts/
  <test-name>/
    browser-console.log           # timestamped lines
    network.har                    # HAR format for replay
    server.log                     # backend stdout/stderr
    dom.html
    screenshot.png
    timeline.json                  # structured events
```

**Fixture usage:**
```ts
test('mode flip', async ({ page, backend, logging }) => {
  logging.mark('starting mode flip test')
  await page.goto(backend.url)
  // ... test body
  logging.mark('verifying response')
  // ...
})
// If test fails ŌåÆ entire artifact dir written + linked in reporter output.
// If test passes ŌåÆ artifact dir deleted (unless BORING_E2E_KEEP=1 set).
```

**Reporter hooks:**
- On `test.afterEach` failure ŌåÆ flush artifacts + print artifact path to stderr.
- On test pass with `BORING_E2E_KEEP=1` ŌåÆ keep artifacts anyway.
- On `test.afterAll` ŌåÆ zip all artifacts into `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` for CI upload.

**CI integration:**
- GitHub Actions: upload `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` as workflow artifact (retained 7 days).
- Debug workflow: download artifact + local replay.

**Tests:**
- Passing test: artifacts not written (unless keep env var set).
- Failing test: all 6 artifact files present; tarball created.
- Server log capture: intentional backend error appears in `server.log`.
- HAR file: openable in Chrome DevTools.

**Logging (of the harness itself):** `[e2e-logging]` prefix. Log artifact dir path + flush events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every e2e test that fails produces a full artifact bundle in `e2e-artifacts/<test-name>/`. CI uploads the bundle on failure. A developer can download + explore the bundle to understand exactly what happened. Log redaction works for Authorization headers. Artifacts auto-clean on pass (keep-env override available).

title: fix(agent): resolve @babel/core bundling failure in pnpm build
description:
**Problem:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` fails at the tsup stage.

```
ERROR: Could not resolve "@babel/preset-typescript/package.json"
  ../../node_modules/.pnpm/@babel+core@7.29.0/node_modules/@babel/core/lib/config/files/module-types.js:165:36:
    165 Ōöé ...t packageJson = require("@babel/preset-typescript/package.json");
```

**Cause:** tsup bundles `@vitejs/plugin-react` ŌåÆ `@babel/plugin-transform-react-jsx-self` ŌåÆ `@babel/core`, which tries to `require('@babel/preset-typescript/package.json')` at build time. preset-typescript isn't in the dep tree.

**Options:**
1. Add `@babel/preset-typescript` as devDependency.
2. Mark `@babel/core` and `@vitejs/plugin-react` as external in `tsup.config.ts` (they shouldn't be bundled into the published CLI anyway).
3. Strip Vite plugin imports from server/CLI bundle entrypoint.

**Recommended:** #2 ŌĆö server/CLI bundle has no business pulling in vite plugins. Mark them external + any other dev-only deps.

**Impact:** blocks `npx @boring/agent` publish. Tests + typecheck still green; only prod build breaks.

**Verification:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` exits 0 and emits `dist/bin/boring-agent.js`.

## Acceptance Criteria

pnpm build succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/ contains: dist/shared.js, dist/server.js, dist/front.js, dist/bin/boring-agent.js. npx-like invocation works.
close_reason: Fixed tsup bundling failure by marking dev-only @vitejs/plugin-react and @babel/core as externals in packages/agent/tsup.config.ts (plus shared external constants). Verification: pnpm --dir packages/agent run build now succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/bin/boring-agent.js emitted and build-artifacts check passes. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: fab7bfc.
